{{Infobox Japanese Burial Mound | name = Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun | image = [[File:Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]] | caption = Mound and stone chamber entrance | alias = | group = {{ill|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster|ja|津屋崎古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Fukutsu City|ja|福津市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, Tebika 1615 (Ishibashi) | latitude_deg = 33 | latitude_min = 46 | latitude_sec = 13.23 | longitude_deg = 130 | longitude_min = 29 | longitude_sec = 52.58 | ISO = JP-40 | type = (Estimated) {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} | size = Diameter 25m | burial_facility = {{ill|Side-entrance chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} type {{ill|Stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}
(Wooden coffin inside) | grave_goods = Iron products, horse gear, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, Silla pottery | associated_tombs = | period = Early [[7th century]] | occupant = | imperial_tomb = | historic_site = Fukutsu City Designated Historic Site "Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun" | tangible_cultural_properties = | notes = | map = Japan Fukuoka | icon = Round Kofun | label = Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun | label_position = top }} {{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|33|46|30|N|130|29|40|E}} | zoom = 14 | float = right | width = 250| height = 200 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun and Miyajidake Kofun}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1 = {{Coord|33|46|13.23|N|130|29|52.58|E}} | label1 = Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun | label-pos1 = bottom | label-color1 = black | mark1 = Round Kofun.png | mark-size1 = 20 | mark-coord2 = {{Coord|33|46|51.10|N|130|29|19.15|E}} | label2 = Miyajidake Kofun | label-pos2 = right | label-color2 = black | mark2 = Round Kofun.png | mark-size2 = 20 }} The '''Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun''' (てびかなみきりふどうこふん) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Tebika, {{ill|Fukutsu City|ja|福津市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}. It is one of the burial mounds that make up the {{ill|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster|ja|津屋崎古墳群}} and has been designated as a historic site by Fukutsu City. == Overview == This burial mound is located on the terminal end of a hill (22–27 meters above sea level) derived from the foothills of Mount Miyaji, in northern Fukuoka Prefecture. It occupies the southernmost position among the Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster. The stone chamber has been open since ancient times, and the mound has been partially leveled. Detailed investigations of the stone chamber were conducted in [[1992]] (Heisei 4), while surveys of the mound were performed during [[2010]]–[[2011]] (Heisei 22–23). Due to leveling at the mound's base, its exact shape is unclear, but the mound is currently estimated to have a diameter of 20 meters and a height of 9 meters. Originally, it is reconstructed to have been approximately 25 meters in diameter{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. The burial facility is a {{ill|Side-entrance chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} type {{ill|Stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening towards the south-southwest. The total length of the stone chamber is 10.8 meters, and large basalt stones were used as its construction material. Despite the chamber being open for centuries, a survey of the tomb path in front of the chamber revealed wooden coffin components (nails and clamps), as well as iron products, horse gear, and fragments of {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} and Silla pottery{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. The construction of the mound is estimated to date to the early [[7th century]], with possible additional burials through the late 7th century{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. Previously considered to follow the {{ill|Miyajidake Kofun|ja|宮地嶽古墳}}, it is now viewed as preceding it, and is considered a successor to the {{ill|Zaiji Kentsuka Kofun|ja|在自剣塚古墳}}, the largest {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} in the Munakata region{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. As a tomb adopting the side-entrance chamber structure typical of the Kinai region, it holds significance for understanding the political landscape of the Munakata region{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. In [[1992]] (Heisei 4), the mound was designated as a historic site by the former Fukuma Town, now Fukutsu City. == Excavation History == * [[Tenpō]] era ([[1830]]–[[1844]]): Mentioned in the ''{{ill|Zoku Fudoki of Chikuzen Province|ja|筑前国続風土記拾遺}}'' (described as a faith site with the stone chamber already open, known as Fudo Cave){{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. * [[1992]]: Detailed measurements of the stone chamber{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. * October 13, 1992: Designated as a Fukuma Town Historic Site (now a Fukutsu City Historic Site){{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. * [[2010]]: Mound survey by the Fukutsu City Board of Education, reported in [[2013]]{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. * [[2011]]: Confirmation survey of the mound's size{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Bird's-eye view of the stone chamber}} ]] [[File:Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the stone chamber}} ]] The burial facility is a {{ill|Side-entrance chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} type {{ill|Stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening south-southwest. The rear part of the main chamber narrows into rear and front chambers, showcasing a multi-chamber structure similar to those in the Kinai region. The dimensions of the stone chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}: * Total length of the stone chamber: 10.8 meters (preserved) * Rear chamber: Length 2.1 meters, width 1.3 meters, height 1.9 meters * Front chamber: Length 1.8 meters, width 1.6 meters, height 2 meters * Passageway The chamber is constructed using basalt slabs, possibly sourced from [[:en:Ainoshima_(Shingū)|Ainoshima]]. Both the passageway and main chamber consist of a single layer of large stones, with five stones on each side wall and one stone on the rear wall. The floor is paved with large flat stones in some parts, while other parts have stone and pebble paving beneath approximately 10 cm of soil{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. == See Also == * (Wooden coffin inside) | grave_goods = Iron products, horse gear, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, Silla pottery | associated_tombs = | period = Early [[7th century]] | occupant = | imperial_tomb = | historic_site = Fukutsu City Designated Historic Site "Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun" | tangible_cultural_properties = | notes = | map = Japan Fukuoka | icon = Round Kofun | label = Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun | label_position = top }} {{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|33|46|30|N|130|29|40|E}} | zoom = 14 | float = right | width = 250| height = 200 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun and Miyajidake Kofun}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1 = {{Coord|33|46|13.23|N|130|29|52.58|E}} | label1 = Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun | label-pos1 = bottom | label-color1 = black | mark1 = Round Kofun.png | mark-size1 = 20 | mark-coord2 = {{Coord|33|46|51.10|N|130|29|19.15|E}} | label2 = Miyajidake Kofun | label-pos2 = right | label-color2 = black | mark2 = Round Kofun.png | mark-size2 = 20 }} The '''Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun''' (てびかなみきりふどうこふん) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Tebika, {{ill|Fukutsu City|ja|福津市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}. It is one of the burial mounds that make up the {{ill|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster|ja|津屋崎古墳群}} and has been designated as a historic site by Fukutsu City. == Overview == This burial mound is located on the terminal end of a hill (22–27 meters above sea level) derived from the foothills of Mount Miyaji, in northern Fukuoka Prefecture. It occupies the southernmost position among the Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster. The stone chamber has been open since ancient times, and the mound has been partially leveled. Detailed investigations of the stone chamber were conducted in [[1992]] (Heisei 4), while surveys of the mound were performed during [[2010]]–[[2011]] (Heisei 22–23). Due to leveling at the mound's base, its exact shape is unclear, but the mound is currently estimated to have a diameter of 20 meters and a height of 9 meters. Originally, it is reconstructed to have been approximately 25 meters in diameter{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. The burial facility is a {{ill|Side-entrance chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} type {{ill|Stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening towards the south-southwest. The total length of the stone chamber is 10.8 meters, and large basalt stones were used as its construction material. Despite the chamber being open for centuries, a survey of the tomb path in front of the chamber revealed wooden coffin components (nails and clamps), as well as iron products, horse gear, and fragments of {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} and Silla pottery{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. The construction of the mound is estimated to date to the early [[7th century]], with possible additional burials through the late 7th century{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. Previously considered to follow the {{ill|Miyajidake Kofun|ja|宮地嶽古墳}}, it is now viewed as preceding it, and is considered a successor to the {{ill|Zaiji Kentsuka Kofun|ja|在自剣塚古墳}}, the largest {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} in the Munakata region{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. As a tomb adopting the side-entrance chamber structure typical of the Kinai region, it holds significance for understanding the political landscape of the Munakata region{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. In [[1992]] (Heisei 4), the mound was designated as a historic site by the former Fukuma Town, now Fukutsu City. == Excavation History == * [[Tenpō]] era ([[1830]]–[[1844]]): Mentioned in the ''{{ill|Zoku Fudoki of Chikuzen Province|ja|筑前国続風土記拾遺}}'' (described as a faith site with the stone chamber already open, known as Fudo Cave){{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. * [[1992]]: Detailed measurements of the stone chamber{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. * October 13, 1992: Designated as a Fukuma Town Historic Site (now a Fukutsu City Historic Site){{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. * [[2010]]: Mound survey by the Fukutsu City Board of Education, reported in [[2013]]{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. * [[2011]]: Confirmation survey of the mound's size{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Bird's-eye view of the stone chamber}} ]] [[File:Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the stone chamber}} ]] The burial facility is a {{ill|Side-entrance chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} type {{ill|Stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening south-southwest. The rear part of the main chamber narrows into rear and front chambers, showcasing a multi-chamber structure similar to those in the Kinai region. The dimensions of the stone chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}: * Total length of the stone chamber: 10.8 meters (preserved) * Rear chamber: Length 2.1 meters, width 1.3 meters, height 1.9 meters * Front chamber: Length 1.8 meters, width 1.6 meters, height 2 meters * Passageway The chamber is constructed using basalt slabs, possibly sourced from [[:en:Ainoshima_(Shingū)|Ainoshima]]. Both the passageway and main chamber consist of a single layer of large stones, with five stones on each side wall and one stone on the rear wall. The floor is paved with large flat stones in some parts, while other parts have stone and pebble paving beneath approximately 10 cm of soil{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. == See Also == * revision.text:
{{Infobox Japanese Burial Mound | name = Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun | image = [[File:Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]] | caption = Mound and stone chamber entrance | alias = | group = {{ill|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster|ja|津屋崎古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Fukutsu City|ja|福津市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, Tebika 1615 (Ishibashi) | latitude_deg = 33 | latitude_min = 46 | latitude_sec = 13.23 | longitude_deg = 130 | longitude_min = 29 | longitude_sec = 52.58 | ISO = JP-40 | type = (Estimated) {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} | size = Diameter 25m | burial_facility = {{ill|Side-entrance chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} type {{ill|Stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}
(Wooden coffin inside) | grave_goods = Iron products, horse gear, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, Silla pottery | associated_tombs = | period = Early [[7th century]] | occupant = | imperial_tomb = | historic_site = Fukutsu City Designated Historic Site "Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun" | tangible_cultural_properties = | notes = | map = Japan Fukuoka | icon = Round Kofun | label = Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun | label_position = top }} {{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|33|46|30|N|130|29|40|E}} | zoom = 14 | float = right | width = 250| height = 200 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun and Miyajidake Kofun}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1 = {{Coord|33|46|13.23|N|130|29|52.58|E}} | label1 = Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun | label-pos1 = bottom | label-color1 = black | mark1 = Round Kofun.png | mark-size1 = 20 | mark-coord2 = {{Coord|33|46|51.10|N|130|29|19.15|E}} | label2 = Miyajidake Kofun | label-pos2 = right | label-color2 = black | mark2 = Round Kofun.png | mark-size2 = 20 }} The '''Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun''' (てびかなみきりふどうこふん) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Tebika, {{ill|Fukutsu City|ja|福津市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}. It is one of the burial mounds that make up the {{ill|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster|ja|津屋崎古墳群}} and has been designated as a historic site by Fukutsu City. == Overview == This burial mound is located on the terminal end of a hill (22–27 meters above sea level) derived from the foothills of Mount Miyaji, in northern Fukuoka Prefecture. It occupies the southernmost position among the Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster. The stone chamber has been open since ancient times, and the mound has been partially leveled. Detailed investigations of the stone chamber were conducted in [[1992]] (Heisei 4), while surveys of the mound were performed during [[2010]]–[[2011]] (Heisei 22–23). Due to leveling at the mound's base, its exact shape is unclear, but the mound is currently estimated to have a diameter of 20 meters and a height of 9 meters. Originally, it is reconstructed to have been approximately 25 meters in diameter{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. The burial facility is a {{ill|Side-entrance chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} type {{ill|Stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening towards the south-southwest. The total length of the stone chamber is 10.8 meters, and large basalt stones were used as its construction material. Despite the chamber being open for centuries, a survey of the tomb path in front of the chamber revealed wooden coffin components (nails and clamps), as well as iron products, horse gear, and fragments of {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} and Silla pottery{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. The construction of the mound is estimated to date to the early [[7th century]], with possible additional burials through the late 7th century{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. Previously considered to follow the {{ill|Miyajidake Kofun|ja|宮地嶽古墳}}, it is now viewed as preceding it, and is considered a successor to the {{ill|Zaiji Kentsuka Kofun|ja|在自剣塚古墳}}, the largest {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} in the Munakata region{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. As a tomb adopting the side-entrance chamber structure typical of the Kinai region, it holds significance for understanding the political landscape of the Munakata region{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. In [[1992]] (Heisei 4), the mound was designated as a historic site by the former Fukuma Town, now Fukutsu City. == Excavation History == * [[Tenpō]] era ([[1830]]–[[1844]]): Mentioned in the ''{{ill|Zoku Fudoki of Chikuzen Province|ja|筑前国続風土記拾遺}}'' (described as a faith site with the stone chamber already open, known as Fudo Cave){{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. * [[1992]]: Detailed measurements of the stone chamber{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. * October 13, 1992: Designated as a Fukuma Town Historic Site (now a Fukutsu City Historic Site){{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. * [[2010]]: Mound survey by the Fukutsu City Board of Education, reported in [[2013]]{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. * [[2011]]: Confirmation survey of the mound's size{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Bird's-eye view of the stone chamber}} ]] [[File:Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the stone chamber}} ]] The burial facility is a {{ill|Side-entrance chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} type {{ill|Stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening south-southwest. The rear part of the main chamber narrows into rear and front chambers, showcasing a multi-chamber structure similar to those in the Kinai region. The dimensions of the stone chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}: * Total length of the stone chamber: 10.8 meters (preserved) * Rear chamber: Length 2.1 meters, width 1.3 meters, height 1.9 meters * Front chamber: Length 1.8 meters, width 1.6 meters, height 2 meters * Passageway The chamber is constructed using basalt slabs, possibly sourced from [[:en:Ainoshima_(Shingū)|Ainoshima]]. Both the passageway and main chamber consist of a single layer of large stones, with five stones on each side wall and one stone on the rear wall. The floor is paved with large flat stones in some parts, while other parts have stone and pebble paving beneath approximately 10 cm of soil{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. == See Also == * [[Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster]] ** [[Miyajidake Kofun]] == References == [[Category:dd]][[Category:Burial sites in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Fukutsu City History]] {{Infobox Japanese Burial Mound | name = Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun | image = [[File:Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]] | caption = Mound and stone chamber entrance | alias = | group = {{ill|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster|ja|津屋崎古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Fukutsu City|ja|福津市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, Tebika 1615 (Ishibashi) | latitude_deg = 33 | latitude_min = 46 | latitude_sec = 13.23 | longitude_deg = 130 | longitude_min = 29 | longitude_sec = 52.58 | ISO = JP-40 | type = (Estimated) {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} | size = Diameter 25m | burial_facility = {{ill|Side-entrance chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} type {{ill|Stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}
(Wooden coffin inside) | grave_goods = Iron products, horse gear, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, Silla pottery | associated_tombs = | period = Early [[7th century]] | occupant = | imperial_tomb = | historic_site = Fukutsu City Designated Historic Site "Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun" | tangible_cultural_properties = | notes = | map = Japan Fukuoka | icon = Round Kofun | label = Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun | label_position = top }} {{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|33|46|30|N|130|29|40|E}} | zoom = 14 | float = right | width = 250| height = 200 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun and Miyajidake Kofun}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1 = {{Coord|33|46|13.23|N|130|29|52.58|E}} | label1 = Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun | label-pos1 = bottom | label-color1 = black | mark1 = Round Kofun.png | mark-size1 = 20 | mark-coord2 = {{Coord|33|46|51.10|N|130|29|19.15|E}} | label2 = Miyajidake Kofun | label-pos2 = right | label-color2 = black | mark2 = Round Kofun.png | mark-size2 = 20 }} The '''Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun''' (てびかなみきりふどうこふん) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Tebika, {{ill|Fukutsu City|ja|福津市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}. It is one of the burial mounds that make up the {{ill|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster|ja|津屋崎古墳群}} and has been designated as a historic site by Fukutsu City. == Overview == This burial mound is located on the terminal end of a hill (22–27 meters above sea level) derived from the foothills of Mount Miyaji, in northern Fukuoka Prefecture. It occupies the southernmost position among the Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster. The stone chamber has been open since ancient times, and the mound has been partially leveled. Detailed investigations of the stone chamber were conducted in [[1992]] (Heisei 4), while surveys of the mound were performed during [[2010]]–[[2011]] (Heisei 22–23). Due to leveling at the mound's base, its exact shape is unclear, but the mound is currently estimated to have a diameter of 20 meters and a height of 9 meters. Originally, it is reconstructed to have been approximately 25 meters in diameter{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. The burial facility is a {{ill|Side-entrance chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} type {{ill|Stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening towards the south-southwest. The total length of the stone chamber is 10.8 meters, and large basalt stones were used as its construction material. Despite the chamber being open for centuries, a survey of the tomb path in front of the chamber revealed wooden coffin components (nails and clamps), as well as iron products, horse gear, and fragments of {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} and Silla pottery{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. The construction of the mound is estimated to date to the early [[7th century]], with possible additional burials through the late 7th century{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. Previously considered to follow the {{ill|Miyajidake Kofun|ja|宮地嶽古墳}}, it is now viewed as preceding it, and is considered a successor to the {{ill|Zaiji Kentsuka Kofun|ja|在自剣塚古墳}}, the largest {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} in the Munakata region{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. As a tomb adopting the side-entrance chamber structure typical of the Kinai region, it holds significance for understanding the political landscape of the Munakata region{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. In [[1992]] (Heisei 4), the mound was designated as a historic site by the former Fukuma Town, now Fukutsu City. == Excavation History == * [[Tenpō]] era ([[1830]]–[[1844]]): Mentioned in the ''{{ill|Zoku Fudoki of Chikuzen Province|ja|筑前国続風土記拾遺}}'' (described as a faith site with the stone chamber already open, known as Fudo Cave){{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. * [[1992]]: Detailed measurements of the stone chamber{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. * October 13, 1992: Designated as a Fukuma Town Historic Site (now a Fukutsu City Historic Site){{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. * [[2010]]: Mound survey by the Fukutsu City Board of Education, reported in [[2013]]{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. * [[2011]]: Confirmation survey of the mound's size{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Bird's-eye view of the stone chamber}} ]] [[File:Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the stone chamber}} ]] The burial facility is a {{ill|Side-entrance chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} type {{ill|Stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening south-southwest. The rear part of the main chamber narrows into rear and front chambers, showcasing a multi-chamber structure similar to those in the Kinai region. The dimensions of the stone chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}: * Total length of the stone chamber: 10.8 meters (preserved) * Rear chamber: Length 2.1 meters, width 1.3 meters, height 1.9 meters * Front chamber: Length 1.8 meters, width 1.6 meters, height 2 meters * Passageway The chamber is constructed using basalt slabs, possibly sourced from [[:en:Ainoshima_(Shingū)|Ainoshima]]. Both the passageway and main chamber consist of a single layer of large stones, with five stones on each side wall and one stone on the rear wall. The floor is paved with large flat stones in some parts, while other parts have stone and pebble paving beneath approximately 10 cm of soil{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. == See Also == * {{Infobox Japanese Burial Mound | name = Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun | image = [[File:Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]] | caption = Mound and stone chamber entrance | alias = | group = {{ill|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster|ja|津屋崎古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Fukutsu City|ja|福津市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, Tebika 1615 (Ishibashi) | latitude_deg = 33 | latitude_min = 46 | latitude_sec = 13.23 | longitude_deg = 130 | longitude_min = 29 | longitude_sec = 52.58 | ISO = JP-40 | type = (Estimated) {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} | size = Diameter 25m | burial_facility = {{ill|Side-entrance chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} type {{ill|Stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}
(Wooden coffin inside) | grave_goods = Iron products, horse gear, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, Silla pottery | associated_tombs = | period = Early [[7th century]] | occupant = | imperial_tomb = | historic_site = Fukutsu City Designated Historic Site "Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun" | tangible_cultural_properties = | notes = | map = Japan Fukuoka | icon = Round Kofun | label = Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun | label_position = top }} {{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|33|46|30|N|130|29|40|E}} | zoom = 14 | float = right | width = 250| height = 200 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun and Miyajidake Kofun}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1 = {{Coord|33|46|13.23|N|130|29|52.58|E}} | label1 = Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun | label-pos1 = bottom | label-color1 = black | mark1 = Round Kofun.png | mark-size1 = 20 | mark-coord2 = {{Coord|33|46|51.10|N|130|29|19.15|E}} | label2 = Miyajidake Kofun | label-pos2 = right | label-color2 = black | mark2 = Round Kofun.png | mark-size2 = 20 }} The '''Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun''' (てびかなみきりふどうこふん) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Tebika, {{ill|Fukutsu City|ja|福津市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}. It is one of the burial mounds that make up the {{ill|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster|ja|津屋崎古墳群}} and has been designated as a historic site by Fukutsu City. == Overview == This burial mound is located on the terminal end of a hill (22–27 meters above sea level) derived from the foothills of Mount Miyaji, in northern Fukuoka Prefecture. It occupies the southernmost position among the Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster. The stone chamber has been open since ancient times, and the mound has been partially leveled. Detailed investigations of the stone chamber were conducted in [[1992]] (Heisei 4), while surveys of the mound were performed during [[2010]]–[[2011]] (Heisei 22–23). Due to leveling at the mound's base, its exact shape is unclear, but the mound is currently estimated to have a diameter of 20 meters and a height of 9 meters. Originally, it is reconstructed to have been approximately 25 meters in diameter{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. The burial facility is a {{ill|Side-entrance chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} type {{ill|Stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening towards the south-southwest. The total length of the stone chamber is 10.8 meters, and large basalt stones were used as its construction material. Despite the chamber being open for centuries, a survey of the tomb path in front of the chamber revealed wooden coffin components (nails and clamps), as well as iron products, horse gear, and fragments of {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} and Silla pottery{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. The construction of the mound is estimated to date to the early [[7th century]], with possible additional burials through the late 7th century{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. Previously considered to follow the {{ill|Miyajidake Kofun|ja|宮地嶽古墳}}, it is now viewed as preceding it, and is considered a successor to the {{ill|Zaiji Kentsuka Kofun|ja|在自剣塚古墳}}, the largest {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} in the Munakata region{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. As a tomb adopting the side-entrance chamber structure typical of the Kinai region, it holds significance for understanding the political landscape of the Munakata region{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. In [[1992]] (Heisei 4), the mound was designated as a historic site by the former Fukuma Town, now Fukutsu City. == Excavation History == * [[Tenpō]] era ([[1830]]–[[1844]]): Mentioned in the ''{{ill|Zoku Fudoki of Chikuzen Province|ja|筑前国続風土記拾遺}}'' (described as a faith site with the stone chamber already open, known as Fudo Cave){{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. * [[1992]]: Detailed measurements of the stone chamber{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. * October 13, 1992: Designated as a Fukuma Town Historic Site (now a Fukutsu City Historic Site){{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. * [[2010]]: Mound survey by the Fukutsu City Board of Education, reported in [[2013]]{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. * [[2011]]: Confirmation survey of the mound's size{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Bird's-eye view of the stone chamber}} ]] [[File:Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the stone chamber}} ]] The burial facility is a {{ill|Side-entrance chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} type {{ill|Stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening south-southwest. The rear part of the main chamber narrows into rear and front chambers, showcasing a multi-chamber structure similar to those in the Kinai region. The dimensions of the stone chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}: * Total length of the stone chamber: 10.8 meters (preserved) * Rear chamber: Length 2.1 meters, width 1.3 meters, height 1.9 meters * Front chamber: Length 1.8 meters, width 1.6 meters, height 2 meters * Passageway The chamber is constructed using basalt slabs, possibly sourced from [[:en:Ainoshima_(Shingū)|Ainoshima]]. Both the passageway and main chamber consist of a single layer of large stones, with five stones on each side wall and one stone on the rear wall. The floor is paved with large flat stones in some parts, while other parts have stone and pebble paving beneath approximately 10 cm of soil{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. == See Also == * revision.text:
{{Infobox Japanese Burial Mound | name = Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun | image = [[File:Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]] | caption = Mound and stone chamber entrance | alias = | group = {{ill|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster|ja|津屋崎古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Fukutsu City|ja|福津市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, Tebika 1615 (Ishibashi) | latitude_deg = 33 | latitude_min = 46 | latitude_sec = 13.23 | longitude_deg = 130 | longitude_min = 29 | longitude_sec = 52.58 | ISO = JP-40 | type = (Estimated) {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} | size = Diameter 25m | burial_facility = {{ill|Side-entrance chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} type {{ill|Stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}
(Wooden coffin inside) | grave_goods = Iron products, horse gear, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, Silla pottery | associated_tombs = | period = Early [[7th century]] | occupant = | imperial_tomb = | historic_site = Fukutsu City Designated Historic Site "Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun" | tangible_cultural_properties = | notes = | map = Japan Fukuoka | icon = Round Kofun | label = Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun | label_position = top }} {{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|33|46|30|N|130|29|40|E}} | zoom = 14 | float = right | width = 250| height = 200 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun and Miyajidake Kofun}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1 = {{Coord|33|46|13.23|N|130|29|52.58|E}} | label1 = Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun | label-pos1 = bottom | label-color1 = black | mark1 = Round Kofun.png | mark-size1 = 20 | mark-coord2 = {{Coord|33|46|51.10|N|130|29|19.15|E}} | label2 = Miyajidake Kofun | label-pos2 = right | label-color2 = black | mark2 = Round Kofun.png | mark-size2 = 20 }} The '''Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun''' (てびかなみきりふどうこふん) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Tebika, {{ill|Fukutsu City|ja|福津市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}. It is one of the burial mounds that make up the {{ill|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster|ja|津屋崎古墳群}} and has been designated as a historic site by Fukutsu City. == Overview == This burial mound is located on the terminal end of a hill (22–27 meters above sea level) derived from the foothills of Mount Miyaji, in northern Fukuoka Prefecture. It occupies the southernmost position among the Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster. The stone chamber has been open since ancient times, and the mound has been partially leveled. Detailed investigations of the stone chamber were conducted in [[1992]] (Heisei 4), while surveys of the mound were performed during [[2010]]–[[2011]] (Heisei 22–23). Due to leveling at the mound's base, its exact shape is unclear, but the mound is currently estimated to have a diameter of 20 meters and a height of 9 meters. Originally, it is reconstructed to have been approximately 25 meters in diameter{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. The burial facility is a {{ill|Side-entrance chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} type {{ill|Stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening towards the south-southwest. The total length of the stone chamber is 10.8 meters, and large basalt stones were used as its construction material. Despite the chamber being open for centuries, a survey of the tomb path in front of the chamber revealed wooden coffin components (nails and clamps), as well as iron products, horse gear, and fragments of {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} and Silla pottery{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. The construction of the mound is estimated to date to the early [[7th century]], with possible additional burials through the late 7th century{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. Previously considered to follow the {{ill|Miyajidake Kofun|ja|宮地嶽古墳}}, it is now viewed as preceding it, and is considered a successor to the {{ill|Zaiji Kentsuka Kofun|ja|在自剣塚古墳}}, the largest {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} in the Munakata region{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. As a tomb adopting the side-entrance chamber structure typical of the Kinai region, it holds significance for understanding the political landscape of the Munakata region{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. In [[1992]] (Heisei 4), the mound was designated as a historic site by the former Fukuma Town, now Fukutsu City. == Excavation History == * [[Tenpō]] era ([[1830]]–[[1844]]): Mentioned in the ''{{ill|Zoku Fudoki of Chikuzen Province|ja|筑前国続風土記拾遺}}'' (described as a faith site with the stone chamber already open, known as Fudo Cave){{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. * [[1992]]: Detailed measurements of the stone chamber{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. * October 13, 1992: Designated as a Fukuma Town Historic Site (now a Fukutsu City Historic Site){{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. * [[2010]]: Mound survey by the Fukutsu City Board of Education, reported in [[2013]]{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. * [[2011]]: Confirmation survey of the mound's size{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Bird's-eye view of the stone chamber}} ]] [[File:Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the stone chamber}} ]] The burial facility is a {{ill|Side-entrance chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} type {{ill|Stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening south-southwest. The rear part of the main chamber narrows into rear and front chambers, showcasing a multi-chamber structure similar to those in the Kinai region. The dimensions of the stone chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}: * Total length of the stone chamber: 10.8 meters (preserved) * Rear chamber: Length 2.1 meters, width 1.3 meters, height 1.9 meters * Front chamber: Length 1.8 meters, width 1.6 meters, height 2 meters * Passageway The chamber is constructed using basalt slabs, possibly sourced from [[:en:Ainoshima_(Shingū)|Ainoshima]]. Both the passageway and main chamber consist of a single layer of large stones, with five stones on each side wall and one stone on the rear wall. The floor is paved with large flat stones in some parts, while other parts have stone and pebble paving beneath approximately 10 cm of soil{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. == See Also == * [[Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster]] ** [[Miyajidake Kofun]] == References == [[Category:dd]][[Category:Burial sites in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Fukutsu City History]] (Wooden coffin inside) | grave_goods = Iron products, horse gear, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, Silla pottery | associated_tombs = | period = Early [[7th century]] | occupant = | imperial_tomb = | historic_site = Fukutsu City Designated Historic Site "Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun" | tangible_cultural_properties = | notes = | map = Japan Fukuoka | icon = Round Kofun | label = Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun | label_position = top }} {{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|33|46|30|N|130|29|40|E}} | zoom = 14 | float = right | width = 250| height = 200 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun and Miyajidake Kofun}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1 = {{Coord|33|46|13.23|N|130|29|52.58|E}} | label1 = Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun | label-pos1 = bottom | label-color1 = black | mark1 = Round Kofun.png | mark-size1 = 20 | mark-coord2 = {{Coord|33|46|51.10|N|130|29|19.15|E}} | label2 = Miyajidake Kofun | label-pos2 = right | label-color2 = black | mark2 = Round Kofun.png | mark-size2 = 20 }} The '''Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun''' (てびかなみきりふどうこふん) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Tebika, {{ill|Fukutsu City|ja|福津市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}. It is one of the burial mounds that make up the {{ill|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster|ja|津屋崎古墳群}} and has been designated as a historic site by Fukutsu City. == Overview == This burial mound is located on the terminal end of a hill (22–27 meters above sea level) derived from the foothills of Mount Miyaji, in northern Fukuoka Prefecture. It occupies the southernmost position among the Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster. The stone chamber has been open since ancient times, and the mound has been partially leveled. Detailed investigations of the stone chamber were conducted in [[1992]] (Heisei 4), while surveys of the mound were performed during [[2010]]–[[2011]] (Heisei 22–23). Due to leveling at the mound's base, its exact shape is unclear, but the mound is currently estimated to have a diameter of 20 meters and a height of 9 meters. Originally, it is reconstructed to have been approximately 25 meters in diameter{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. The burial facility is a {{ill|Side-entrance chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} type {{ill|Stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening towards the south-southwest. The total length of the stone chamber is 10.8 meters, and large basalt stones were used as its construction material. Despite the chamber being open for centuries, a survey of the tomb path in front of the chamber revealed wooden coffin components (nails and clamps), as well as iron products, horse gear, and fragments of {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} and Silla pottery{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. The construction of the mound is estimated to date to the early [[7th century]], with possible additional burials through the late 7th century{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. Previously considered to follow the {{ill|Miyajidake Kofun|ja|宮地嶽古墳}}, it is now viewed as preceding it, and is considered a successor to the {{ill|Zaiji Kentsuka Kofun|ja|在自剣塚古墳}}, the largest {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} in the Munakata region{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. As a tomb adopting the side-entrance chamber structure typical of the Kinai region, it holds significance for understanding the political landscape of the Munakata region{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. In [[1992]] (Heisei 4), the mound was designated as a historic site by the former Fukuma Town, now Fukutsu City. == Excavation History == * [[Tenpō]] era ([[1830]]–[[1844]]): Mentioned in the ''{{ill|Zoku Fudoki of Chikuzen Province|ja|筑前国続風土記拾遺}}'' (described as a faith site with the stone chamber already open, known as Fudo Cave){{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. * [[1992]]: Detailed measurements of the stone chamber{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. * October 13, 1992: Designated as a Fukuma Town Historic Site (now a Fukutsu City Historic Site){{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. * [[2010]]: Mound survey by the Fukutsu City Board of Education, reported in [[2013]]{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. * [[2011]]: Confirmation survey of the mound's size{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Bird's-eye view of the stone chamber}} ]] [[File:Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the stone chamber}} ]] The burial facility is a {{ill|Side-entrance chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} type {{ill|Stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening south-southwest. The rear part of the main chamber narrows into rear and front chambers, showcasing a multi-chamber structure similar to those in the Kinai region. The dimensions of the stone chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}: * Total length of the stone chamber: 10.8 meters (preserved) * Rear chamber: Length 2.1 meters, width 1.3 meters, height 1.9 meters * Front chamber: Length 1.8 meters, width 1.6 meters, height 2 meters * Passageway The chamber is constructed using basalt slabs, possibly sourced from [[:en:Ainoshima_(Shingū)|Ainoshima]]. Both the passageway and main chamber consist of a single layer of large stones, with five stones on each side wall and one stone on the rear wall. The floor is paved with large flat stones in some parts, while other parts have stone and pebble paving beneath approximately 10 cm of soil{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. == See Also == * {{Infobox Japanese Burial Mound | name = Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun | image = [[File:Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]] | caption = Mound and stone chamber entrance | alias = | group = {{ill|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster|ja|津屋崎古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Fukutsu City|ja|福津市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, Tebika 1615 (Ishibashi) | latitude_deg = 33 | latitude_min = 46 | latitude_sec = 13.23 | longitude_deg = 130 | longitude_min = 29 | longitude_sec = 52.58 | ISO = JP-40 | type = (Estimated) {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} | size = Diameter 25m | burial_facility = {{ill|Side-entrance chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} type {{ill|Stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}
(Wooden coffin inside) | grave_goods = Iron products, horse gear, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, Silla pottery | associated_tombs = | period = Early [[7th century]] | occupant = | imperial_tomb = | historic_site = Fukutsu City Designated Historic Site "Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun" | tangible_cultural_properties = | notes = | map = Japan Fukuoka | icon = Round Kofun | label = Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun | label_position = top }} {{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|33|46|30|N|130|29|40|E}} | zoom = 14 | float = right | width = 250| height = 200 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun and Miyajidake Kofun}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1 = {{Coord|33|46|13.23|N|130|29|52.58|E}} | label1 = Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun | label-pos1 = bottom | label-color1 = black | mark1 = Round Kofun.png | mark-size1 = 20 | mark-coord2 = {{Coord|33|46|51.10|N|130|29|19.15|E}} | label2 = Miyajidake Kofun | label-pos2 = right | label-color2 = black | mark2 = Round Kofun.png | mark-size2 = 20 }} The '''Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun''' (てびかなみきりふどうこふん) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Tebika, {{ill|Fukutsu City|ja|福津市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}. It is one of the burial mounds that make up the {{ill|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster|ja|津屋崎古墳群}} and has been designated as a historic site by Fukutsu City. == Overview == This burial mound is located on the terminal end of a hill (22–27 meters above sea level) derived from the foothills of Mount Miyaji, in northern Fukuoka Prefecture. It occupies the southernmost position among the Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster. The stone chamber has been open since ancient times, and the mound has been partially leveled. Detailed investigations of the stone chamber were conducted in [[1992]] (Heisei 4), while surveys of the mound were performed during [[2010]]–[[2011]] (Heisei 22–23). Due to leveling at the mound's base, its exact shape is unclear, but the mound is currently estimated to have a diameter of 20 meters and a height of 9 meters. Originally, it is reconstructed to have been approximately 25 meters in diameter{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. The burial facility is a {{ill|Side-entrance chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} type {{ill|Stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening towards the south-southwest. The total length of the stone chamber is 10.8 meters, and large basalt stones were used as its construction material. Despite the chamber being open for centuries, a survey of the tomb path in front of the chamber revealed wooden coffin components (nails and clamps), as well as iron products, horse gear, and fragments of {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} and Silla pottery{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. The construction of the mound is estimated to date to the early [[7th century]], with possible additional burials through the late 7th century{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. Previously considered to follow the {{ill|Miyajidake Kofun|ja|宮地嶽古墳}}, it is now viewed as preceding it, and is considered a successor to the {{ill|Zaiji Kentsuka Kofun|ja|在自剣塚古墳}}, the largest {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} in the Munakata region{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. As a tomb adopting the side-entrance chamber structure typical of the Kinai region, it holds significance for understanding the political landscape of the Munakata region{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. In [[1992]] (Heisei 4), the mound was designated as a historic site by the former Fukuma Town, now Fukutsu City. == Excavation History == * [[Tenpō]] era ([[1830]]–[[1844]]): Mentioned in the ''{{ill|Zoku Fudoki of Chikuzen Province|ja|筑前国続風土記拾遺}}'' (described as a faith site with the stone chamber already open, known as Fudo Cave){{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. * [[1992]]: Detailed measurements of the stone chamber{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. * October 13, 1992: Designated as a Fukuma Town Historic Site (now a Fukutsu City Historic Site){{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. * [[2010]]: Mound survey by the Fukutsu City Board of Education, reported in [[2013]]{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. * [[2011]]: Confirmation survey of the mound's size{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Bird's-eye view of the stone chamber}} ]] [[File:Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the stone chamber}} ]] The burial facility is a {{ill|Side-entrance chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} type {{ill|Stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening south-southwest. The rear part of the main chamber narrows into rear and front chambers, showcasing a multi-chamber structure similar to those in the Kinai region. The dimensions of the stone chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}: * Total length of the stone chamber: 10.8 meters (preserved) * Rear chamber: Length 2.1 meters, width 1.3 meters, height 1.9 meters * Front chamber: Length 1.8 meters, width 1.6 meters, height 2 meters * Passageway The chamber is constructed using basalt slabs, possibly sourced from [[:en:Ainoshima_(Shingū)|Ainoshima]]. Both the passageway and main chamber consist of a single layer of large stones, with five stones on each side wall and one stone on the rear wall. The floor is paved with large flat stones in some parts, while other parts have stone and pebble paving beneath approximately 10 cm of soil{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. == See Also == * The dimensions of the stone chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}: * Total length of the stone chamber: 10.8 meters (preserved) * Rear chamber: Length 2.1 meters, width 1.3 meters, height 1.9 meters * Front chamber: Length 1.8 meters, width 1.6 meters, height 2 meters * Passageway The chamber is constructed using basalt slabs, possibly sourced from [[:en:Ainoshima_(Shingū)|Ainoshima]]. Both the passageway and main chamber consist of a single layer of large stones, with five stones on each side wall and one stone on the rear wall. The floor is paved with large flat stones in some parts, while other parts have stone and pebble paving beneath approximately 10 cm of soil{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. == See Also == * revision.comment: Text replacement - "Burial Mound" to "Kofun"
revision.text:
{{Infobox Japanese Kofun | name = Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun | image = [[File:Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]] | caption = Mound and stone chamber entrance | alias = | group = {{ill|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster|ja|津屋崎古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Fukutsu City|ja|福津市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, Tebika 1615 (Ishibashi) | latitude_deg = 33 | latitude_min = 46 | latitude_sec = 13.23 | longitude_deg = 130 | longitude_min = 29 | longitude_sec = 52.58 | ISO = JP-40 | type = (Estimated) {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} | size = Diameter 25m | burial_facility = {{ill|Side-entrance chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} type {{ill|Stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}
(Wooden coffin inside) | grave_goods = Iron products, horse gear, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, Silla pottery | associated_tombs = | period = Early [[7th century]] | occupant = | imperial_tomb = | historic_site = Fukutsu City Designated Historic Site "Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun" | tangible_cultural_properties = | notes = | map = Japan Fukuoka | icon = Round Kofun | label = Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun | label_position = top }} {{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|33|46|30|N|130|29|40|E}} | zoom = 14 | float = right | width = 250| height = 200 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun and Miyajidake Kofun}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1 = {{Coord|33|46|13.23|N|130|29|52.58|E}} | label1 = Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun | label-pos1 = bottom | label-color1 = black | mark1 = Round Kofun.png | mark-size1 = 20 | mark-coord2 = {{Coord|33|46|51.10|N|130|29|19.15|E}} | label2 = Miyajidake Kofun | label-pos2 = right | label-color2 = black | mark2 = Round Kofun.png | mark-size2 = 20 }} The '''Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun''' (てびかなみきりふどうこふん) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Tebika, {{ill|Fukutsu City|ja|福津市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}. It is one of the burial mounds that make up the {{ill|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster|ja|津屋崎古墳群}} and has been designated as a historic site by Fukutsu City. == Overview == This burial mound is located on the terminal end of a hill (22–27 meters above sea level) derived from the foothills of Mount Miyaji, in northern Fukuoka Prefecture. It occupies the southernmost position among the Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster. The stone chamber has been open since ancient times, and the mound has been partially leveled. Detailed investigations of the stone chamber were conducted in [[1992]] (Heisei 4), while surveys of the mound were performed during [[2010]]–[[2011]] (Heisei 22–23). Due to leveling at the mound's base, its exact shape is unclear, but the mound is currently estimated to have a diameter of 20 meters and a height of 9 meters. Originally, it is reconstructed to have been approximately 25 meters in diameter{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. The burial facility is a {{ill|Side-entrance chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} type {{ill|Stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening towards the south-southwest. The total length of the stone chamber is 10.8 meters, and large basalt stones were used as its construction material. Despite the chamber being open for centuries, a survey of the tomb path in front of the chamber revealed wooden coffin components (nails and clamps), as well as iron products, horse gear, and fragments of {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} and Silla pottery{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. The construction of the mound is estimated to date to the early [[7th century]], with possible additional burials through the late 7th century{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. Previously considered to follow the {{ill|Miyajidake Kofun|ja|宮地嶽古墳}}, it is now viewed as preceding it, and is considered a successor to the {{ill|Zaiji Kentsuka Kofun|ja|在自剣塚古墳}}, the largest {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} in the Munakata region{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. As a tomb adopting the side-entrance chamber structure typical of the Kinai region, it holds significance for understanding the political landscape of the Munakata region{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. In [[1992]] (Heisei 4), the mound was designated as a historic site by the former Fukuma Town, now Fukutsu City. == Excavation History == * [[Tenpō]] era ([[1830]]–[[1844]]): Mentioned in the ''{{ill|Zoku Fudoki of Chikuzen Province|ja|筑前国続風土記拾遺}}'' (described as a faith site with the stone chamber already open, known as Fudo Cave){{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. * [[1992]]: Detailed measurements of the stone chamber{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. * October 13, 1992: Designated as a Fukuma Town Historic Site (now a Fukutsu City Historic Site){{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. * [[2010]]: Mound survey by the Fukutsu City Board of Education, reported in [[2013]]{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. * [[2011]]: Confirmation survey of the mound's size{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Bird's-eye view of the stone chamber}} ]] [[File:Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the stone chamber}} ]] The burial facility is a {{ill|Side-entrance chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} type {{ill|Stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening south-southwest. The rear part of the main chamber narrows into rear and front chambers, showcasing a multi-chamber structure similar to those in the Kinai region. The dimensions of the stone chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}: * Total length of the stone chamber: 10.8 meters (preserved) * Rear chamber: Length 2.1 meters, width 1.3 meters, height 1.9 meters * Front chamber: Length 1.8 meters, width 1.6 meters, height 2 meters * Passageway The chamber is constructed using basalt slabs, possibly sourced from [[:en:Ainoshima_(Shingū)|Ainoshima]]. Both the passageway and main chamber consist of a single layer of large stones, with five stones on each side wall and one stone on the rear wall. The floor is paved with large flat stones in some parts, while other parts have stone and pebble paving beneath approximately 10 cm of soil{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. == See Also == * [[Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster]] ** [[Miyajidake Kofun]] == References == [[Category:qq]][[Category:Burial sites in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:Fukutsu City History]] (Wooden coffin inside) | grave_goods = Iron products, horse gear, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, Silla pottery | associated_tombs = | period = Early [[7th century]] | occupant = | imperial_tomb = | historic_site = Fukutsu City Designated Historic Site "Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun" | tangible_cultural_properties = | notes = | map = Japan Fukuoka | icon = Round Kofun | label = Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun | label_position = top }} {{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|33|46|30|N|130|29|40|E}} | zoom = 14 | float = right | width = 250| height = 200 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun and Miyajidake Kofun}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1 = {{Coord|33|46|13.23|N|130|29|52.58|E}} | label1 = Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun | label-pos1 = bottom | label-color1 = black | mark1 = Round Kofun.png | mark-size1 = 20 | mark-coord2 = {{Coord|33|46|51.10|N|130|29|19.15|E}} | label2 = Miyajidake Kofun | label-pos2 = right | label-color2 = black | mark2 = Round Kofun.png | mark-size2 = 20 }} The '''Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun''' (てびかなみきりふどうこふん) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Tebika, {{ill|Fukutsu City|ja|福津市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}. It is one of the Kofuns that make up the {{ill|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster|ja|津屋崎古墳群}} and has been designated as a historic site by Fukutsu City. == Overview == This Kofun is located on the terminal end of a hill (22–27 meters above sea level) derived from the foothills of Mount Miyaji, in northern Fukuoka Prefecture. It occupies the southernmost position among the Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster. The stone chamber has been open since ancient times, and the mound has been partially leveled. Detailed investigations of the stone chamber were conducted in [[1992]] (Heisei 4), while surveys of the mound were performed during [[2010]]–[[2011]] (Heisei 22–23). Due to leveling at the mound's base, its exact shape is unclear, but the mound is currently estimated to have a diameter of 20 meters and a height of 9 meters. Originally, it is reconstructed to have been approximately 25 meters in diameter{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. The burial facility is a {{ill|Side-entrance chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} type {{ill|Stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening towards the south-southwest. The total length of the stone chamber is 10.8 meters, and large basalt stones were used as its construction material. Despite the chamber being open for centuries, a survey of the tomb path in front of the chamber revealed wooden coffin components (nails and clamps), as well as iron products, horse gear, and fragments of {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} and Silla pottery{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. The construction of the mound is estimated to date to the early [[7th century]], with possible additional burials through the late 7th century{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. Previously considered to follow the {{ill|Miyajidake Kofun|ja|宮地嶽古墳}}, it is now viewed as preceding it, and is considered a successor to the {{ill|Zaiji Kentsuka Kofun|ja|在自剣塚古墳}}, the largest {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} in the Munakata region{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. As a tomb adopting the side-entrance chamber structure typical of the Kinai region, it holds significance for understanding the political landscape of the Munakata region{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. In [[1992]] (Heisei 4), the mound was designated as a historic site by the former Fukuma Town, now Fukutsu City. == Excavation History == * [[Tenpō]] era ([[1830]]–[[1844]]): Mentioned in the ''{{ill|Zoku Fudoki of Chikuzen Province|ja|筑前国続風土記拾遺}}'' (described as a faith site with the stone chamber already open, known as Fudo Cave){{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. * [[1992]]: Detailed measurements of the stone chamber{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. * October 13, 1992: Designated as a Fukuma Town Historic Site (now a Fukutsu City Historic Site){{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. * [[2010]]: Mound survey by the Fukutsu City Board of Education, reported in [[2013]]{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. * [[2011]]: Confirmation survey of the mound's size{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Bird's-eye view of the stone chamber}} ]] [[File:Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the stone chamber}} ]] The burial facility is a {{ill|Side-entrance chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} type {{ill|Stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening south-southwest. The rear part of the main chamber narrows into rear and front chambers, showcasing a multi-chamber structure similar to those in the Kinai region. The dimensions of the stone chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}: * Total length of the stone chamber: 10.8 meters (preserved) * Rear chamber: Length 2.1 meters, width 1.3 meters, height 1.9 meters * Front chamber: Length 1.8 meters, width 1.6 meters, height 2 meters * Passageway The chamber is constructed using basalt slabs, possibly sourced from [[:en:Ainoshima_(Shingū)|Ainoshima]]. Both the passageway and main chamber consist of a single layer of large stones, with five stones on each side wall and one stone on the rear wall. The floor is paved with large flat stones in some parts, while other parts have stone and pebble paving beneath approximately 10 cm of soil{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. == See Also == * It is one of the Kofuns that make up the {{ill|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster|ja|津屋崎古墳群}} and has been designated as a historic site by Fukutsu City. == Overview == This Kofun is located on the terminal end of a hill (22–27 meters above sea level) derived from the foothills of Mount Miyaji, in northern Fukuoka Prefecture. It occupies the southernmost position among the Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster. The stone chamber has been open since ancient times, and the mound has been partially leveled. Detailed investigations of the stone chamber were conducted in [[1992]] (Heisei 4), while surveys of the mound were performed during [[2010]]–[[2011]] (Heisei 22–23). Due to leveling at the mound's base, its exact shape is unclear, but the mound is currently estimated to have a diameter of 20 meters and a height of 9 meters. Originally, it is reconstructed to have been approximately 25 meters in diameter{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. The burial facility is a {{ill|Side-entrance chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} type {{ill|Stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening towards the south-southwest. The total length of the stone chamber is 10.8 meters, and large basalt stones were used as its construction material. Despite the chamber being open for centuries, a survey of the tomb path in front of the chamber revealed wooden coffin components (nails and clamps), as well as iron products, horse gear, and fragments of {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} and Silla pottery{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. The construction of the mound is estimated to date to the early [[7th century]], with possible additional burials through the late 7th century{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. Previously considered to follow the {{ill|Miyajidake Kofun|ja|宮地嶽古墳}}, it is now viewed as preceding it, and is considered a successor to the {{ill|Zaiji Kentsuka Kofun|ja|在自剣塚古墳}}, the largest {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} in the Munakata region{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. As a tomb adopting the side-entrance chamber structure typical of the Kinai region, it holds significance for understanding the political landscape of the Munakata region{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. In [[1992]] (Heisei 4), the mound was designated as a historic site by the former Fukuma Town, now Fukutsu City. == Excavation History == * [[Tenpō]] era ([[1830]]–[[1844]]): Mentioned in the ''{{ill|Zoku Fudoki of Chikuzen Province|ja|筑前国続風土記拾遺}}'' (described as a faith site with the stone chamber already open, known as Fudo Cave){{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. * [[1992]]: Detailed measurements of the stone chamber{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. * October 13, 1992: Designated as a Fukuma Town Historic Site (now a Fukutsu City Historic Site){{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. * [[2010]]: Mound survey by the Fukutsu City Board of Education, reported in [[2013]]{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. * [[2011]]: Confirmation survey of the mound's size{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Bird's-eye view of the stone chamber}} ]] [[File:Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the stone chamber}} ]] The burial facility is a {{ill|Side-entrance chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} type {{ill|Stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening south-southwest. The rear part of the main chamber narrows into rear and front chambers, showcasing a multi-chamber structure similar to those in the Kinai region. The dimensions of the stone chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}: * Total length of the stone chamber: 10.8 meters (preserved) * Rear chamber: Length 2.1 meters, width 1.3 meters, height 1.9 meters * Front chamber: Length 1.8 meters, width 1.6 meters, height 2 meters * Passageway The chamber is constructed using basalt slabs, possibly sourced from [[:en:Ainoshima_(Shingū)|Ainoshima]]. Both the passageway and main chamber consist of a single layer of large stones, with five stones on each side wall and one stone on the rear wall. The floor is paved with large flat stones in some parts, while other parts have stone and pebble paving beneath approximately 10 cm of soil{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. == See Also == * revision.text:
{{Infobox Japanese Kofun | name = Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun | image = [[File:Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]] | caption = Mound and stone chamber entrance | alias = | group = {{ill|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster|ja|津屋崎古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Fukutsu City|ja|福津市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, Tebika 1615 (Ishibashi) | latitude_deg = 33 | latitude_min = 46 | latitude_sec = 13.23 | longitude_deg = 130 | longitude_min = 29 | longitude_sec = 52.58 | ISO = JP-40 | type = (Estimated) {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} | size = Diameter 25m | burial_facility = {{ill|Side-entrance chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} type {{ill|Stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}
(Wooden coffin inside) | grave_goods = Iron products, horse gear, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, Silla pottery | associated_tombs = | period = Early [[7th century]] | occupant = | imperial_tomb = | historic_site = Fukutsu City Designated Historic Site "Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun" | tangible_cultural_properties = | notes = | map = Japan Fukuoka | icon = Round Kofun | label = Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun | label_position = top }} {{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|33|46|30|N|130|29|40|E}} | zoom = 14 | float = right | width = 250| height = 200 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun and Miyajidake Kofun}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1 = {{Coord|33|46|13.23|N|130|29|52.58|E}} | label1 = Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun | label-pos1 = bottom | label-color1 = black | mark1 = Round Kofun.png | mark-size1 = 20 | mark-coord2 = {{Coord|33|46|51.10|N|130|29|19.15|E}} | label2 = Miyajidake Kofun | label-pos2 = right | label-color2 = black | mark2 = Round Kofun.png | mark-size2 = 20 }} The '''Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun''' (てびかなみきりふどうこふん) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Tebika, {{ill|Fukutsu City|ja|福津市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}. It is one of the Kofuns that make up the {{ill|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster|ja|津屋崎古墳群}} and has been designated as a historic site by Fukutsu City. == Overview == This Kofun is located on the terminal end of a hill (22–27 meters above sea level) derived from the foothills of Mount Miyaji, in northern Fukuoka Prefecture. It occupies the southernmost position among the Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster. The stone chamber has been open since ancient times, and the mound has been partially leveled. Detailed investigations of the stone chamber were conducted in [[1992]] (Heisei 4), while surveys of the mound were performed during [[2010]]–[[2011]] (Heisei 22–23). Due to leveling at the mound's base, its exact shape is unclear, but the mound is currently estimated to have a diameter of 20 meters and a height of 9 meters. Originally, it is reconstructed to have been approximately 25 meters in diameter{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. The burial facility is a {{ill|Side-entrance chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} type {{ill|Stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening towards the south-southwest. The total length of the stone chamber is 10.8 meters, and large basalt stones were used as its construction material. Despite the chamber being open for centuries, a survey of the tomb path in front of the chamber revealed wooden coffin components (nails and clamps), as well as iron products, horse gear, and fragments of {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} and Silla pottery{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. The construction of the mound is estimated to date to the early [[7th century]], with possible additional burials through the late 7th century{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. Previously considered to follow the {{ill|Miyajidake Kofun|ja|宮地嶽古墳}}, it is now viewed as preceding it, and is considered a successor to the {{ill|Zaiji Kentsuka Kofun|ja|在自剣塚古墳}}, the largest {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} in the Munakata region{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. As a tomb adopting the side-entrance chamber structure typical of the Kinai region, it holds significance for understanding the political landscape of the Munakata region{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. In [[1992]] (Heisei 4), the mound was designated as a historic site by the former Fukuma Town, now Fukutsu City. == Excavation History == * [[Tenpō]] era ([[1830]]–[[1844]]): Mentioned in the ''{{ill|Zoku Fudoki of Chikuzen Province|ja|筑前国続風土記拾遺}}'' (described as a faith site with the stone chamber already open, known as Fudo Cave){{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. * [[1992]]: Detailed measurements of the stone chamber{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. * October 13, 1992: Designated as a Fukuma Town Historic Site (now a Fukutsu City Historic Site){{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. * [[2010]]: Mound survey by the Fukutsu City Board of Education, reported in [[2013]]{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. * [[2011]]: Confirmation survey of the mound's size{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Bird's-eye view of the stone chamber}} ]] [[File:Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the stone chamber}} ]] The burial facility is a {{ill|Side-entrance chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} type {{ill|Stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening south-southwest. The rear part of the main chamber narrows into rear and front chambers, showcasing a multi-chamber structure similar to those in the Kinai region. The dimensions of the stone chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}: * Total length of the stone chamber: 10.8 meters (preserved) * Rear chamber: Length 2.1 meters, width 1.3 meters, height 1.9 meters * Front chamber: Length 1.8 meters, width 1.6 meters, height 2 meters * Passageway The chamber is constructed using basalt slabs, possibly sourced from [[:en:Ainoshima_(Shingū)|Ainoshima]]. Both the passageway and main chamber consist of a single layer of large stones, with five stones on each side wall and one stone on the rear wall. The floor is paved with large flat stones in some parts, while other parts have stone and pebble paving beneath approximately 10 cm of soil{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. == See Also == * (Wooden coffin inside) | grave_goods = Iron products, horse gear, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, Silla pottery | associated_tombs = | period = Early [[7th century]] | occupant = | imperial_tomb = | historic_site = Fukutsu City Designated Historic Site "Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun" | tangible_cultural_properties = | notes = | map = Japan Fukuoka | icon = Round Kofun | label = Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun | label_position = top }} {{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|33|46|30|N|130|29|40|E}} | zoom = 14 | float = right | width = 250| height = 200 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun and Miyajidake Kofun}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1 = {{Coord|33|46|13.23|N|130|29|52.58|E}} | label1 = Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun | label-pos1 = bottom | label-color1 = black | mark1 = Round Kofun.png | mark-size1 = 20 | mark-coord2 = {{Coord|33|46|51.10|N|130|29|19.15|E}} | label2 = Miyajidake Kofun | label-pos2 = right | label-color2 = black | mark2 = Round Kofun.png | mark-size2 = 20 }} The '''Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun''' (手光波切不動古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Tebika, {{ill|Fukutsu City|ja|福津市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}. It is one of the Kofuns that make up the {{ill|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster|ja|津屋崎古墳群}} and has been designated as a historic site by Fukutsu City. == Overview == This Kofun is located on the terminal end of a hill (22–27 meters above sea level) derived from the foothills of Mount Miyaji, in northern Fukuoka Prefecture. It occupies the southernmost position among the Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster. The stone chamber has been open since ancient times, and the mound has been partially leveled. Detailed investigations of the stone chamber were conducted in [[1992]] (Heisei 4), while surveys of the mound were performed during [[2010]]–[[2011]] (Heisei 22–23). Due to leveling at the mound's base, its exact shape is unclear, but the mound is currently estimated to have a diameter of 20 meters and a height of 9 meters. Originally, it is reconstructed to have been approximately 25 meters in diameter{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. The burial facility is a {{ill|Side-entrance chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} type {{ill|Stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening towards the south-southwest. The total length of the stone chamber is 10.8 meters, and large basalt stones were used as its construction material. Despite the chamber being open for centuries, a survey of the tomb path in front of the chamber revealed wooden coffin components (nails and clamps), as well as iron products, horse gear, and fragments of {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} and Silla pottery{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. The construction of the mound is estimated to date to the early [[7th century]], with possible additional burials through the late 7th century{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. Previously considered to follow the {{ill|Miyajidake Kofun|ja|宮地嶽古墳}}, it is now viewed as preceding it, and is considered a successor to the {{ill|Zaiji Kentsuka Kofun|ja|在自剣塚古墳}}, the largest {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} in the Munakata region{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. As a tomb adopting the side-entrance chamber structure typical of the Kinai region, it holds significance for understanding the political landscape of the Munakata region{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. In [[1992]] (Heisei 4), the mound was designated as a historic site by the former Fukuma Town, now Fukutsu City. == Excavation History == * [[Tenpō]] era ([[1830]]–[[1844]]): Mentioned in the ''{{ill|Zoku Fudoki of Chikuzen Province|ja|筑前国続風土記拾遺}}'' (described as a faith site with the stone chamber already open, known as Fudo Cave){{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. * [[1992]]: Detailed measurements of the stone chamber{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. * October 13, 1992: Designated as a Fukuma Town Historic Site (now a Fukutsu City Historic Site){{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. * [[2010]]: Mound survey by the Fukutsu City Board of Education, reported in [[2013]]{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. * [[2011]]: Confirmation survey of the mound's size{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Bird's-eye view of the stone chamber}} ]] [[File:Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the stone chamber}} ]] The burial facility is a {{ill|Side-entrance chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} type {{ill|Stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening south-southwest. The rear part of the main chamber narrows into rear and front chambers, showcasing a multi-chamber structure similar to those in the Kinai region. The dimensions of the stone chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}: * Total length of the stone chamber: 10.8 meters (preserved) * Rear chamber: Length 2.1 meters, width 1.3 meters, height 1.9 meters * Front chamber: Length 1.8 meters, width 1.6 meters, height 2 meters * Passageway The chamber is constructed using basalt slabs, possibly sourced from [[:en:Ainoshima_(Shingū)|Ainoshima]]. Both the passageway and main chamber consist of a single layer of large stones, with five stones on each side wall and one stone on the rear wall. The floor is paved with large flat stones in some parts, while other parts have stone and pebble paving beneath approximately 10 cm of soil{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. == See Also == *
