This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Japanese Kofun | name = Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun | image = [[File:Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]] | caption = Mound and stone chamber entrance | alternate_name = | part_of = {{ill|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster|ja|津屋崎古墳群|12=draft}} | location = {{ill|Fukutsu City|ja|福津市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県|12=draft}}, Tebika 1615 (Ishibashi) | coordinates = 33 | coordinates = 46 | coordinates = 13.23 | coordinates = 130 | coordinates = 29 | coordinates = 52.58 | omitted = JP-40 | type = (Estimated) {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} | area = Diameter 25m | Burial Facility = {{ill|Side-entrance chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}} type {{ill|Stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}
(Wooden coffin inside) | burial goods = Iron products, horse gear, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}}, Silla pottery | associated_tombs = | period = Early [[7th century]] | occupant = | imperial_tomb = | historic_site = Fukutsu City Designated Historic Site "Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun" | tangible_cultural_properties = | notes = | map = Japan Fukuoka | type = Round Kofun | map_dot_label = Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|33|46|30|N|130|29|40|E}} | zoom = 14 | float = right | width = 250| height = 200 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun and Miyajidake Kofun}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1 = {{Coord|33|46|13.23|N|130|29|52.58|E}} | map_dot_label1 = Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun | map_dot_label-pos1 = bottom | map_dot_label-color1 = black | mark1 = Round Kofun.png | mark-size1 = 20 | mark-coord2 = {{Coord|33|46|51.10|N|130|29|19.15|E}} | map_dot_label2 = Miyajidake Kofun | map_dot_label-pos2 = right | map_dot_label-color2 = black | mark2 = Round Kofun.png | mark-size2 = 20 }} The '''Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun''' (手光波切不動古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} located in Tebika, {{ill|Fukutsu City|ja|福津市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県|12=draft}}. It is one of the Kofuns that make up the {{ill|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster|ja|津屋崎古墳群|12=draft}} and has been Historic Site1 as a historic site by Fukutsu City. == Overview == This Kofun is located on the terminal end of a hill (22–27 meters above sea level) derived from the foothills of Mount Miyaji, in northern Fukuoka Prefecture. It occupies the southernmost position among the Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster. The stone chamber has been open since ancient times, and the mound has been partially leveled. Detailed investigations of the stone chamber were conducted in [[1992]] (Heisei 4), while surveys of the mound were pertypeed during [[2010]]–[[2011]] (Heisei 22–23). Due to leveling at the mound's base, its exact type is unclear, but the mound is currently estimated to have a diameter of 20 meters and a height of 9 meters. Originally, it is reconstructed to have been approximately 25 meters in diameter{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. The burial facility is a {{ill|Side-entrance chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}} type {{ill|Stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}, opening towards the south-southwest. The total length of the stone chamber is 10.8 meters, and large basalt stones were used as its construction material. Despite the chamber being open for centuries, a survey of the tomb path in front of the chamber revealed wooden coffin components (nails and clamps), as well as iron products, horse gear, and fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}} and Silla pottery{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. The construction of the mound is estimated to date to the early [[7th century]], with possible additional burials through the late 7th century{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. Previously considered to follow the {{ill|Miyajidake Kofun|ja|宮地嶽古墳|12=draft}}, it is now viewed as preceding it, and is considered a successor to the {{ill|Zaiji Kentsuka Kofun|ja|在自剣塚古墳|12=draft}}, the largest {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} in the Munakata region{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. As a tomb adopting the side-entrance chamber structure typical of the Kinai region, it holds significance for understanding the political landscape of the Munakata region{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. In [[1992]] (Heisei 4), the mound was Historic Site1 as a historic site by the typeer Fukuma Town, now Fukutsu City. == Excavation History == * [[Tenpō]] era ([[1830]]–[[1844]]): Mentioned in the ''{{ill|Zoku Fudoki of Chikuzen Province|ja|筑前国続風土記拾遺|12=draft}}'' (described as a faith site with the stone chamber already open, known as Fudo Cave){{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. * [[1992]]: Detailed measurements of the stone chamber{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. * October 13, 1992: Designated as a Fukuma Town Historic Site (now a Fukutsu City Historic Site){{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. * [[2010]]: Mound survey by the Fukutsu City Board of Education, reported in [[2013]]{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. * [[2011]]: Confirmation survey of the mound's size{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. == burial == [[File:Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Bird's-eye view of the stone chamber}} ]] [[File:Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the stone chamber}} ]] The burial facility is a {{ill|Side-entrance chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}} type {{ill|Stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}, opening south-southwest. The rear part of the main chamber narrows into rear and front chambers, showcasing a multi-chamber structure similar to those in the Kinai region. The area of the stone chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}: * Total length of the stone chamber: 10.8 meters (preserved) * Rear chamber: Length 2.1 meters, width 1.3 meters, height 1.9 meters * Front chamber: Length 1.8 meters, width 1.6 meters, height 2 meters * Passageway The chamber is constructed using basalt slabs, possibly sourced from [[:en:Ainoshima_(Shingū)|Ainoshima]]. Both the passageway and main chamber consist of a single layer of large stones, with five stones on each side wall and one stone on the rear wall. The floor is paved with large flat stones in some parts, while other parts have stone and pebble paving beneath approximately 10 cm of soil{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. == See Also == * [[Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster]] ** [[Miyajidake Kofun]] == References == [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]][[sn:Tebika Namikirifudō Kofun]][[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[sn:Tebika Namikirifudō Kofun]][[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]][[sn:Tebika Namikirifudō Kofun]][[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[sn:Tebika Namikirifudō Kofun]]{{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Burial sites in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Fukutsu City History]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Munakata clan|Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun]] [[Category:Tebika Namikirifudō Kofun]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Tebika Namikirifudō Kofun]] [[en:Tebika Namikirifudō Kofun]] [[ja:手光波切不動古墳]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]][[sn:Tebika Namikirifudō Kofun]][[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[sn:Tebika Namikirifudō Kofun]]{{Kofun navbar}}

In [[1992]] (Heisei 4), the mound was Historic Site1 as a historic site by the typeer Fukuma Town, now Fukutsu City. == Excavation History == * [[Tenpō]] era ([[1830]]–[[1844]]): Mentioned in the ''{{ill|Zoku Fudoki of Chikuzen Province|ja|筑前国続風土記拾遺|12=draft}}'' (described as a faith site with the stone chamber already open, known as Fudo Cave){{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. * [[1992]]: Detailed measurements of the stone chamber{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. * October 13, 1992: Designated as a Fukuma Town Historic Site (now a Fukutsu City Historic Site){{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. * [[2010]]: Mound survey by the Fukutsu City Board of Education, reported in [[2013]]{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. * [[2011]]: Confirmation survey of the mound's size{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. == burial == [[File:Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Bird's-eye view of the stone chamber}} ]] [[File:Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the stone chamber}} ]] The burial facility is a {{ill|Side-entrance chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}} type {{ill|Stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}, opening south-southwest. The rear part of the main chamber narrows into rear and front chambers, showcasing a multi-chamber structure similar to those in the Kinai region. The area of the stone chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}: * Total length of the stone chamber: 10.8 meters (preserved) * Rear chamber: Length 2.1 meters, width 1.3 meters, height 1.9 meters * Front chamber: Length 1.8 meters, width 1.6 meters, height 2 meters * Passageway The chamber is constructed using basalt slabs, possibly sourced from [[:en:Ainoshima_(Shingū)|Ainoshima]]. Both the passageway and main chamber consist of a single layer of large stones, with five stones on each side wall and one stone on the rear wall. The floor is paved with large flat stones in some parts, while other parts have stone and pebble paving beneath approximately 10 cm of soil{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. == See Also == * [[Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster]] ** [[Miyajidake Kofun]] == References == [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]][[sn:Tebika Namikirifudō Kofun]][[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[sn:Tebika Namikirifudō Kofun]][[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]][[sn:Tebika Namikirifudō Kofun]][[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[sn:Tebika Namikirifudō Kofun]]{{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Burial sites in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Fukutsu City History]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Munakata clan|Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun]] [[Category:Tebika Namikirifudō Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Tebika Namikirifudō Kofun]] [[en:Tebika Namikirifudō Kofun]] [[ja:手光波切不動古墳]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]][[sn:Tebika Namikirifudō Kofun]][[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[sn:Tebika Namikirifudō Kofun]]{{Kofun navbar}}

revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:New]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Japanese Kofun | name = Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun | image = [[File:Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]] | caption = Mound and stone chamber entrance | alternate_name = | part_of = {{ill|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster|ja|津屋崎古墳群|12=draft}} | location = {{ill|Fukutsu City|ja|福津市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県|12=draft}}, Tebika 1615 (Ishibashi) | coordinates = 33 | coordinates = 46 | coordinates = 13.23 | coordinates = 130 | coordinates = 29 | coordinates = 52.58 | omitted = JP-40 | type = (Estimated) {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} | area = Diameter 25m | Burial Facility = {{ill|Side-entrance chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}} type {{ill|Stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}
(Wooden coffin inside) | burial goods = Iron products, horse gear, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}}, Silla pottery | associated_tombs = | period = Early [[7th century]] | occupant = | imperial_tomb = | historic_site = Fukutsu City Designated Historic Site "Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun" | tangible_cultural_properties = | notes = | map = Japan Fukuoka | type = Round Kofun | map_dot_label = Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|33|46|30|N|130|29|40|E}} | zoom = 14 | float = right | width = 250| height = 200 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun and Miyajidake Kofun}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1 = {{Coord|33|46|13.23|N|130|29|52.58|E}} | map_dot_label1 = Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun | map_dot_label-pos1 = bottom | map_dot_label-color1 = black | mark1 = Round Kofun.png | mark-size1 = 20 | mark-coord2 = {{Coord|33|46|51.10|N|130|29|19.15|E}} | map_dot_label2 = Miyajidake Kofun | map_dot_label-pos2 = right | map_dot_label-color2 = black | mark2 = Round Kofun.png | mark-size2 = 20 }} The '''Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun''' (手光波切不動古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} located in Tebika, {{ill|Fukutsu City|ja|福津市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県|12=draft}}. It is one of the Kofuns that make up the {{ill|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster|ja|津屋崎古墳群|12=draft}} and has been Historic Site1 as a historic site by Fukutsu City. == Overview == This Kofun is located on the terminal end of a hill (22–27 meters above sea level) derived from the foothills of Mount Miyaji, in northern Fukuoka Prefecture. It occupies the southernmost position among the Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster. The stone chamber has been open since ancient times, and the mound has been partially leveled. Detailed investigations of the stone chamber were conducted in [[1992]] (Heisei 4), while surveys of the mound were pertypeed during [[2010]]–[[2011]] (Heisei 22–23). Due to leveling at the mound's base, its exact type is unclear, but the mound is currently estimated to have a diameter of 20 meters and a height of 9 meters. Originally, it is reconstructed to have been approximately 25 meters in diameter{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. The burial facility is a {{ill|Side-entrance chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}} type {{ill|Stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}, opening towards the south-southwest. The total length of the stone chamber is 10.8 meters, and large basalt stones were used as its construction material. Despite the chamber being open for centuries, a survey of the tomb path in front of the chamber revealed wooden coffin components (nails and clamps), as well as iron products, horse gear, and fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}} and Silla pottery{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. The construction of the mound is estimated to date to the early [[7th century]], with possible additional burials through the late 7th century{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. Previously considered to follow the {{ill|Miyajidake Kofun|ja|宮地嶽古墳|12=draft}}, it is now viewed as preceding it, and is considered a successor to the {{ill|Zaiji Kentsuka Kofun|ja|在自剣塚古墳|12=draft}}, the largest {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} in the Munakata region{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. As a tomb adopting the side-entrance chamber structure typical of the Kinai region, it holds significance for understanding the political landscape of the Munakata region{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. In [[1992]] (Heisei 4), the mound was Historic Site1 as a historic site by the typeer Fukuma Town, now Fukutsu City. == Excavation History == * [[Tenpō]] era ([[1830]]–[[1844]]): Mentioned in the ''{{ill|Zoku Fudoki of Chikuzen Province|ja|筑前国続風土記拾遺|12=draft}}'' (described as a faith site with the stone chamber already open, known as Fudo Cave){{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. * [[1992]]: Detailed measurements of the stone chamber{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. * October 13, 1992: Designated as a Fukuma Town Historic Site (now a Fukutsu City Historic Site){{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. * [[2010]]: Mound survey by the Fukutsu City Board of Education, reported in [[2013]]{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. * [[2011]]: Confirmation survey of the mound's size{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. == burial == [[File:Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Bird's-eye view of the stone chamber}} ]] [[File:Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the stone chamber}} ]] The burial facility is a {{ill|Side-entrance chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}} type {{ill|Stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}, opening south-southwest. The rear part of the main chamber narrows into rear and front chambers, showcasing a multi-chamber structure similar to those in the Kinai region. The area of the stone chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}: * Total length of the stone chamber: 10.8 meters (preserved) * Rear chamber: Length 2.1 meters, width 1.3 meters, height 1.9 meters * Front chamber: Length 1.8 meters, width 1.6 meters, height 2 meters * Passageway The chamber is constructed using basalt slabs, possibly sourced from [[:en:Ainoshima_(Shingū)|Ainoshima]]. Both the passageway and main chamber consist of a single layer of large stones, with five stones on each side wall and one stone on the rear wall. The floor is paved with large flat stones in some parts, while other parts have stone and pebble paving beneath approximately 10 cm of soil{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. == See Also == * [[Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster]] ** [[Miyajidake Kofun]] == References == [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]][[sn:Tebika Namikirifudō Kofun]][[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[sn:Tebika Namikirifudō Kofun]][[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]][[sn:Tebika Namikirifudō Kofun]][[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[sn:Tebika Namikirifudō Kofun]]{{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Burial sites in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Fukutsu City History]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Munakata clan|Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun]] [[Category:Tebika Namikirifudō Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Tebika Namikirifudō Kofun]] [[en:Tebika Namikirifudō Kofun]] [[ja:手光波切不動古墳]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]][[sn:Tebika Namikirifudō Kofun]][[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[sn:Tebika Namikirifudō Kofun]]{{Kofun navbar}}

But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Japanese Kofun | name = Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun | image = [[File:Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]] | caption = Mound and stone chamber entrance | alternate_name = | part_of = {{ill|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster|ja|津屋崎古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}} | location = {{ill|Fukutsu City|ja|福津市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県|12=draft|12=draft}}, Tebika 1615 (Ishibashi) | coordinates = 33 | coordinates = 46 | coordinates = 13.23 | coordinates = 130 | coordinates = 29 | coordinates = 52.58 | omitted = JP-40 | type = (Estimated) {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} | area = Diameter 25m | Burial Facility = {{ill|Side-entrance chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} type {{ill|Stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}}
(Wooden coffin inside) | burial goods = Iron products, horse gear, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft|12=draft}}, Silla pottery | associated_tombs = | period = Early [[7th century]] | occupant = | imperial_tomb = | historic_site = Fukutsu City Designated Historic Site "Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun" | tangible_cultural_properties = | notes = | map = Japan Fukuoka | type = Round Kofun | map_dot_label = Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|33|46|30|N|130|29|40|E}} | zoom = 14 | float = right | width = 250| height = 200 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun and Miyajidake Kofun}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1 = {{Coord|33|46|13.23|N|130|29|52.58|E}} | map_dot_label1 = Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun | map_dot_label-pos1 = bottom | map_dot_label-color1 = black | mark1 = Round Kofun.png | mark-size1 = 20 | mark-coord2 = {{Coord|33|46|51.10|N|130|29|19.15|E}} | map_dot_label2 = Miyajidake Kofun | map_dot_label-pos2 = right | map_dot_label-color2 = black | mark2 = Round Kofun.png | mark-size2 = 20 }} The '''Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun''' (手光波切不動古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} located in Tebika, {{ill|Fukutsu City|ja|福津市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県|12=draft|12=draft}}. It is one of the Kofuns that make up the {{ill|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster|ja|津屋崎古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}} and has been Historic Site1 as a historic site by Fukutsu City. == Overview == This Kofun is located on the terminal end of a hill (22–27 meters above sea level) derived from the foothills of Mount Miyaji, in northern Fukuoka Prefecture. It occupies the southernmost position among the Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster. The stone chamber has been open since ancient times, and the mound has been partially leveled. Detailed investigations of the stone chamber were conducted in [[1992]] (Heisei 4), while surveys of the mound were pertypeed during [[2010]]–[[2011]] (Heisei 22–23). Due to leveling at the mound's base, its exact type is unclear, but the mound is currently estimated to have a diameter of 20 meters and a height of 9 meters. Originally, it is reconstructed to have been approximately 25 meters in diameter{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. The burial facility is a {{ill|Side-entrance chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} type {{ill|Stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}}, opening towards the south-southwest. The total length of the stone chamber is 10.8 meters, and large basalt stones were used as its construction material. Despite the chamber being open for centuries, a survey of the tomb path in front of the chamber revealed wooden coffin components (nails and clamps), as well as iron products, horse gear, and fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft|12=draft}} and Silla pottery{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. The construction of the mound is estimated to date to the early [[7th century]], with possible additional burials through the late 7th century{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. Previously considered to follow the {{ill|Miyajidake Kofun|ja|宮地嶽古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}, it is now viewed as preceding it, and is considered a successor to the {{ill|Zaiji Kentsuka Kofun|ja|在自剣塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}, the largest {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}} in the Munakata region{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. As a tomb adopting the side-entrance chamber structure typical of the Kinai region, it holds significance for understanding the political landscape of the Munakata region{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. In [[1992]] (Heisei 4), the mound was Historic Site1 as a historic site by the typeer Fukuma Town, now Fukutsu City. == Excavation History == * [[Tenpō]] era ([[1830]]–[[1844]]): Mentioned in the ''{{ill|Zoku Fudoki of Chikuzen Province|ja|筑前国続風土記拾遺|12=draft|12=draft}}'' (described as a faith site with the stone chamber already open, known as Fudo Cave){{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. * [[1992]]: Detailed measurements of the stone chamber{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. * October 13, 1992: Designated as a Fukuma Town Historic Site (now a Fukutsu City Historic Site){{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. * [[2010]]: Mound survey by the Fukutsu City Board of Education, reported in [[2013]]{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. * [[2011]]: Confirmation survey of the mound's size{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. == burial == [[File:Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Bird's-eye view of the stone chamber}} ]] [[File:Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the stone chamber}} ]] The burial facility is a {{ill|Side-entrance chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} type {{ill|Stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}}, opening south-southwest. The rear part of the main chamber narrows into rear and front chambers, showcasing a multi-chamber structure similar to those in the Kinai region. The area of the stone chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}: * Total length of the stone chamber: 10.8 meters (preserved) * Rear chamber: Length 2.1 meters, width 1.3 meters, height 1.9 meters * Front chamber: Length 1.8 meters, width 1.6 meters, height 2 meters * Passageway The chamber is constructed using basalt slabs, possibly sourced from [[:en:Ainoshima_(Shingū)|Ainoshima]]. Both the passageway and main chamber consist of a single layer of large stones, with five stones on each side wall and one stone on the rear wall. The floor is paved with large flat stones in some parts, while other parts have stone and pebble paving beneath approximately 10 cm of soil{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. == See Also == * [[Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster]] ** [[Miyajidake Kofun]] == References == {{draft categories| }} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Burial sites in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Fukutsu City History]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Munakata clan|Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun]] [[Category:Tebika Namikirifudō Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Tebika Namikirifudō Kofun]] [[en:Tebika Namikirifudō Kofun]] [[ja:手光波切不動古墳]]

As a tomb adopting the side-entrance chamber structure typical of the Kinai region, it holds significance for understanding the political landscape of the Munakata region{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. In [[1992]] (Heisei 4), the mound was Historic Site1 as a historic site by the typeer Fukuma Town, now Fukutsu City. == Excavation History == * [[Tenpō]] era ([[1830]]–[[1844]]): Mentioned in the ''{{ill|Zoku Fudoki of Chikuzen Province|ja|筑前国続風土記拾遺|12=draft|12=draft}}'' (described as a faith site with the stone chamber already open, known as Fudo Cave){{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. * [[1992]]: Detailed measurements of the stone chamber{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. * October 13, 1992: Designated as a Fukuma Town Historic Site (now a Fukutsu City Historic Site){{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. * [[2010]]: Mound survey by the Fukutsu City Board of Education, reported in [[2013]]{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. * [[2011]]: Confirmation survey of the mound's size{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. == burial == [[File:Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Bird's-eye view of the stone chamber}} ]] [[File:Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the stone chamber}} ]] The burial facility is a {{ill|Side-entrance chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} type {{ill|Stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}}, opening south-southwest. The rear part of the main chamber narrows into rear and front chambers, showcasing a multi-chamber structure similar to those in the Kinai region. The area of the stone chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}: * Total length of the stone chamber: 10.8 meters (preserved) * Rear chamber: Length 2.1 meters, width 1.3 meters, height 1.9 meters * Front chamber: Length 1.8 meters, width 1.6 meters, height 2 meters * Passageway The chamber is constructed using basalt slabs, possibly sourced from [[:en:Ainoshima_(Shingū)|Ainoshima]]. Both the passageway and main chamber consist of a single layer of large stones, with five stones on each side wall and one stone on the rear wall. The floor is paved with large flat stones in some parts, while other parts have stone and pebble paving beneath approximately 10 cm of soil{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. == See Also == * [[Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster]] ** [[Miyajidake Kofun]] == References == {{draft categories| }} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Burial sites in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Fukutsu City History]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Munakata clan|Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun]] [[Category:Tebika Namikirifudō Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tebika Namikirifudō Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tebika Namikirifudō Kofun]] [[en:Tebika Namikirifudō Kofun]] [[ja:手光波切不動古墳]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "|前方後円墳|" to "|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|"
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=See [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Japanese Kofun | name = Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun | image = [[File:Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]] | caption = Mound and stone chamber entrance | alternate_name = | part_of = {{ill|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster|ja|津屋崎古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}} | location = {{ill|Fukutsu City|ja|福津市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県|12=draft|12=draft}}, Tebika 1615 (Ishibashi) | coordinates = 33 | coordinates = 46 | coordinates = 13.23 | coordinates = 130 | coordinates = 29 | coordinates = 52.58 | omitted = JP-40 | type = (Estimated) {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} | area = Diameter 25m | Burial Facility = {{ill|Side-entrance chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} type {{ill|Stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}}
(Wooden coffin inside) | burial goods = Iron products, horse gear, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft|12=draft}}, Silla pottery | associated_tombs = | period = Early [[7th century]] | occupant = | imperial_tomb = | historic_site = Fukutsu City Designated Historic Site "Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun" | tangible_cultural_properties = | notes = | map = Japan Fukuoka | type = Round Kofun | map_dot_label = Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|33|46|30|N|130|29|40|E}} | zoom = 14 | float = right | width = 250| height = 200 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun and Miyajidake Kofun}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1 = {{Coord|33|46|13.23|N|130|29|52.58|E}} | map_dot_label1 = Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun | map_dot_label-pos1 = bottom | map_dot_label-color1 = black | mark1 = Round Kofun.png | mark-size1 = 20 | mark-coord2 = {{Coord|33|46|51.10|N|130|29|19.15|E}} | map_dot_label2 = Miyajidake Kofun | map_dot_label-pos2 = right | map_dot_label-color2 = black | mark2 = Round Kofun.png | mark-size2 = 20 }} The '''Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun''' (手光波切不動古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} located in Tebika, {{ill|Fukutsu City|ja|福津市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県|12=draft|12=draft}}. It is one of the Kofuns that make up the {{ill|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster|ja|津屋崎古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}} and has been Historic Site1 as a historic site by Fukutsu City. == Overview == This Kofun is located on the terminal end of a hill (22–27 meters above sea level) derived from the foothills of Mount Miyaji, in northern Fukuoka Prefecture. It occupies the southernmost position among the Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster. The stone chamber has been open since ancient times, and the mound has been partially leveled. Detailed investigations of the stone chamber were conducted in [[1992]] (Heisei 4), while surveys of the mound were pertypeed during [[2010]]–[[2011]] (Heisei 22–23). Due to leveling at the mound's base, its exact type is unclear, but the mound is currently estimated to have a diameter of 20 meters and a height of 9 meters. Originally, it is reconstructed to have been approximately 25 meters in diameter{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. The burial facility is a {{ill|Side-entrance chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} type {{ill|Stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}}, opening towards the south-southwest. The total length of the stone chamber is 10.8 meters, and large basalt stones were used as its construction material. Despite the chamber being open for centuries, a survey of the tomb path in front of the chamber revealed wooden coffin components (nails and clamps), as well as iron products, horse gear, and fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft|12=draft}} and Silla pottery{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. The construction of the mound is estimated to date to the early [[7th century]], with possible additional burials through the late 7th century{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. Previously considered to follow the {{ill|Miyajidake Kofun|ja|宮地嶽古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}, it is now viewed as preceding it, and is considered a successor to the {{ill|Zaiji Kentsuka Kofun|ja|在自剣塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}, the largest {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}} in the Munakata region{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. As a tomb adopting the side-entrance chamber structure typical of the Kinai region, it holds significance for understanding the political landscape of the Munakata region{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. In [[1992]] (Heisei 4), the mound was Historic Site1 as a historic site by the typeer Fukuma Town, now Fukutsu City. == Excavation History == * [[Tenpō]] era ([[1830]]–[[1844]]): Mentioned in the ''{{ill|Zoku Fudoki of Chikuzen Province|ja|筑前国続風土記拾遺|12=draft|12=draft}}'' (described as a faith site with the stone chamber already open, known as Fudo Cave){{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. * [[1992]]: Detailed measurements of the stone chamber{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. * October 13, 1992: Designated as a Fukuma Town Historic Site (now a Fukutsu City Historic Site){{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. * [[2010]]: Mound survey by the Fukutsu City Board of Education, reported in [[2013]]{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. * [[2011]]: Confirmation survey of the mound's size{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. == burial == [[File:Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Bird's-eye view of the stone chamber}} ]] [[File:Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the stone chamber}} ]] The burial facility is a {{ill|Side-entrance chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} type {{ill|Stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}}, opening south-southwest. The rear part of the main chamber narrows into rear and front chambers, showcasing a multi-chamber structure similar to those in the Kinai region. The area of the stone chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}: * Total length of the stone chamber: 10.8 meters (preserved) * Rear chamber: Length 2.1 meters, width 1.3 meters, height 1.9 meters * Front chamber: Length 1.8 meters, width 1.6 meters, height 2 meters * Passageway The chamber is constructed using basalt slabs, possibly sourced from [[:en:Ainoshima_(Shingū)|Ainoshima]]. Both the passageway and main chamber consist of a single layer of large stones, with five stones on each side wall and one stone on the rear wall. The floor is paved with large flat stones in some parts, while other parts have stone and pebble paving beneath approximately 10 cm of soil{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. == See Also == * [[Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster]] ** [[Miyajidake Kofun]] == References == {{draft categories| }} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Burial sites in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Fukutsu City History]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Munakata clan|Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun]] [[Category:Tebika Namikirifudō Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tebika Namikirifudō Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tebika Namikirifudō Kofun]] [[en:Tebika Namikirifudō Kofun]] [[ja:手光波切不動古墳]]

But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Japanese Kofun | name = Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun | image = [[File:Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]] | caption = Mound and stone chamber entrance | alternate_name = | part_of = {{ill|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster|ja|津屋崎古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}} | location = {{ill|Fukutsu City|ja|福津市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県|12=draft|12=draft}}, Tebika 1615 (Ishibashi) | coordinates = 33 | coordinates = 46 | coordinates = 13.23 | coordinates = 130 | coordinates = 29 | coordinates = 52.58 | omitted = JP-40 | type = (Estimated) {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} | area = Diameter 25m | Burial Facility = {{ill|Side-entrance chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} type {{ill|Stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}}
(Wooden coffin inside) | burial goods = Iron products, horse gear, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft|12=draft}}, Silla pottery | associated_tombs = | period = Early [[7th century]] | occupant = | imperial_tomb = | historic_site = Fukutsu City Designated Historic Site "Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun" | tangible_cultural_properties = | notes = | map = Japan Fukuoka | type = Round Kofun | map_dot_label = Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|33|46|30|N|130|29|40|E}} | zoom = 14 | float = right | width = 250| height = 200 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun and Miyajidake Kofun}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1 = {{Coord|33|46|13.23|N|130|29|52.58|E}} | map_dot_label1 = Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun | map_dot_label-pos1 = bottom | map_dot_label-color1 = black | mark1 = Round Kofun.png | mark-size1 = 20 | mark-coord2 = {{Coord|33|46|51.10|N|130|29|19.15|E}} | map_dot_label2 = Miyajidake Kofun | map_dot_label-pos2 = right | map_dot_label-color2 = black | mark2 = Round Kofun.png | mark-size2 = 20 }} The '''Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun''' (手光波切不動古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} located in Tebika, {{ill|Fukutsu City|ja|福津市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県|12=draft|12=draft}}. It is one of the Kofuns that make up the {{ill|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster|ja|津屋崎古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}} and has been Historic Site1 as a historic site by Fukutsu City. == Overview == This Kofun is located on the terminal end of a hill (22–27 meters above sea level) derived from the foothills of Mount Miyaji, in northern Fukuoka Prefecture. It occupies the southernmost position among the Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster. The stone chamber has been open since ancient times, and the mound has been partially leveled. Detailed investigations of the stone chamber were conducted in [[1992]] (Heisei 4), while surveys of the mound were pertypeed during [[2010]]–[[2011]] (Heisei 22–23). Due to leveling at the mound's base, its exact type is unclear, but the mound is currently estimated to have a diameter of 20 meters and a height of 9 meters. Originally, it is reconstructed to have been approximately 25 meters in diameter{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. The burial facility is a {{ill|Side-entrance chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} type {{ill|Stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}}, opening towards the south-southwest. The total length of the stone chamber is 10.8 meters, and large basalt stones were used as its construction material. Despite the chamber being open for centuries, a survey of the tomb path in front of the chamber revealed wooden coffin components (nails and clamps), as well as iron products, horse gear, and fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft|12=draft}} and Silla pottery{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. The construction of the mound is estimated to date to the early [[7th century]], with possible additional burials through the late 7th century{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. Previously considered to follow the {{ill|Miyajidake Kofun|ja|宮地嶽古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}, it is now viewed as preceding it, and is considered a successor to the {{ill|Zaiji Kentsuka Kofun|ja|在自剣塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}, the largest {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}} in the Munakata region{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. As a tomb adopting the side-entrance chamber structure typical of the Kinai region, it holds significance for understanding the political landscape of the Munakata region{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. In [[1992]] (Heisei 4), the mound was Historic Site1 as a historic site by the typeer Fukuma Town, now Fukutsu City. == Excavation History == * [[Tenpō]] era ([[1830]]–[[1844]]): Mentioned in the ''{{ill|Zoku Fudoki of Chikuzen Province|ja|筑前国続風土記拾遺|12=draft|12=draft}}'' (described as a faith site with the stone chamber already open, known as Fudo Cave){{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. * [[1992]]: Detailed measurements of the stone chamber{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. * October 13, 1992: Designated as a Fukuma Town Historic Site (now a Fukutsu City Historic Site){{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. * [[2010]]: Mound survey by the Fukutsu City Board of Education, reported in [[2013]]{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. * [[2011]]: Confirmation survey of the mound's size{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. == burial == [[File:Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Bird's-eye view of the stone chamber}} ]] [[File:Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the stone chamber}} ]] The burial facility is a {{ill|Side-entrance chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} type {{ill|Stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}}, opening south-southwest. The rear part of the main chamber narrows into rear and front chambers, showcasing a multi-chamber structure similar to those in the Kinai region. The area of the stone chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}: * Total length of the stone chamber: 10.8 meters (preserved) * Rear chamber: Length 2.1 meters, width 1.3 meters, height 1.9 meters * Front chamber: Length 1.8 meters, width 1.6 meters, height 2 meters * Passageway The chamber is constructed using basalt slabs, possibly sourced from [[:en:Ainoshima_(Shingū)|Ainoshima]]. Both the passageway and main chamber consist of a single layer of large stones, with five stones on each side wall and one stone on the rear wall. The floor is paved with large flat stones in some parts, while other parts have stone and pebble paving beneath approximately 10 cm of soil{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. == See Also == * [[Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster]] ** [[Miyajidake Kofun]] == References == {{draft categories| }} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Burial sites in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Fukutsu City History]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Munakata clan|Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun]] [[Category:Tebika Namikirifudō Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tebika Namikirifudō Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tebika Namikirifudō Kofun]] [[en:Tebika Namikirifudō Kofun]] [[ja:手光波切不動古墳]]

Despite the chamber being open for centuries, a survey of the tomb path in front of the chamber revealed wooden coffin components (nails and clamps), as well as iron products, horse gear, and fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft|12=draft}} and Silla pottery{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. The construction of the mound is estimated to date to the early [[7th century]], with possible additional burials through the late 7th century{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. Previously considered to follow the {{ill|Miyajidake Kofun|ja|宮地嶽古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}, it is now viewed as preceding it, and is considered a successor to the {{ill|Zaiji Kentsuka Kofun|ja|在自剣塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}, the largest {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}} in the Munakata region{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. As a tomb adopting the side-entrance chamber structure typical of the Kinai region, it holds significance for understanding the political landscape of the Munakata region{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. In [[1992]] (Heisei 4), the mound was Historic Site1 as a historic site by the typeer Fukuma Town, now Fukutsu City. == Excavation History == * [[Tenpō]] era ([[1830]]–[[1844]]): Mentioned in the ''{{ill|Zoku Fudoki of Chikuzen Province|ja|筑前国続風土記拾遺|12=draft|12=draft}}'' (described as a faith site with the stone chamber already open, known as Fudo Cave){{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. * [[1992]]: Detailed measurements of the stone chamber{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. * October 13, 1992: Designated as a Fukuma Town Historic Site (now a Fukutsu City Historic Site){{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. * [[2010]]: Mound survey by the Fukutsu City Board of Education, reported in [[2013]]{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. * [[2011]]: Confirmation survey of the mound's size{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. == burial == [[File:Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Bird's-eye view of the stone chamber}} ]] [[File:Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the stone chamber}} ]] The burial facility is a {{ill|Side-entrance chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} type {{ill|Stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}}, opening south-southwest. The rear part of the main chamber narrows into rear and front chambers, showcasing a multi-chamber structure similar to those in the Kinai region. The area of the stone chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}: * Total length of the stone chamber: 10.8 meters (preserved) * Rear chamber: Length 2.1 meters, width 1.3 meters, height 1.9 meters * Front chamber: Length 1.8 meters, width 1.6 meters, height 2 meters * Passageway The chamber is constructed using basalt slabs, possibly sourced from [[:en:Ainoshima_(Shingū)|Ainoshima]]. Both the passageway and main chamber consist of a single layer of large stones, with five stones on each side wall and one stone on the rear wall. The floor is paved with large flat stones in some parts, while other parts have stone and pebble paving beneath approximately 10 cm of soil{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. == See Also == * [[Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster]] ** [[Miyajidake Kofun]] == References == {{draft categories| }} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Burial sites in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Fukutsu City History]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Munakata clan|Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun]] [[Category:Tebika Namikirifudō Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tebika Namikirifudō Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tebika Namikirifudō Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tebika Namikirifudō Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tebika Namikirifudō Kofun]] [[en:Tebika Namikirifudō Kofun]] [[ja:手光波切不動古墳]]

{{afc comment|1=See [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Japanese Kofun | name = Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun | image = [[File:Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]] | caption = Mound and stone chamber entrance | alternate_name = | part_of = {{ill|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster|ja|津屋崎古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}} | location = {{ill|Fukutsu City|ja|福津市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県|12=draft|12=draft}}, Tebika 1615 (Ishibashi) | coordinates = 33 | coordinates = 46 | coordinates = 13.23 | coordinates = 130 | coordinates = 29 | coordinates = 52.58 | omitted = JP-40 | type = (Estimated) {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} | area = Diameter 25m | Burial Facility = {{ill|Side-entrance chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} type {{ill|Stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}}
(Wooden coffin inside) | burial goods = Iron products, horse gear, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft|12=draft}}, Silla pottery | associated_tombs = | period = Early [[7th century]] | occupant = | imperial_tomb = | historic_site = Fukutsu City Designated Historic Site "Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun" | tangible_cultural_properties = | notes = | map = Japan Fukuoka | type = Round Kofun | map_dot_label = Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|33|46|30|N|130|29|40|E}} | zoom = 14 | float = right | width = 250| height = 200 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun and Miyajidake Kofun}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1 = {{Coord|33|46|13.23|N|130|29|52.58|E}} | map_dot_label1 = Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun | map_dot_label-pos1 = bottom | map_dot_label-color1 = black | mark1 = Round Kofun.png | mark-size1 = 20 | mark-coord2 = {{Coord|33|46|51.10|N|130|29|19.15|E}} | map_dot_label2 = Miyajidake Kofun | map_dot_label-pos2 = right | map_dot_label-color2 = black | mark2 = Round Kofun.png | mark-size2 = 20 }} The '''Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun''' (手光波切不動古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} located in Tebika, {{ill|Fukutsu City|ja|福津市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県|12=draft|12=draft}}. It is one of the Kofuns that make up the {{ill|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster|ja|津屋崎古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}} and has been Historic Site1 as a historic site by Fukutsu City. == Overview == This Kofun is located on the terminal end of a hill (22–27 meters above sea level) derived from the foothills of Mount Miyaji, in northern Fukuoka Prefecture. It occupies the southernmost position among the Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster. The stone chamber has been open since ancient times, and the mound has been partially leveled. Detailed investigations of the stone chamber were conducted in [[1992]] (Heisei 4), while surveys of the mound were pertypeed during [[2010]]–[[2011]] (Heisei 22–23). Due to leveling at the mound's base, its exact type is unclear, but the mound is currently estimated to have a diameter of 20 meters and a height of 9 meters. Originally, it is reconstructed to have been approximately 25 meters in diameter{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. The burial facility is a {{ill|Side-entrance chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} type {{ill|Stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}}, opening towards the south-southwest. The total length of the stone chamber is 10.8 meters, and large basalt stones were used as its construction material. Despite the chamber being open for centuries, a survey of the tomb path in front of the chamber revealed wooden coffin components (nails and clamps), as well as iron products, horse gear, and fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft|12=draft}} and Silla pottery{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. The construction of the mound is estimated to date to the early [[7th century]], with possible additional burials through the late 7th century{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. Previously considered to follow the {{ill|Miyajidake Kofun|ja|宮地嶽古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}, it is now viewed as preceding it, and is considered a successor to the {{ill|Zaiji Kentsuka Kofun|ja|在自剣塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}, the largest {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}} in the Munakata region{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. As a tomb adopting the side-entrance chamber structure typical of the Kinai region, it holds significance for understanding the political landscape of the Munakata region{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. In [[1992]] (Heisei 4), the mound was Historic Site1 as a historic site by the typeer Fukuma Town, now Fukutsu City. == Excavation History == * [[Tenpō]] era ([[1830]]–[[1844]]): Mentioned in the ''{{ill|Zoku Fudoki of Chikuzen Province|ja|筑前国続風土記拾遺|12=draft|12=draft}}'' (described as a faith site with the stone chamber already open, known as Fudo Cave){{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. * [[1992]]: Detailed measurements of the stone chamber{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. * October 13, 1992: Designated as a Fukuma Town Historic Site (now a Fukutsu City Historic Site){{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. * [[2010]]: Mound survey by the Fukutsu City Board of Education, reported in [[2013]]{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. * [[2011]]: Confirmation survey of the mound's size{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. == burial == [[File:Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Bird's-eye view of the stone chamber}} ]] [[File:Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the stone chamber}} ]] The burial facility is a {{ill|Side-entrance chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} type {{ill|Stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}}, opening south-southwest. The rear part of the main chamber narrows into rear and front chambers, showcasing a multi-chamber structure similar to those in the Kinai region. The area of the stone chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}: * Total length of the stone chamber: 10.8 meters (preserved) * Rear chamber: Length 2.1 meters, width 1.3 meters, height 1.9 meters * Front chamber: Length 1.8 meters, width 1.6 meters, height 2 meters * Passageway The chamber is constructed using basalt slabs, possibly sourced from [[:en:Ainoshima_(Shingū)|Ainoshima]]. Both the passageway and main chamber consist of a single layer of large stones, with five stones on each side wall and one stone on the rear wall. The floor is paved with large flat stones in some parts, while other parts have stone and pebble paving beneath approximately 10 cm of soil{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. == See Also == * [[Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster]] ** [[Miyajidake Kofun]] == References == {{draft categories| }} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Burial sites in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Fukutsu City History]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Munakata clan|Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun]] [[Category:Tebika Namikirifudō Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tebika Namikirifudō Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tebika Namikirifudō Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tebika Namikirifudō Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tebika Namikirifudō Kofun]] [[en:Tebika Namikirifudō Kofun]] [[ja:手光波切不動古墳]]

So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Japanese Kofun | name = Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun | image = [[File:Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]] | caption = Mound and stone chamber entrance | alternate_name = | part_of = {{ill|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster|ja|津屋崎古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}} | location = {{ill|Fukutsu City|ja|福津市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県|12=draft|12=draft}}, Tebika 1615 (Ishibashi) | coordinates = 33 | coordinates = 46 | coordinates = 13.23 | coordinates = 130 | coordinates = 29 | coordinates = 52.58 | omitted = JP-40 | type = (Estimated) {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} | area = Diameter 25m | Burial Facility = {{ill|Side-entrance chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} type {{ill|Stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}}
(Wooden coffin inside) | burial goods = Iron products, horse gear, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft|12=draft}}, Silla pottery | associated_tombs = | period = Early [[7th century]] | occupant = | imperial_tomb = | historic_site = Fukutsu City Designated Historic Site "Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun" | tangible_cultural_properties = | notes = | map = Japan Fukuoka | type = Round Kofun | map_dot_label = Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|33|46|30|N|130|29|40|E}} | zoom = 14 | float = right | width = 250| height = 200 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun and Miyajidake Kofun}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1 = {{Coord|33|46|13.23|N|130|29|52.58|E}} | map_dot_label1 = Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun | map_dot_label-pos1 = bottom | map_dot_label-color1 = black | mark1 = Round Kofun.png | mark-size1 = 20 | mark-coord2 = {{Coord|33|46|51.10|N|130|29|19.15|E}} | map_dot_label2 = Miyajidake Kofun | map_dot_label-pos2 = right | map_dot_label-color2 = black | mark2 = Round Kofun.png | mark-size2 = 20 }} The '''Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun''' (手光波切不動古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} located in Tebika, {{ill|Fukutsu City|ja|福津市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県|12=draft|12=draft}}. It is one of the Kofuns that make up the {{ill|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster|ja|津屋崎古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}} and has been Historic Site1 as a historic site by Fukutsu City. == Overview == This Kofun is located on the terminal end of a hill (22–27 meters above sea level) derived from the foothills of Mount Miyaji, in northern Fukuoka Prefecture. It occupies the southernmost position among the Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster. The stone chamber has been open since ancient times, and the mound has been partially leveled. Detailed investigations of the stone chamber were conducted in [[1992]] (Heisei 4), while surveys of the mound were pertypeed during [[2010]]–[[2011]] (Heisei 22–23). Due to leveling at the mound's base, its exact type is unclear, but the mound is currently estimated to have a diameter of 20 meters and a height of 9 meters. Originally, it is reconstructed to have been approximately 25 meters in diameter{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. The burial facility is a {{ill|Side-entrance chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} type {{ill|Stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}}, opening towards the south-southwest. The total length of the stone chamber is 10.8 meters, and large basalt stones were used as its construction material. Despite the chamber being open for centuries, a survey of the tomb path in front of the chamber revealed wooden coffin components (nails and clamps), as well as iron products, horse gear, and fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft|12=draft}} and Silla pottery{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. The construction of the mound is estimated to date to the early [[7th century]], with possible additional burials through the late 7th century{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. Previously considered to follow the {{ill|Miyajidake Kofun|ja|宮地嶽古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}, it is now viewed as preceding it, and is considered a successor to the {{ill|Zaiji Kentsuka Kofun|ja|在自剣塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}, the largest {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}} in the Munakata region{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. As a tomb adopting the side-entrance chamber structure typical of the Kinai region, it holds significance for understanding the political landscape of the Munakata region{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. In [[1992]] (Heisei 4), the mound was Historic Site1 as a historic site by the typeer Fukuma Town, now Fukutsu City. == Excavation History == * [[Tenpō]] era ([[1830]]–[[1844]]): Mentioned in the ''{{ill|Zoku Fudoki of Chikuzen Province|ja|筑前国続風土記拾遺|12=draft|12=draft}}'' (described as a faith site with the stone chamber already open, known as Fudo Cave){{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. * [[1992]]: Detailed measurements of the stone chamber{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. * October 13, 1992: Designated as a Fukuma Town Historic Site (now a Fukutsu City Historic Site){{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. * [[2010]]: Mound survey by the Fukutsu City Board of Education, reported in [[2013]]{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. * [[2011]]: Confirmation survey of the mound's size{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. == burial == [[File:Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Bird's-eye view of the stone chamber}} ]] [[File:Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the stone chamber}} ]] The burial facility is a {{ill|Side-entrance chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} type {{ill|Stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}}, opening south-southwest. The rear part of the main chamber narrows into rear and front chambers, showcasing a multi-chamber structure similar to those in the Kinai region. The area of the stone chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}: * Total length of the stone chamber: 10.8 meters (preserved) * Rear chamber: Length 2.1 meters, width 1.3 meters, height 1.9 meters * Front chamber: Length 1.8 meters, width 1.6 meters, height 2 meters * Passageway The chamber is constructed using basalt slabs, possibly sourced from [[:en:Ainoshima_(Shingū)|Ainoshima]]. Both the passageway and main chamber consist of a single layer of large stones, with five stones on each side wall and one stone on the rear wall. The floor is paved with large flat stones in some parts, while other parts have stone and pebble paving beneath approximately 10 cm of soil{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. == See Also == * [[Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster]] ** [[Miyajidake Kofun]] == References == {{draft categories| }} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Burial sites in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Fukutsu City History]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Munakata clan|Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun]] [[Category:Tebika Namikirifudō Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tebika Namikirifudō Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tebika Namikirifudō Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tebika Namikirifudō Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tebika Namikirifudō Kofun]] [[en:Tebika Namikirifudō Kofun]] [[ja:手光波切不動古墳]]

(Wooden coffin inside) | burial goods = Iron products, horse gear, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft|12=draft}}, Silla pottery | associated_tombs = | period = Early [[7th century]] | occupant = | imperial_tomb = | historic_site = Fukutsu City Designated Historic Site "Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun" | tangible_cultural_properties = | notes = | map = Japan Fukuoka | type = Round Kofun | map_dot_label = Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|33|46|30|N|130|29|40|E}} | zoom = 14 | float = right | width = 250| height = 200 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun and Miyajidake Kofun}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1 = {{Coord|33|46|13.23|N|130|29|52.58|E}} | map_dot_label1 = Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun | map_dot_label-pos1 = bottom | map_dot_label-color1 = black | mark1 = Round Kofun.png | mark-size1 = 20 | mark-coord2 = {{Coord|33|46|51.10|N|130|29|19.15|E}} | map_dot_label2 = Miyajidake Kofun | map_dot_label-pos2 = right | map_dot_label-color2 = black | mark2 = Round Kofun.png | mark-size2 = 20 }} The '''Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun''' (手光波切不動古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} located in Tebika, {{ill|Fukutsu City|ja|福津市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県|12=draft|12=draft}}. It occupies the southernmost position among the Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster. The stone chamber has been open since ancient times, and the mound has been partially leveled. The total length of the stone chamber is 10.8 meters, and large basalt stones were used as its construction material. Despite the chamber being open for centuries, a survey of the tomb path in front of the chamber revealed wooden coffin components (nails and clamps), as well as iron products, horse gear, and fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft|12=draft}} and Silla pottery{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. As a tomb adopting the side-entrance chamber structure typical of the Kinai region, it holds significance for understanding the political landscape of the Munakata region{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. In [[1992]] (Heisei 4), the mound was Historic Site1 as a historic site by the typeer Fukuma Town, now Fukutsu City. == Excavation History == * [[Tenpō]] era ([[1830]]–[[1844]]): Mentioned in the ''{{ill|Zoku Fudoki of Chikuzen Province|ja|筑前国続風土記拾遺|12=draft|12=draft}}'' (described as a faith site with the stone chamber already open, known as Fudo Cave){{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. * [[1992]]: Detailed measurements of the stone chamber{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. * October 13, 1992: Designated as a Fukuma Town Historic Site (now a Fukutsu City Historic Site){{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. * [[2010]]: Mound survey by the Fukutsu City Board of Education, reported in [[2013]]{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. * [[2011]]: Confirmation survey of the mound's size{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. == burial == [[File:Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Bird's-eye view of the stone chamber}} ]] [[File:Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the stone chamber}} ]] The burial facility is a {{ill|Side-entrance chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} type {{ill|Stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}}, opening south-southwest. The rear part of the main chamber narrows into rear and front chambers, showcasing a multi-chamber structure similar to those in the Kinai region.
