Both the passageway and main chamber consist of a single layer of large stones, with five stones on each side wall and one stone on the rear wall. The floor is paved with large flat stones in some parts, while other parts have stone and pebble paving beneath approximately 10 cm of soil{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. == See Also == * [[Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster]] ** [[Miyajidake Kofun]] == References == {{draft categories| }} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Burial sites in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Fukutsu City History]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Munakata clan|Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun]] [[Category:Tebika Namikirifudō Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tebika Namikirifudō Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tebika Namikirifudō Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tebika Namikirifudō Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tebika Namikirifudō Kofun]] [[en:Tebika Namikirifudō Kofun]] [[ja:手光波切不動古墳]]

(Wooden coffin inside) | burial goods = Iron products, horse gear, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft|12=draft}}, Silla pottery | associated_tombs = | period = Early [[7th century]] | occupant = | imperial_tomb = | historic_site = Fukutsu City Designated Historic Site "Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun" | tangible_cultural_properties = | notes = | map = Japan Fukuoka | type = Round Kofun | map_dot_label = Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|33|46|30|N|130|29|40|E}} | zoom = 14 | float = right | width = 250| height = 200 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun and Miyajidake Kofun}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1 = {{Coord|33|46|13.23|N|130|29|52.58|E}} | map_dot_label1 = Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun | map_dot_label-pos1 = bottom | map_dot_label-color1 = black | mark1 = Round Kofun.png | mark-size1 = 20 | mark-coord2 = {{Coord|33|46|51.10|N|130|29|19.15|E}} | map_dot_label2 = Miyajidake Kofun | map_dot_label-pos2 = right | map_dot_label-color2 = black | mark2 = Round Kofun.png | mark-size2 = 20 }} The '''Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun''' (手光波切不動古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} located in Tebika, {{ill|Fukutsu City|ja|福津市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県|12=draft|12=draft}}. It is one of the Kofuns that make up the {{ill|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster|ja|津屋崎古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}} and has been Historic Site1 as a historic site by Fukutsu City. == Overview == This Kofun is located on the terminal end of a hill (22–27 meters above sea level) derived from the foothills of Mount Miyaji, in northern Fukuoka Prefecture. It occupies the southernmost position among the Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster. The stone chamber has been open since ancient times, and the mound has been partially leveled. Detailed investigations of the stone chamber were conducted in [[1992]] (Heisei 4), while surveys of the mound were pertypeed during [[2010]]–[[2011]] (Heisei 22–23). Due to leveling at the mound's base, its exact type is unclear, but the mound is currently estimated to have a diameter of 20 meters and a height of 9 meters. Originally, it is reconstructed to have been approximately 25 meters in diameter{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. The burial facility is a {{ill|Side-entrance chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} type {{ill|Stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}}, opening towards the south-southwest. The total length of the stone chamber is 10.8 meters, and large basalt stones were used as its construction material. Despite the chamber being open for centuries, a survey of the tomb path in front of the chamber revealed wooden coffin components (nails and clamps), as well as iron products, horse gear, and fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft|12=draft}} and Silla pottery{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. The construction of the mound is estimated to date to the early [[7th century]], with possible additional burials through the late 7th century{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. Previously considered to follow the {{ill|Miyajidake Kofun|ja|宮地嶽古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}, it is now viewed as preceding it, and is considered a successor to the {{ill|Zaiji Kentsuka Kofun|ja|在自剣塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}, the largest {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}} in the Munakata region{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. As a tomb adopting the side-entrance chamber structure typical of the Kinai region, it holds significance for understanding the political landscape of the Munakata region{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. In [[1992]] (Heisei 4), the mound was Historic Site1 as a historic site by the typeer Fukuma Town, now Fukutsu City. == Excavation History == * [[Tenpō]] era ([[1830]]–[[1844]]): Mentioned in the ''{{ill|Zoku Fudoki of Chikuzen Province|ja|筑前国続風土記拾遺|12=draft|12=draft}}'' (described as a faith site with the stone chamber already open, known as Fudo Cave){{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. * [[1992]]: Detailed measurements of the stone chamber{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. * October 13, 1992: Designated as a Fukuma Town Historic Site (now a Fukutsu City Historic Site){{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. * [[2010]]: Mound survey by the Fukutsu City Board of Education, reported in [[2013]]{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. * [[2011]]: Confirmation survey of the mound's size{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. == burial == [[File:Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Bird's-eye view of the stone chamber}} ]] [[File:Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the stone chamber}} ]] The burial facility is a {{ill|Side-entrance chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} type {{ill|Stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}}, opening south-southwest. The rear part of the main chamber narrows into rear and front chambers, showcasing a multi-chamber structure similar to those in the Kinai region. The area of the stone chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}: * Total length of the stone chamber: 10.8 meters (preserved) * Rear chamber: Length 2.1 meters, width 1.3 meters, height 1.9 meters * Front chamber: Length 1.8 meters, width 1.6 meters, height 2 meters * Passageway The chamber is constructed using basalt slabs, possibly sourced from [[:en:Ainoshima_(Shingū)|Ainoshima]]. Both the passageway and main chamber consist of a single layer of large stones, with five stones on each side wall and one stone on the rear wall. The floor is paved with large flat stones in some parts, while other parts have stone and pebble paving beneath approximately 10 cm of soil{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. == See Also == * [[Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster]] ** [[Miyajidake Kofun]] == References == {{draft categories| }} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Burial sites in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Fukutsu City History]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Munakata clan|Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun]] [[Category:Tebika Namikirifudō Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tebika Namikirifudō Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tebika Namikirifudō Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tebika Namikirifudō Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tebika Namikirifudō Kofun]] [[en:Tebika Namikirifudō Kofun]] [[ja:手光波切不動古墳]]

But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Japanese Kofun | name = Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun | image = [[File:Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]] | caption = Mound and stone chamber entrance | alternate_name = | part_of = {{ill|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster|ja|津屋崎古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | location = {{ill|Fukutsu City|ja|福津市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Tebika 1615 (Ishibashi) | coordinates = 33 | coordinates = 46 | coordinates = 13.23 | coordinates = 130 | coordinates = 29 | coordinates = 52.58 | omitted = JP-40 | type = (Estimated) {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | area = Diameter 25m | Burial Facility = {{ill|Side-entrance chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}} type {{ill|Stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
(Wooden coffin inside) | burial goods = Iron products, horse gear, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Silla pottery | associated_tombs = | period = Early [[7th century]] | occupant = | imperial_tomb = | historic_site = Fukutsu City Designated Historic Site "Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun" | tangible_cultural_properties = | notes = | map = Japan Fukuoka | type = Round Kofun | map_dot_label = Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|33|46|30|N|130|29|40|E}} | zoom = 14 | float = right | width = 250| height = 200 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun and Miyajidake Kofun}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1 = {{Coord|33|46|13.23|N|130|29|52.58|E}} | map_dot_label1 = Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun | map_dot_label-pos1 = bottom | map_dot_label-color1 = black | mark1 = Round Kofun.png | mark-size1 = 20 | mark-coord2 = {{Coord|33|46|51.10|N|130|29|19.15|E}} | map_dot_label2 = Miyajidake Kofun | map_dot_label-pos2 = right | map_dot_label-color2 = black | mark2 = Round Kofun.png | mark-size2 = 20 }} The '''Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun''' (手光波切不動古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in Tebika, {{ill|Fukutsu City|ja|福津市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is one of the Kofuns that make up the {{ill|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster|ja|津屋崎古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and has been Historic Site1 as a historic site by Fukutsu City. == Overview == This Kofun is located on the terminal end of a hill (22–27 meters above sea level) derived from the foothills of Mount Miyaji, in northern Fukuoka Prefecture. It occupies the southernmost position among the Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster. The stone chamber has been open since ancient times, and the mound has been partially leveled. Detailed investigations of the stone chamber were conducted in [[1992]] (Heisei 4), while surveys of the mound were pertypeed during [[2010]]–[[2011]] (Heisei 22–23). Due to leveling at the mound's base, its exact type is unclear, but the mound is currently estimated to have a diameter of 20 meters and a height of 9 meters. Originally, it is reconstructed to have been approximately 25 meters in diameter{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. The burial facility is a {{ill|Side-entrance chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}} type {{ill|Stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, opening towards the south-southwest. The total length of the stone chamber is 10.8 meters, and large basalt stones were used as its construction material. Despite the chamber being open for centuries, a survey of the tomb path in front of the chamber revealed wooden coffin components (nails and clamps), as well as iron products, horse gear, and fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and Silla pottery{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. The construction of the mound is estimated to date to the early [[7th century]], with possible additional burials through the late 7th century{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. Previously considered to follow the {{ill|Miyajidake Kofun|ja|宮地嶽古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it is now viewed as preceding it, and is considered a successor to the {{ill|Zaiji Kentsuka Kofun|ja|在自剣塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the largest {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in the Munakata region{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. As a tomb adopting the side-entrance chamber structure typical of the Kinai region, it holds significance for understanding the political landscape of the Munakata region{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. In [[1992]] (Heisei 4), the mound was Historic Site1 as a historic site by the typeer Fukuma Town, now Fukutsu City. == Excavation History == * [[Tenpō]] era ([[1830]]–[[1844]]): Mentioned in the ''{{ill|Zoku Fudoki of Chikuzen Province|ja|筑前国続風土記拾遺|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'' (described as a faith site with the stone chamber already open, known as Fudo Cave){{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. * [[1992]]: Detailed measurements of the stone chamber{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. * October 13, 1992: Designated as a Fukuma Town Historic Site (now a Fukutsu City Historic Site){{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. * [[2010]]: Mound survey by the Fukutsu City Board of Education, reported in [[2013]]{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. * [[2011]]: Confirmation survey of the mound's size{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. == burial == [[File:Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Bird's-eye view of the stone chamber}} ]] [[File:Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the stone chamber}} ]] The burial facility is a {{ill|Side-entrance chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}} type {{ill|Stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, opening south-southwest. The rear part of the main chamber narrows into rear and front chambers, showcasing a multi-chamber structure similar to those in the Kinai region. The area of the stone chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}: * Total length of the stone chamber: 10.8 meters (preserved) * Rear chamber: Length 2.1 meters, width 1.3 meters, height 1.9 meters * Front chamber: Length 1.8 meters, width 1.6 meters, height 2 meters * Passageway The chamber is constructed using basalt slabs, possibly sourced from [[:en:Ainoshima_(Shingū)|Ainoshima]]. Both the passageway and main chamber consist of a single layer of large stones, with five stones on each side wall and one stone on the rear wall. The floor is paved with large flat stones in some parts, while other parts have stone and pebble paving beneath approximately 10 cm of soil{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. == See Also == * [[Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster]] ** [[Miyajidake Kofun]] == References == {{draft categories| }} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Burial sites in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Fukutsu City History]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Munakata clan|Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun]] [[Category:Tebika Namikirifudō Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tebika Namikirifudō Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tebika Namikirifudō Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tebika Namikirifudō Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tebika Namikirifudō Kofun]] [[en:Tebika Namikirifudō Kofun]] [[ja:手光波切不動古墳]]

revision.comment: 11 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:手光波切不動古墳 up to rev 102446439
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 手光波切不動古墳 |画像 = [[File:Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]] |画像キャプション = 墳丘・石室開口部 |別名 = |所属 = [[津屋崎古墳群]] |所在地 = [[福岡県]][[福津市]]手光1615（字石橋） |緯度度 = 33|緯度分 = 46|緯度秒 = 13.23 |経度度 = 130|経度分 = 29|経度秒 = 52.58 |ISO = JP-40 |形状 = （推定）[[円墳]] |規模 = 直径25m |埋葬施設 = [[横口式石槨]]系[[横穴式石室]]
（内部に木棺） |出土品 = 鉄製品・馬具・[[須恵器]]・新羅土器 |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[7世紀]]前半 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = 福津市指定史跡「手光波切不動古墳」 |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Fukuoka |アイコン = 円墳 |ラベル = 手光波切
不動古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} {{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|33|46|30|N|130|29|40|E}} | zoom = 14 | float = right | width = 250| height =200 | caption = {{center|手光波切不動古墳・宮地嶽古墳分布図}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|33|46|13.23|N|130|29|52.58|E}} | label1=手光波切不動古墳 | label-pos1=bottom | label-color1=black | mark1=円墳.png | mark-size1=20 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|33|46|51.10|N|130|29|19.15|E}} | label2=宮地嶽古墳 | label-pos2=right | label-color2=black | mark2=円墳.png | mark-size2=20 }} '''手光波切不動古墳'''（てびかなみきりふどうこふん）は、[[福岡県]][[福津市]]手光にある[[古墳]]。[[津屋崎古墳群]]を構成する古墳の1つ。福津市指定史跡に指定されている。 == 概要 == 福岡県北部、宮地岳山麓から派生する丘陵の先端部（標高22-27メートル）に築造された古墳である。[[津屋崎古墳群]]のうちでは南端に位置する。古くから石室が開口し、墳丘周囲は削平されているほか、[[1992年]]（[[平成]]4年）に石室実測調査が、[[2010年|2010]]-[[2011年]]度（平成22-23年度）に墳丘の測量・確認調査が実施されている。 墳丘裾の削平のため墳形は詳らかでないが、現在は直径20メートル・高さ9メートルを測り、元は直径25メートル程度の円形と推定復元される{{Sfn|井浦一|2014}}。埋葬施設は[[横口式石槨]]系の[[横穴式石室]]で、南南西方向に開口する。石室全長10.8メートルを測る大型石室で、石室の石材には[[玄武岩]]の巨石が使用される。古くから開口するため石室内の調査では副葬品を詳らかとしないが、石室前の墓道の調査では木棺部材（釘・鎹）のほか鉄製品・馬具・[[須恵器]]片・新羅土器片などが検出されている{{Sfn|井浦一|2014}}。 築造時期は、[[古墳時代]][[終末期古墳|終末期]]の[[7世紀]]前半頃と推定され、7世紀後半頃までの追葬が想定される{{Sfn|井浦一|2014}}。かつては[[宮地嶽古墳]]（福津市宮司元町）の後続古墳と位置づけられたが、近年では先行古墳の可能性が高いとされ、津屋崎古墳群では[[在自剣塚古墳]]（福津市在自、宗像地域最大の前方後円墳）の後続古墳に位置づけられる{{Sfn|津屋崎古墳群III|2013}}。畿内の横口式石槨同様の石室構造を採った首長墓であり、宗像地域の政治情勢を考察するうえで重要視される古墳になる{{Sfn|津屋崎古墳群III|2013}}。 古墳域は[[1992年]]（平成4年）に福間町指定史跡（現在は福津市指定史跡）に指定されている。 == 遺跡歴 == * [[天保]]年間（[[1830年|1830]]-[[1844年]]）編纂の『[[筑前国続風土記|筑前国続風土記拾遺]]』に記載（当時には石室開口、不動窟として信仰）{{Sfn|井浦一|2014}}。 * [[1992年]]（[[平成]]4年）、石室実測調査{{Sfn|井浦一|2014}}。 * 1992年（平成4年）10月13日、福間町指定史跡に指定（現在は福津市指定史跡）{{Sfn|井浦一|2014}}。 * [[2010年]]度（平成22年度）、墳丘測量調査（福津市教育委員会、[[2013年]]に報告書刊行）{{Sfn|津屋崎古墳群III|2013}}。 * [[2011年]]度（平成23年度）、古墳規模の確認調査（福津市教育委員会、2013年に報告書刊行）{{Sfn|津屋崎古墳群III|2013}}。 == 埋葬施設 == [[File:Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|石室俯瞰図}}]] [[File:Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|石室展開図}}]] 埋葬施設としては[[横口式石槨]]系[[横穴式石室]]が構築されており、南南西方向に開口する。羨道・玄室のうち、玄室の奥側は一回り狭まって奥室・前室に区分され、畿内の横口式石槨同様の複室構造を呈する。石室の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|津屋崎古墳群III|2013}}。 * 石室全長：現存10.8メートル * 奥室：長さ2.1メートル、幅1.3メートル、高さ1.9メートル * 前室：長さ1.8メートル、幅1.6メートル、高さ2メートル * 羨道 石室の石材は板状切石の[[玄武岩]]で、[[相島 (福岡県)|相島]]産の可能性があるとして注目される{{Sfn|津屋崎古墳群III|2013}}。羨道・玄室はいずれも巨石の1段積みで、側壁は各5石、奥壁は1石である。床面は、奥室・前室および羨道の一部に板状一枚石の敷石を置き、羨道の敷石の無い部分では深さ約10センチメートルの土砂の下で敷石・玉砂利敷が検出されている{{Sfn|津屋崎古墳群III|2013}}。天井石は奥室・前室で2石、羨道で4石{{Sfn|津屋崎古墳群III|2013}}。なお、石材表面には白色部分が認められるが、漆喰ではなくカビと見られる{{Sfn|津屋崎古墳群III|2013}}。 奥室・前室の間は、高さ0.5メートルの板石を立てて仕切り、仕切り石の中央には浅い凹みが認められる{{Sfn|津屋崎古墳群III|2013}}。石室形態は[[宮地嶽古墳]]と共通するが、そのほかには一般に九州地方では見られない一方、仕切り石の浅い凹みは九州地方に特有の構造になる{{Sfn|井浦一|2014}}。 古くから開口するため石室内の調査では副葬品を詳らかとしないが、石室前の墓道の調査では馬具等の副葬品が検出されている。 Miyajidake Kofun, 3D-1.jpg|参考：[[宮地嶽古墳]]石室俯瞰図 Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun, zenshitsu-1.jpg|奥室・前室（奥壁方向） Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun, zenshitsu-2.jpg|前室（開口部方向） Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|羨道（開口部方向） Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|羨道（奥室方向） Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun, entrance.jpg|開口部 == 出土品 == 2011年度（平成23年度）調査で石室前の墓道から検出された副葬品は次の通り{{Sfn|井浦一|2014}}。 * 木棺部材 ** 鉄釘 ** 鎹 * ガラス玉 * 鉄製品 ** 鉄鏃片 ** 鉄刀片 * 馬具 ** 輪鐙 ** 轡 ** 辻金具 ** 金銅製鞍金具 * [[須恵器]]片 - 高坏、器台。器台は円孔を複数有し、[[沖ノ島|沖ノ島祭祀遺跡]]と類似する土器として注目される。 * 新羅土器片 - 細頸壺。 == 文化財 == === 福津市指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** 手光波切不動古墳 - 1992年（平成4年）10月13日指定。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 史跡説明板（福津市教育委員会設置） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=杉原敏之|year=2002|chapter=手光波切不動古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=978-4490105995|ref={{Harvid|手光波切不動古墳（続古墳）|2002}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2013|chapter=|title=津屋崎古墳群III -手光波切不動古墳の調査 手光湯ノ浦古墳群の調査-|series=福津市文化財調査報告書第7集|publisher=福津市教育委員会|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|津屋崎古墳群III|2013}}}} * {{Cite journal|和書|author=井浦一|title={{PDFlink|[http://www.city-fukutsu.ed.jp/kyouiku/index.cfm/9,68,c,html/68/bunnkafukutu-9.pdf 福津の歴史 -手光波切不動古墳の調査成果-]}}|date=2014|journal=文化福津|publisher=福津市文化協会|volume=|number=9|pages=6-10|url=|naid=|doi=|ref={{Harvid|井浦一|2014}}}} - リンクは福津市教育委員会。 == 関連項目 == {{Commonscat|Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun}} * [[津屋崎古墳群]] ** [[宮地嶽古墳]] {{DEFAULTSORT:てひかなみきりふとうこふん}} [[Category:福岡県の古墳]] [[Category:福津市の歴史]] [[Category:円墳]] [[Category:福岡県内の市町村指定史跡]] [[Category:宗像氏|墓てひかなみきりふとうこふん]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[手光波切不動古墳]] to [[Tebika Namikirifudō Kofun]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 手光波切不動古墳 |画像 = [[File:Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]] |画像キャプション = 墳丘・石室開口部 |別名 = |所属 = [[津屋崎古墳群]] |所在地 = [[福岡県]][[福津市]]手光1615（字石橋） |緯度度 = 33|緯度分 = 46|緯度秒 = 13.23 |経度度 = 130|経度分 = 29|経度秒 = 52.58 |ISO = JP-40 |形状 = （推定）[[円墳]] |規模 = 直径25m |埋葬施設 = [[横口式石槨]]系[[横穴式石室]]
（内部に木棺） |出土品 = 鉄製品・馬具・[[須恵器]]・新羅土器 |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[7世紀]]前半 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = 福津市指定史跡「手光波切不動古墳」 |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Fukuoka |アイコン = 円墳 |ラベル = 手光波切
不動古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} {{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|33|46|30|N|130|29|40|E}} | zoom = 14 | float = right | width = 250| height =200 | caption = {{center|手光波切不動古墳・宮地嶽古墳分布図}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|33|46|13.23|N|130|29|52.58|E}} | label1=手光波切不動古墳 | label-pos1=bottom | label-color1=black | mark1=円墳.png | mark-size1=20 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|33|46|51.10|N|130|29|19.15|E}} | label2=宮地嶽古墳 | label-pos2=right | label-color2=black | mark2=円墳.png | mark-size2=20 }} '''手光波切不動古墳'''（てびかなみきりふどうこふん）は、[[福岡県]][[福津市]]手光にある[[古墳]]。[[津屋崎古墳群]]を構成する古墳の1つ。福津市指定史跡に指定されている。 == 概要 == 福岡県北部、宮地岳山麓から派生する丘陵の先端部（標高22-27メートル）に築造された古墳である。[[津屋崎古墳群]]のうちでは南端に位置する。古くから石室が開口し、墳丘周囲は削平されているほか、[[1992年]]（[[平成]]4年）に石室実測調査が、[[2010年|2010]]-[[2011年]]度（平成22-23年度）に墳丘の測量・確認調査が実施されている。 墳丘裾の削平のため墳形は詳らかでないが、現在は直径20メートル・高さ9メートルを測り、元は直径25メートル程度の円形と推定復元される{{Sfn|井浦一|2014}}。埋葬施設は[[横口式石槨]]系の[[横穴式石室]]で、南南西方向に開口する。石室全長10.8メートルを測る大型石室で、石室の石材には[[玄武岩]]の巨石が使用される。古くから開口するため石室内の調査では副葬品を詳らかとしないが、石室前の墓道の調査では木棺部材（釘・鎹）のほか鉄製品・馬具・[[須恵器]]片・新羅土器片などが検出されている{{Sfn|井浦一|2014}}。 築造時期は、[[古墳時代]][[終末期古墳|終末期]]の[[7世紀]]前半頃と推定され、7世紀後半頃までの追葬が想定される{{Sfn|井浦一|2014}}。かつては[[宮地嶽古墳]]（福津市宮司元町）の後続古墳と位置づけられたが、近年では先行古墳の可能性が高いとされ、津屋崎古墳群では[[在自剣塚古墳]]（福津市在自、宗像地域最大の前方後円墳）の後続古墳に位置づけられる{{Sfn|津屋崎古墳群III|2013}}。畿内の横口式石槨同様の石室構造を採った首長墓であり、宗像地域の政治情勢を考察するうえで重要視される古墳になる{{Sfn|津屋崎古墳群III|2013}}。 古墳域は[[1992年]]（平成4年）に福間町指定史跡（現在は福津市指定史跡）に指定されている。 == 遺跡歴 == * [[天保]]年間（[[1830年|1830]]-[[1844年]]）編纂の『[[筑前国続風土記|筑前国続風土記拾遺]]』に記載（当時には石室開口、不動窟として信仰）{{Sfn|井浦一|2014}}。 * [[1992年]]（[[平成]]4年）、石室実測調査{{Sfn|井浦一|2014}}。 * 1992年（平成4年）10月13日、福間町指定史跡に指定（現在は福津市指定史跡）{{Sfn|井浦一|2014}}。 * [[2010年]]度（平成22年度）、墳丘測量調査（福津市教育委員会、[[2013年]]に報告書刊行）{{Sfn|津屋崎古墳群III|2013}}。 * [[2011年]]度（平成23年度）、古墳規模の確認調査（福津市教育委員会、2013年に報告書刊行）{{Sfn|津屋崎古墳群III|2013}}。 == 埋葬施設 == [[File:Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|石室俯瞰図}}]] [[File:Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|石室展開図}}]] 埋葬施設としては[[横口式石槨]]系[[横穴式石室]]が構築されており、南南西方向に開口する。羨道・玄室のうち、玄室の奥側は一回り狭まって奥室・前室に区分され、畿内の横口式石槨同様の複室構造を呈する。石室の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|津屋崎古墳群III|2013}}。 * 石室全長：現存10.8メートル * 奥室：長さ2.1メートル、幅1.3メートル、高さ1.9メートル * 前室：長さ1.8メートル、幅1.6メートル、高さ2メートル * 羨道 石室の石材は板状切石の[[玄武岩]]で、[[相島 (福岡県)|相島]]産の可能性があるとして注目される{{Sfn|津屋崎古墳群III|2013}}。羨道・玄室はいずれも巨石の1段積みで、側壁は各5石、奥壁は1石である。床面は、奥室・前室および羨道の一部に板状一枚石の敷石を置き、羨道の敷石の無い部分では深さ約10センチメートルの土砂の下で敷石・玉砂利敷が検出されている{{Sfn|津屋崎古墳群III|2013}}。天井石は奥室・前室で2石、羨道で4石{{Sfn|津屋崎古墳群III|2013}}。なお、石材表面には白色部分が認められるが、漆喰ではなくカビと見られる{{Sfn|津屋崎古墳群III|2013}}。 奥室・前室の間は、高さ0.5メートルの板石を立てて仕切り、仕切り石の中央には浅い凹みが認められる{{Sfn|津屋崎古墳群III|2013}}。石室形態は[[宮地嶽古墳]]と共通するが、そのほかには一般に九州地方では見られない一方、仕切り石の浅い凹みは九州地方に特有の構造になる{{Sfn|井浦一|2014}}。 古くから開口するため石室内の調査では副葬品を詳らかとしないが、石室前の墓道の調査では馬具等の副葬品が検出されている。 Miyajidake Kofun, 3D-1.jpg|参考：[[宮地嶽古墳]]石室俯瞰図 Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun, zenshitsu-1.jpg|奥室・前室（奥壁方向） Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun, zenshitsu-2.jpg|前室（開口部方向） Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|羨道（開口部方向） Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|羨道（奥室方向） Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun, entrance.jpg|開口部 == 出土品 == 2011年度（平成23年度）調査で石室前の墓道から検出された副葬品は次の通り{{Sfn|井浦一|2014}}。 * 木棺部材 ** 鉄釘 ** 鎹 * ガラス玉 * 鉄製品 ** 鉄鏃片 ** 鉄刀片 * 馬具 ** 輪鐙 ** 轡 ** 辻金具 ** 金銅製鞍金具 * [[須恵器]]片 - 高坏、器台。器台は円孔を複数有し、[[沖ノ島|沖ノ島祭祀遺跡]]と類似する土器として注目される。 * 新羅土器片 - 細頸壺。 == 文化財 == === 福津市指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** 手光波切不動古墳 - 1992年（平成4年）10月13日指定。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 史跡説明板（福津市教育委員会設置） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=杉原敏之|year=2002|chapter=手光波切不動古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=978-4490105995|ref={{Harvid|手光波切不動古墳（続古墳）|2002}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2013|chapter=|title=津屋崎古墳群III -手光波切不動古墳の調査 手光湯ノ浦古墳群の調査-|series=福津市文化財調査報告書第7集|publisher=福津市教育委員会|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|津屋崎古墳群III|2013}}}} * {{Cite journal|和書|author=井浦一|title={{PDFlink|[http://www.city-fukutsu.ed.jp/kyouiku/index.cfm/9,68,c,html/68/bunnkafukutu-9.pdf 福津の歴史 -手光波切不動古墳の調査成果-]}}|date=2014|journal=文化福津|publisher=福津市文化協会|volume=|number=9|pages=6-10|url=|naid=|doi=|ref={{Harvid|井浦一|2014}}}} - リンクは福津市教育委員会。 == 関連項目 == {{Commonscat|Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun}} * [[津屋崎古墳群]] ** [[宮地嶽古墳]] {{DEFAULTSORT:てひかなみきりふとうこふん}} [[Category:福岡県の古墳]] [[Category:福津市の歴史]] [[Category:円墳]] [[Category:福岡県内の市町村指定史跡]] [[Category:宗像氏|墓てひかなみきりふとうこふん]]

{{afc comment|1=See [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Japanese Kofun | name = Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun | image = [[File:Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]] | caption = Mound and stone chamber entrance | alternate_name = | part_of = {{ill|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster|ja|津屋崎古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | location = {{ill|Fukutsu City|ja|福津市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Tebika 1615 (Ishibashi) | coordinates = 33 | coordinates = 46 | coordinates = 13.23 | coordinates = 130 | coordinates = 29 | coordinates = 52.58 | omitted = JP-40 | type = (Estimated) {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | area = Diameter 25m | Burial Facility = {{ill|Side-entrance chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}} type {{ill|Stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
(Wooden coffin inside) | burial goods = Iron products, horse gear, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Silla pottery | associated_tombs = | period = Early [[7th century]] | occupant = | imperial_tomb = | historic_site = Fukutsu City Designated Historic Site "Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun" | tangible_cultural_properties = | notes = | map = Japan Fukuoka | type = Round Kofun | map_dot_label = Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|33|46|30|N|130|29|40|E}} | zoom = 14 | float = right | width = 250| height = 200 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun and Miyajidake Kofun}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1 = {{Coord|33|46|13.23|N|130|29|52.58|E}} | map_dot_label1 = Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun | map_dot_label-pos1 = bottom | map_dot_label-color1 = black | mark1 = Round Kofun.png | mark-size1 = 20 | mark-coord2 = {{Coord|33|46|51.10|N|130|29|19.15|E}} | map_dot_label2 = Miyajidake Kofun | map_dot_label-pos2 = right | map_dot_label-color2 = black | mark2 = Round Kofun.png | mark-size2 = 20 }} The '''Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun''' (手光波切不動古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in Tebika, {{ill|Fukutsu City|ja|福津市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is one of the Kofuns that make up the {{ill|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster|ja|津屋崎古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and has been Historic Site1 as a historic site by Fukutsu City. == Overview == This Kofun is located on the terminal end of a hill (22–27 meters above sea level) derived from the foothills of Mount Miyaji, in northern Fukuoka Prefecture. It occupies the southernmost position among the Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster. The stone chamber has been open since ancient times, and the mound has been partially leveled. Detailed investigations of the stone chamber were conducted in [[1992]] (Heisei 4), while surveys of the mound were pertypeed during [[2010]]–[[2011]] (Heisei 22–23). Due to leveling at the mound's base, its exact type is unclear, but the mound is currently estimated to have a diameter of 20 meters and a height of 9 meters. Originally, it is reconstructed to have been approximately 25 meters in diameter{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. The burial facility is a {{ill|Side-entrance chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}} type {{ill|Stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, opening towards the south-southwest. The total length of the stone chamber is 10.8 meters, and large basalt stones were used as its construction material. Despite the chamber being open for centuries, a survey of the tomb path in front of the chamber revealed wooden coffin components (nails and clamps), as well as iron products, horse gear, and fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and Silla pottery{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. The construction of the mound is estimated to date to the early [[7th century]], with possible additional burials through the late 7th century{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. Previously considered to follow the {{ill|Miyajidake Kofun|ja|宮地嶽古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it is now viewed as preceding it, and is considered a successor to the {{ill|Zaiji Kentsuka Kofun|ja|在自剣塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the largest {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in the Munakata region{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. As a tomb adopting the side-entrance chamber structure typical of the Kinai region, it holds significance for understanding the political landscape of the Munakata region{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. In [[1992]] (Heisei 4), the mound was Historic Site1 as a historic site by the typeer Fukuma Town, now Fukutsu City. == Excavation History == * [[Tenpō]] era ([[1830]]–[[1844]]): Mentioned in the ''{{ill|Zoku Fudoki of Chikuzen Province|ja|筑前国続風土記拾遺|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'' (described as a faith site with the stone chamber already open, known as Fudo Cave){{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. * [[1992]]: Detailed measurements of the stone chamber{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. * October 13, 1992: Designated as a Fukuma Town Historic Site (now a Fukutsu City Historic Site){{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. * [[2010]]: Mound survey by the Fukutsu City Board of Education, reported in [[2013]]{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. * [[2011]]: Confirmation survey of the mound's size{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. == burial == [[File:Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Bird's-eye view of the stone chamber}} ]] [[File:Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the stone chamber}} ]] The burial facility is a {{ill|Side-entrance chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}} type {{ill|Stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, opening south-southwest. The rear part of the main chamber narrows into rear and front chambers, showcasing a multi-chamber structure similar to those in the Kinai region. The area of the stone chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}: * Total length of the stone chamber: 10.8 meters (preserved) * Rear chamber: Length 2.1 meters, width 1.3 meters, height 1.9 meters * Front chamber: Length 1.8 meters, width 1.6 meters, height 2 meters * Passageway The chamber is constructed using basalt slabs, possibly sourced from [[:en:Ainoshima_(Shingū)|Ainoshima]]. Both the passageway and main chamber consist of a single layer of large stones, with five stones on each side wall and one stone on the rear wall. The floor is paved with large flat stones in some parts, while other parts have stone and pebble paving beneath approximately 10 cm of soil{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. == See Also == * [[Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster]] ** [[Miyajidake Kofun]] == References == {{draft categories| }} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Burial sites in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Fukutsu City History]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Munakata clan|Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun]] [[Category:Tebika Namikirifudō Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tebika Namikirifudō Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tebika Namikirifudō Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tebika Namikirifudō Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tebika Namikirifudō Kofun]] [[en:Tebika Namikirifudō Kofun]] [[ja:手光波切不動古墳]] {{translated page|ja|手光波切不動古墳|version=102446439|comment=Imported full ja history}}

{{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Japanese Kofun | name = Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun | image = [[File:Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]] | caption = Mound and stone chamber entrance | alternate_name = | part_of = {{ill|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster|ja|津屋崎古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | location = {{ill|Fukutsu City|ja|福津市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Tebika 1615 (Ishibashi) | coordinates = 33 | coordinates = 46 | coordinates = 13.23 | coordinates = 130 | coordinates = 29 | coordinates = 52.58 | omitted = JP-40 | type = (Estimated) {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | area = Diameter 25m | Burial Facility = {{ill|Side-entrance chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}} type {{ill|Stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
(Wooden coffin inside) | burial goods = Iron products, horse gear, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Silla pottery | associated_tombs = | period = Early [[7th century]] | occupant = | imperial_tomb = | historic_site = Fukutsu City Designated Historic Site "Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun" | tangible_cultural_properties = | notes = | map = Japan Fukuoka | type = Round Kofun | map_dot_label = Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|33|46|30|N|130|29|40|E}} | zoom = 14 | float = right | width = 250| height = 200 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun and Miyajidake Kofun}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1 = {{Coord|33|46|13.23|N|130|29|52.58|E}} | map_dot_label1 = Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun | map_dot_label-pos1 = bottom | map_dot_label-color1 = black | mark1 = Round Kofun.png | mark-size1 = 20 | mark-coord2 = {{Coord|33|46|51.10|N|130|29|19.15|E}} | map_dot_label2 = Miyajidake Kofun | map_dot_label-pos2 = right | map_dot_label-color2 = black | mark2 = Round Kofun.png | mark-size2 = 20 }} The '''Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun''' (手光波切不動古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in Tebika, {{ill|Fukutsu City|ja|福津市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is one of the Kofuns that make up the {{ill|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster|ja|津屋崎古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and has been Historic Site1 as a historic site by Fukutsu City. == Overview == This Kofun is located on the terminal end of a hill (22–27 meters above sea level) derived from the foothills of Mount Miyaji, in northern Fukuoka Prefecture. It occupies the southernmost position among the Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster. The stone chamber has been open since ancient times, and the mound has been partially leveled. Detailed investigations of the stone chamber were conducted in [[1992]] (Heisei 4), while surveys of the mound were pertypeed during [[2010]]–[[2011]] (Heisei 22–23). Due to leveling at the mound's base, its exact type is unclear, but the mound is currently estimated to have a diameter of 20 meters and a height of 9 meters. Originally, it is reconstructed to have been approximately 25 meters in diameter{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. The burial facility is a {{ill|Side-entrance chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}} type {{ill|Stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, opening towards the south-southwest. The total length of the stone chamber is 10.8 meters, and large basalt stones were used as its construction material. Despite the chamber being open for centuries, a survey of the tomb path in front of the chamber revealed wooden coffin components (nails and clamps), as well as iron products, horse gear, and fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and Silla pottery{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. The construction of the mound is estimated to date to the early [[7th century]], with possible additional burials through the late 7th century{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. Previously considered to follow the {{ill|Miyajidake Kofun|ja|宮地嶽古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it is now viewed as preceding it, and is considered a successor to the {{ill|Zaiji Kentsuka Kofun|ja|在自剣塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the largest {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in the Munakata region{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. As a tomb adopting the side-entrance chamber structure typical of the Kinai region, it holds significance for understanding the political landscape of the Munakata region{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. In [[1992]] (Heisei 4), the mound was Historic Site1 as a historic site by the typeer Fukuma Town, now Fukutsu City. == Excavation History == * [[Tenpō]] era ([[1830]]–[[1844]]): Mentioned in the ''{{ill|Zoku Fudoki of Chikuzen Province|ja|筑前国続風土記拾遺|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'' (described as a faith site with the stone chamber already open, known as Fudo Cave){{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. * [[1992]]: Detailed measurements of the stone chamber{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. * October 13, 1992: Designated as a Fukuma Town Historic Site (now a Fukutsu City Historic Site){{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. * [[2010]]: Mound survey by the Fukutsu City Board of Education, reported in [[2013]]{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. * [[2011]]: Confirmation survey of the mound's size{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. == burial == [[File:Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Bird's-eye view of the stone chamber}} ]] [[File:Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the stone chamber}} ]] The burial facility is a {{ill|Side-entrance chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}} type {{ill|Stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, opening south-southwest. The rear part of the main chamber narrows into rear and front chambers, showcasing a multi-chamber structure similar to those in the Kinai region. The area of the stone chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}: * Total length of the stone chamber: 10.8 meters (preserved) * Rear chamber: Length 2.1 meters, width 1.3 meters, height 1.9 meters * Front chamber: Length 1.8 meters, width 1.6 meters, height 2 meters * Passageway The chamber is constructed using basalt slabs, possibly sourced from [[:en:Ainoshima_(Shingū)|Ainoshima]]. Both the passageway and main chamber consist of a single layer of large stones, with five stones on each side wall and one stone on the rear wall. The floor is paved with large flat stones in some parts, while other parts have stone and pebble paving beneath approximately 10 cm of soil{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. == See Also == * [[Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster]] ** [[Miyajidake Kofun]] == References == {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|手光波切不動古墳|version=102446439|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Burial sites in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Fukutsu City History]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Munakata clan|Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun]] [[Category:Tebika Namikirifudō Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tebika Namikirifudō Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tebika Namikirifudō Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tebika Namikirifudō Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tebika Namikirifudō Kofun]] [[en:Tebika Namikirifudō Kofun]] [[ja:手光波切不動古墳]]

This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Japanese Kofun | name = Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun | image = [[File:Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]] | caption = Mound and stone chamber entrance | alternate_name = | part_of = {{ill|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster|ja|津屋崎古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster|qq=}} | location = {{ill|Fukutsu City|ja|福津市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukutsu City|qq=}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka Prefecture|qq=}}, Tebika 1615 (Ishibashi) | coordinates = 33 | coordinates = 46 | coordinates = 13.23 | coordinates = 130 | coordinates = 29 | coordinates = 52.58 | omitted = JP-40 | type = (Estimated) {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}} | area = Diameter 25m | Burial Facility = {{ill|Side-entrance chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} type {{ill|Stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}
(Wooden coffin inside) | burial goods = Iron products, horse gear, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|qq=}}, Silla pottery | associated_tombs = | period = Early [[7th century]] | occupant = | imperial_tomb = | historic_site = Fukutsu City Designated Historic Site "Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun" | tangible_cultural_properties = | notes = | map = Japan Fukuoka | type = Round Kofun | map_dot_label = Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|33|46|30|N|130|29|40|E}} | zoom = 14 | float = right | width = 250| height = 200 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun and Miyajidake Kofun}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1 = {{Coord|33|46|13.23|N|130|29|52.58|E}} | map_dot_label1 = Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun | map_dot_label-pos1 = bottom | map_dot_label-color1 = black | mark1 = Round Kofun.png | mark-size1 = 20 | mark-coord2 = {{Coord|33|46|51.10|N|130|29|19.15|E}} | map_dot_label2 = Miyajidake Kofun | map_dot_label-pos2 = right | map_dot_label-color2 = black | mark2 = Round Kofun.png | mark-size2 = 20 }} The '''Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun''' (手光波切不動古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun|qq=}} located in Tebika, {{ill|Fukutsu City|ja|福津市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukutsu City|qq=}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka Prefecture|qq=}}. It is one of the Kofuns that make up the {{ill|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster|ja|津屋崎古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster|qq=}} and has been Historic Site1 as a historic site by Fukutsu City. == Overview == This Kofun is located on the terminal end of a hill (22–27 meters above sea level) derived from the foothills of Mount Miyaji, in northern Fukuoka Prefecture. It occupies the southernmost position among the Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster. The stone chamber has been open since ancient times, and the mound has been partially leveled. Detailed investigations of the stone chamber were conducted in [[1992]] (Heisei 4), while surveys of the mound were pertypeed during [[2010]]–[[2011]] (Heisei 22–23). Due to leveling at the mound's base, its exact type is unclear, but the mound is currently estimated to have a diameter of 20 meters and a height of 9 meters. Originally, it is reconstructed to have been approximately 25 meters in diameter{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. The burial facility is a {{ill|Side-entrance chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} type {{ill|Stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening towards the south-southwest. The total length of the stone chamber is 10.8 meters, and large basalt stones were used as its construction material. Despite the chamber being open for centuries, a survey of the tomb path in front of the chamber revealed wooden coffin components (nails and clamps), as well as iron products, horse gear, and fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|qq=}} and Silla pottery{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. The construction of the mound is estimated to date to the early [[7th century]], with possible additional burials through the late 7th century{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. Previously considered to follow the {{ill|Miyajidake Kofun|ja|宮地嶽古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyajidake Kofun|qq=}}, it is now viewed as preceding it, and is considered a successor to the {{ill|Zaiji Kentsuka Kofun|ja|在自剣塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zaiji Kentsuka Kofun|qq=}}, the largest {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keyhole-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} in the Munakata region{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. As a tomb adopting the side-entrance chamber structure typical of the Kinai region, it holds significance for understanding the political landscape of the Munakata region{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. In [[1992]] (Heisei 4), the mound was Historic Site1 as a historic site by the typeer Fukuma Town, now Fukutsu City. == Excavation History == * [[Tenpō]] era ([[1830]]–[[1844]]): Mentioned in the ''{{ill|Zoku Fudoki of Chikuzen Province|ja|筑前国続風土記拾遺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Fudoki of Chikuzen Province|qq=}}'' (described as a faith site with the stone chamber already open, known as Fudo Cave){{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. * [[1992]]: Detailed measurements of the stone chamber{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. * October 13, 1992: Designated as a Fukuma Town Historic Site (now a Fukutsu City Historic Site){{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. * [[2010]]: Mound survey by the Fukutsu City Board of Education, reported in [[2013]]{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. * [[2011]]: Confirmation survey of the mound's size{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. == burial == [[File:Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Bird's-eye view of the stone chamber}} ]] [[File:Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the stone chamber}} ]] The burial facility is a {{ill|Side-entrance chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} type {{ill|Stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening south-southwest. The rear part of the main chamber narrows into rear and front chambers, showcasing a multi-chamber structure similar to those in the Kinai region. The area of the stone chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}: * Total length of the stone chamber: 10.8 meters (preserved) * Rear chamber: Length 2.1 meters, width 1.3 meters, height 1.9 meters * Front chamber: Length 1.8 meters, width 1.6 meters, height 2 meters * Passageway The chamber is constructed using basalt slabs, possibly sourced from [[:en:Ainoshima_(Shingū)|Ainoshima]]. Both the passageway and main chamber consist of a single layer of large stones, with five stones on each side wall and one stone on the rear wall. The floor is paved with large flat stones in some parts, while other parts have stone and pebble paving beneath approximately 10 cm of soil{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. == See Also == * [[Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster]] ** [[Miyajidake Kofun]] == References == {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|手光波切不動古墳|version=102446439|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Burial sites in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Fukutsu City History]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Munakata clan|Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun]] [[Category:Tebika Namikirifudō Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Tebika Namikirifudō Kofun]][[sn:Tebika Namikirifudō Kofun]][[sn:Tebika Namikirifudō Kofun]]}} [[sn:Tebika Namikirifudō Kofun]] [[en:Tebika Namikirifudō Kofun]] [[ja:手光波切不動古墳]]

The area of the stone chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}: * Total length of the stone chamber: 10.8 meters (preserved) * Rear chamber: Length 2.1 meters, width 1.3 meters, height 1.9 meters * Front chamber: Length 1.8 meters, width 1.6 meters, height 2 meters * Passageway The chamber is constructed using basalt slabs, possibly sourced from [[:en:Ainoshima_(Shingū)|Ainoshima]]. Both the passageway and main chamber consist of a single layer of large stones, with five stones on each side wall and one stone on the rear wall. The floor is paved with large flat stones in some parts, while other parts have stone and pebble paving beneath approximately 10 cm of soil{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. == See Also == * [[Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster]] ** [[Miyajidake Kofun]] == References == {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|手光波切不動古墳|version=102446439|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Burial sites in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Fukutsu City History]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Munakata clan|Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun]] [[Category:Tebika Namikirifudō Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Tebika Namikirifudō Kofun]][[sn:Tebika Namikirifudō Kofun]][[sn:Tebika Namikirifudō Kofun]]}} [[sn:Tebika Namikirifudō Kofun]] [[en:Tebika Namikirifudō Kofun]] [[ja:手光波切不動古墳]]

So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Japanese Kofun | name = Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun | image = [[File:Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]] | caption = Mound and stone chamber entrance | alternate_name = | part_of = {{ill|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster|ja|津屋崎古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster|qq=}} | location = {{ill|Fukutsu City|ja|福津市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukutsu City|qq=}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka Prefecture|qq=}}, Tebika 1615 (Ishibashi) | coordinates = 33 | coordinates = 46 | coordinates = 13.23 | coordinates = 130 | coordinates = 29 | coordinates = 52.58 | omitted = JP-40 | type = (Estimated) {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}} | area = Diameter 25m | Burial Facility = {{ill|Side-entrance chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} type {{ill|Stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}
(Wooden coffin inside) | burial goods = Iron products, horse gear, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|qq=}}, Silla pottery | associated_tombs = | period = Early [[7th century]] | occupant = | imperial_tomb = | historic_site = Fukutsu City Designated Historic Site "Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun" | tangible_cultural_properties = | notes = | map = Japan Fukuoka | type = Round Kofun | map_dot_label = Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|33|46|30|N|130|29|40|E}} | zoom = 14 | float = right | width = 250| height = 200 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun and Miyajidake Kofun}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1 = {{Coord|33|46|13.23|N|130|29|52.58|E}} | map_dot_label1 = Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun | map_dot_label-pos1 = bottom | map_dot_label-color1 = black | mark1 = Round Kofun.png | mark-size1 = 20 | mark-coord2 = {{Coord|33|46|51.10|N|130|29|19.15|E}} | map_dot_label2 = Miyajidake Kofun | map_dot_label-pos2 = right | map_dot_label-color2 = black | mark2 = Round Kofun.png | mark-size2 = 20 }} The '''Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun''' (手光波切不動古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun|qq=}} located in Tebika, {{ill|Fukutsu City|ja|福津市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukutsu City|qq=}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka Prefecture|qq=}}. It is one of the Kofuns that make up the {{ill|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster|ja|津屋崎古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster|qq=}} and has been Historic Site1 as a historic site by Fukutsu City. == Overview == This Kofun is located on the terminal end of a hill (22–27 meters above sea level) derived from the foothills of Mount Miyaji, in northern Fukuoka Prefecture. It occupies the southernmost position among the Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster. The stone chamber has been open since ancient times, and the mound has been partially leveled. Detailed investigations of the stone chamber were conducted in [[1992]] (Heisei 4), while surveys of the mound were pertypeed during [[2010]]–[[2011]] (Heisei 22–23). Due to leveling at the mound's base, its exact type is unclear, but the mound is currently estimated to have a diameter of 20 meters and a height of 9 meters. Originally, it is reconstructed to have been approximately 25 meters in diameter{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. The burial facility is a {{ill|Side-entrance chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} type {{ill|Stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening towards the south-southwest. The total length of the stone chamber is 10.8 meters, and large basalt stones were used as its construction material. Despite the chamber being open for centuries, a survey of the tomb path in front of the chamber revealed wooden coffin components (nails and clamps), as well as iron products, horse gear, and fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|qq=}} and Silla pottery{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. The construction of the mound is estimated to date to the early [[7th century]], with possible additional burials through the late 7th century{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. Previously considered to follow the {{ill|Miyajidake Kofun|ja|宮地嶽古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyajidake Kofun|qq=}}, it is now viewed as preceding it, and is considered a successor to the {{ill|Zaiji Kentsuka Kofun|ja|在自剣塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zaiji Kentsuka Kofun|qq=}}, the largest {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keyhole-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} in the Munakata region{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. As a tomb adopting the side-entrance chamber structure typical of the Kinai region, it holds significance for understanding the political landscape of the Munakata region{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. In [[1992]] (Heisei 4), the mound was Historic Site1 as a historic site by the typeer Fukuma Town, now Fukutsu City. == Excavation History == * [[Tenpō]] era ([[1830]]–[[1844]]): Mentioned in the ''{{ill|Zoku Fudoki of Chikuzen Province|ja|筑前国続風土記拾遺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Fudoki of Chikuzen Province|qq=}}'' (described as a faith site with the stone chamber already open, known as Fudo Cave){{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. * [[1992]]: Detailed measurements of the stone chamber{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. * October 13, 1992: Designated as a Fukuma Town Historic Site (now a Fukutsu City Historic Site){{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. * [[2010]]: Mound survey by the Fukutsu City Board of Education, reported in [[2013]]{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. * [[2011]]: Confirmation survey of the mound's size{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. == burial == [[File:Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Bird's-eye view of the stone chamber}} ]] [[File:Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the stone chamber}} ]] The burial facility is a {{ill|Side-entrance chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} type {{ill|Stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening south-southwest. The rear part of the main chamber narrows into rear and front chambers, showcasing a multi-chamber structure similar to those in the Kinai region. The area of the stone chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}: * Total length of the stone chamber: 10.8 meters (preserved) * Rear chamber: Length 2.1 meters, width 1.3 meters, height 1.9 meters * Front chamber: Length 1.8 meters, width 1.6 meters, height 2 meters * Passageway The chamber is constructed using basalt slabs, possibly sourced from [[:en:Ainoshima_(Shingū)|Ainoshima]]. Both the passageway and main chamber consist of a single layer of large stones, with five stones on each side wall and one stone on the rear wall. The floor is paved with large flat stones in some parts, while other parts have stone and pebble paving beneath approximately 10 cm of soil{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. == See Also == * [[Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster]] ** [[Miyajidake Kofun]] == References == {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|手光波切不動古墳|version=102446439|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Burial sites in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Fukutsu City History]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Munakata clan|Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Tebika Namikirifudō Kofun]][[sn:Tebika Namikirifudō Kofun]][[sn:Tebika Namikirifudō Kofun]]}} [[sn:Tebika Namikirifudō Kofun]] [[en:Tebika Namikirifudō Kofun]] [[ja:手光波切不動古墳]]

The burial facility is a {{ill|Side-entrance chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} type {{ill|Stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening towards the south-southwest. The total length of the stone chamber is 10.8 meters, and large basalt stones were used as its construction material. Despite the chamber being open for centuries, a survey of the tomb path in front of the chamber revealed wooden coffin components (nails and clamps), as well as iron products, horse gear, and fragments of [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] and Silla pottery{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. The construction of the mound is estimated to date to the early [[7th century]], with possible additional burials through the late 7th century{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. Previously considered to follow the {{ill|Miyajidake Kofun|ja|宮地嶽古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyajidake Kofun|qq=}}, it is now viewed as preceding it, and is considered a successor to the {{ill|Zaiji Kentsuka Kofun|ja|在自剣塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zaiji Kentsuka Kofun|qq=}}, the largest [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] in the Munakata region{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. As a tomb adopting the side-entrance chamber structure typical of the Kinai region, it holds significance for understanding the political landscape of the Munakata region{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. In [[1992]] (Heisei 4), the mound was Historic Site1 as a historic site by the typeer Fukuma Town, now Fukutsu City. == Excavation History == * [[Tenpō]] era ([[1830]]–[[1844]]): Mentioned in the ''{{ill|Zoku Fudoki of Chikuzen Province|ja|筑前国続風土記拾遺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Fudoki of Chikuzen Province|qq=}}'' (described as a faith site with the stone chamber already open, known as Fudo Cave){{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. * [[1992]]: Detailed measurements of the stone chamber{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. * October 13, 1992: Designated as a Fukuma Town Historic Site (now a Fukutsu City Historic Site){{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. * [[2010]]: Mound survey by the Fukutsu City Board of Education, reported in [[2013]]{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. * [[2011]]: Confirmation survey of the mound's size{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. == burial == [[File:Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Bird's-eye view of the stone chamber}} ]] [[File:Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the stone chamber}} ]] The burial facility is a {{ill|Side-entrance chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} type {{ill|Stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening south-southwest. The rear part of the main chamber narrows into rear and front chambers, showcasing a multi-chamber structure similar to those in the Kinai region. The area of the stone chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}: * Total length of the stone chamber: 10.8 meters (preserved) * Rear chamber: Length 2.1 meters, width 1.3 meters, height 1.9 meters * Front chamber: Length 1.8 meters, width 1.6 meters, height 2 meters * Passageway The chamber is constructed using basalt slabs, possibly sourced from [[:en:Ainoshima_(Shingū)|Ainoshima]]. Both the passageway and main chamber consist of a single layer of large stones, with five stones on each side wall and one stone on the rear wall. The floor is paved with large flat stones in some parts, while other parts have stone and pebble paving beneath approximately 10 cm of soil{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. == See Also == * [[Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster]] ** [[Miyajidake Kofun]] == References == {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|手光波切不動古墳|version=102446439|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Burial sites in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Fukutsu City History]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Munakata clan|Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Tebika Namikirifudō Kofun]][[sn:Tebika Namikirifudō Kofun]][[sn:Tebika Namikirifudō Kofun]]}} [[sn:Tebika Namikirifudō Kofun]] [[en:Tebika Namikirifudō Kofun]] [[ja:手光波切不動古墳]]
