This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Japanese Kofun | name = Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun | image = [[File:Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]] | caption = Mound and stone chamber entrance | alternate_name = | part_of = [[:en:Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster]] | location = [[:en:Fukutsu, Fukuoka|Fukutsu City]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]], Tebika 1615 (Ishibashi) | coordinates = 33 | coordinates = 46 | coordinates = 13.23 | coordinates = 130 | coordinates = 29 | coordinates = 52.58 | omitted = JP-40 | type = (Estimated) {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | area = Diameter 25m | Burial Facility = {{ill|Side-entrance chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} type {{ill|Stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}
(Wooden coffin inside) | burial goods = Iron products, horse gear, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], Silla pottery | associated_tombs = | period = Early [[:en:7th century|7th century]] | occupant = | imperial_tomb = | historic_site = Fukutsu City Designated Historic Site "Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun" | tangible_cultural_properties = | notes = | map = Japan Fukuoka | type = Round Kofun | map_dot_label = Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|33|46|30|N|130|29|40|E}} | zoom = 14 | float = right | width = 250| height = 200 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun and Miyajidake Kofun}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1 = {{Coord|33|46|13.23|N|130|29|52.58|E}} | map_dot_label1 = Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun | map_dot_label-pos1 = bottom | map_dot_label-color1 = black | mark1 = Round Kofun.png | mark-size1 = 20 | mark-coord2 = {{Coord|33|46|51.10|N|130|29|19.15|E}} | map_dot_label2 = Miyajidake Kofun | map_dot_label-pos2 = right | map_dot_label-color2 = black | mark2 = Round Kofun.png | mark-size2 = 20 }} The '''Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun''' (手光波切不動古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in Tebika, [[:en:Fukutsu, Fukuoka|Fukutsu City]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]]. It is one of the Kofuns that make up the [[:en:Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster]] and has been Historic Site1 as a historic site by Fukutsu City. == Overview == This Kofun is located on the terminal end of a hill (22–27 meters above sea level) derived from the foothills of Mount Miyaji, in northern Fukuoka Prefecture. It occupies the southernmost position among the Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster. The stone chamber has been open since ancient times, and the mound has been partially leveled. Detailed investigations of the stone chamber were conducted in [[:en:1992|1992]] (Heisei 4), while surveys of the mound were pertypeed during [[:en:2010|2010]]–[[:en:2011|2011]] (Heisei 22–23). Due to leveling at the mound's base, its exact type is unclear, but the mound is currently estimated to have a diameter of 20 meters and a height of 9 meters. Originally, it is reconstructed to have been approximately 25 meters in diameter{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. The burial facility is a {{ill|Side-entrance chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} type {{ill|Stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening towards the south-southwest. The total length of the stone chamber is 10.8 meters, and large basalt stones were used as its construction material. Despite the chamber being open for centuries, a survey of the tomb path in front of the chamber revealed wooden coffin components (nails and clamps), as well as iron products, horse gear, and fragments of [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] and Silla pottery{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. The construction of the mound is estimated to date to the early [[:en:7th century|7th century]], with possible additional burials through the late 7th century{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. Previously considered to follow the {{ill|Miyajidake Kofun|ja|宮地嶽古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyajidake Kofun|qq=}}, it is now viewed as preceding it, and is considered a successor to the {{ill|Zaiji Kentsuka Kofun|ja|在自剣塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zaiji Kentsuka Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the largest [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] in the Munakata region{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. As a tomb adopting the side-entrance chamber structure typical of the Kinai region, it holds significance for understanding the political landscape of the Munakata region{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. In [[:en:1992|1992]] (Heisei 4), the mound was Historic Site1 as a historic site by the typeer Fukuma Town, now Fukutsu City. == Excavation History == * [[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] era ([[:en:1830|1830]]–[[:en:1844|1844]]): Mentioned in the ''{{ill|Zoku Fudoki of Chikuzen Province|ja|筑前国続風土記拾遺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Fudoki of Chikuzen Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' (described as a faith site with the stone chamber already open, known as Fudo Cave){{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. * [[:en:1992|1992]]: Detailed measurements of the stone chamber{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. * October 13, 1992: Designated as a Fukuma Town Historic Site (now a Fukutsu City Historic Site){{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. * [[:en:2010|2010]]: Mound survey by the Fukutsu City Board of Education, reported in [[:en:2013|2013]]{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. * [[:en:2011|2011]]: Confirmation survey of the mound's size{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. == burial == [[File:Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Bird's-eye view of the stone chamber}} ]] [[File:Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the stone chamber}} ]] The burial facility is a {{ill|Side-entrance chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} type {{ill|Stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening south-southwest. The rear part of the main chamber narrows into rear and front chambers, showcasing a multi-chamber structure similar to those in the Kinai region. The area of the stone chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}: * Total length of the stone chamber: 10.8 meters (preserved) * Rear chamber: Length 2.1 meters, width 1.3 meters, height 1.9 meters * Front chamber: Length 1.8 meters, width 1.6 meters, height 2 meters * Passageway The chamber is constructed using basalt slabs, possibly sourced from [[:en:Ainoshima_(Shingū)|Ainoshima]]. Both the passageway and main chamber consist of a single layer of large stones, with five stones on each side wall and one stone on the rear wall. The floor is paved with large flat stones in some parts, while other parts have stone and pebble paving beneath approximately 10 cm of soil{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. == See Also == * [[:en:Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster]] ** [[Miyajidake Kofun]] == References == {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|手光波切不動古墳|version=102446439|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:手光波切不動古墳]] {{Commons category|Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q117452644}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Has wikidata]] [[Category:Kofun of Fukuoka prefecture]] [[Category:Munakata clan]] [[Category:Munakata clan|Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Fukutsu]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

Due to leveling at the mound's base, its exact type is unclear, but the mound is currently estimated to have a diameter of 20 meters and a height of 9 meters. Originally, it is reconstructed to have been approximately 25 meters in diameter{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. The burial facility is a {{ill|Side-entrance chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} type {{ill|Stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening towards the south-southwest. The total length of the stone chamber is 10.8 meters, and large basalt stones were used as its construction material. Despite the chamber being open for centuries, a survey of the tomb path in front of the chamber revealed wooden coffin components (nails and clamps), as well as iron products, horse gear, and fragments of [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] and Silla pottery{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. The construction of the mound is estimated to date to the early [[:en:7th century|7th century]], with possible additional burials through the late 7th century{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. Previously considered to follow the {{ill|Miyajidake Kofun|ja|宮地嶽古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyajidake Kofun|qq=}}, it is now viewed as preceding it, and is considered a successor to the {{ill|Zaiji Kentsuka Kofun|ja|在自剣塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zaiji Kentsuka Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the largest [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] in the Munakata region{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. As a tomb adopting the side-entrance chamber structure typical of the Kinai region, it holds significance for understanding the political landscape of the Munakata region{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. In [[:en:1992|1992]] (Heisei 4), the mound was Historic Site1 as a historic site by the typeer Fukuma Town, now Fukutsu City. == Excavation History == * [[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] era ([[:en:1830|1830]]–[[:en:1844|1844]]): Mentioned in the ''{{ill|Zoku Fudoki of Chikuzen Province|ja|筑前国続風土記拾遺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Fudoki of Chikuzen Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' (described as a faith site with the stone chamber already open, known as Fudo Cave){{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. * [[:en:1992|1992]]: Detailed measurements of the stone chamber{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. * October 13, 1992: Designated as a Fukuma Town Historic Site (now a Fukutsu City Historic Site){{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. * [[:en:2010|2010]]: Mound survey by the Fukutsu City Board of Education, reported in [[:en:2013|2013]]{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. * [[:en:2011|2011]]: Confirmation survey of the mound's size{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. == burial == [[File:Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Bird's-eye view of the stone chamber}} ]] [[File:Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the stone chamber}} ]] The burial facility is a {{ill|Side-entrance chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} type {{ill|Stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening south-southwest. The rear part of the main chamber narrows into rear and front chambers, showcasing a multi-chamber structure similar to those in the Kinai region. The area of the stone chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}: * Total length of the stone chamber: 10.8 meters (preserved) * Rear chamber: Length 2.1 meters, width 1.3 meters, height 1.9 meters * Front chamber: Length 1.8 meters, width 1.6 meters, height 2 meters * Passageway The chamber is constructed using basalt slabs, possibly sourced from [[:en:Ainoshima_(Shingū)|Ainoshima]]. Both the passageway and main chamber consist of a single layer of large stones, with five stones on each side wall and one stone on the rear wall. The floor is paved with large flat stones in some parts, while other parts have stone and pebble paving beneath approximately 10 cm of soil{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. == See Also == * [[:en:Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster]] ** [[Miyajidake Kofun]] == References == {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|手光波切不動古墳|version=102446439|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:手光波切不動古墳]] {{Commons category|Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q117452644}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Has wikidata]] [[Category:Kofun of Fukuoka prefecture]] [[Category:Munakata clan]] [[Category:Munakata clan|Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Fukutsu]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

(Wooden coffin inside) | burial goods = Iron products, horse gear, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], Silla pottery | associated_tombs = | period = Early [[:en:7th century|7th century]] | occupant = | imperial_tomb = | historic_site = Fukutsu City Designated Historic Site "Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun" | tangible_cultural_properties = | notes = | map = Japan Fukuoka | type = Round Kofun | map_dot_label = Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|33|46|30|N|130|29|40|E}} | zoom = 14 | float = right | width = 250| height = 200 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun and Miyajidake Kofun}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1 = {{Coord|33|46|13.23|N|130|29|52.58|E}} | map_dot_label1 = Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun | map_dot_label-pos1 = bottom | map_dot_label-color1 = black | mark1 = Round Kofun.png | mark-size1 = 20 | mark-coord2 = {{Coord|33|46|51.10|N|130|29|19.15|E}} | map_dot_label2 = Miyajidake Kofun | map_dot_label-pos2 = right | map_dot_label-color2 = black | mark2 = Round Kofun.png | mark-size2 = 20 }} The '''Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun''' (手光波切不動古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in Tebika, [[:en:Fukutsu, Fukuoka|Fukutsu City]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]]. It is one of the Kofuns that make up the [[:en:Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster]] and has been Historic Site1 as a historic site by Fukutsu City. == Overview == This Kofun is located on the terminal end of a hill (22–27 meters above sea level) derived from the foothills of Mount Miyaji, in northern Fukuoka Prefecture. It occupies the southernmost position among the Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster. The stone chamber has been open since ancient times, and the mound has been partially leveled. Detailed investigations of the stone chamber were conducted in [[:en:1992|1992]] (Heisei 4), while surveys of the mound were pertypeed during [[:en:2010|2010]]–[[:en:2011|2011]] (Heisei 22–23). Due to leveling at the mound's base, its exact type is unclear, but the mound is currently estimated to have a diameter of 20 meters and a height of 9 meters. Originally, it is reconstructed to have been approximately 25 meters in diameter{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. The burial facility is a {{ill|Side-entrance chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} type {{ill|Stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening towards the south-southwest. The total length of the stone chamber is 10.8 meters, and large basalt stones were used as its construction material. Despite the chamber being open for centuries, a survey of the tomb path in front of the chamber revealed wooden coffin components (nails and clamps), as well as iron products, horse gear, and fragments of [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] and Silla pottery{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. The construction of the mound is estimated to date to the early [[:en:7th century|7th century]], with possible additional burials through the late 7th century{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. Previously considered to follow the {{ill|Miyajidake Kofun|ja|宮地嶽古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyajidake Kofun|qq=}}, it is now viewed as preceding it, and is considered a successor to the {{ill|Zaiji Kentsuka Kofun|ja|在自剣塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zaiji Kentsuka Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the largest [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] in the Munakata region{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. As a tomb adopting the side-entrance chamber structure typical of the Kinai region, it holds significance for understanding the political landscape of the Munakata region{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. In [[:en:1992|1992]] (Heisei 4), the mound was Historic Site1 as a historic site by the typeer Fukuma Town, now Fukutsu City. == Excavation History == * [[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] era ([[:en:1830|1830]]–[[:en:1844|1844]]): Mentioned in the ''{{ill|Zoku Fudoki of Chikuzen Province|ja|筑前国続風土記拾遺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Fudoki of Chikuzen Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' (described as a faith site with the stone chamber already open, known as Fudo Cave){{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. * [[:en:1992|1992]]: Detailed measurements of the stone chamber{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. * October 13, 1992: Designated as a Fukuma Town Historic Site (now a Fukutsu City Historic Site){{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. * [[:en:2010|2010]]: Mound survey by the Fukutsu City Board of Education, reported in [[:en:2013|2013]]{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. * [[:en:2011|2011]]: Confirmation survey of the mound's size{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. == burial == [[File:Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Bird's-eye view of the stone chamber}} ]] [[File:Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the stone chamber}} ]] The burial facility is a {{ill|Side-entrance chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} type {{ill|Stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening south-southwest. The rear part of the main chamber narrows into rear and front chambers, showcasing a multi-chamber structure similar to those in the Kinai region. Both the passageway and main chamber consist of a single layer of large stones, with five stones on each side wall and one stone on the rear wall. The floor is paved with large flat stones in some parts, while other parts have stone and pebble paving beneath approximately 10 cm of soil{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. == See Also == * [[:en:Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster]] ** [[Miyajidake Kofun]] == References == {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|手光波切不動古墳|version=102446439|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:手光波切不動古墳]] {{Commons category|Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q117452644}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Has wikidata]] [[Category:Kofun of Fukuoka prefecture]] [[Category:Munakata clan]] [[Category:Munakata clan|Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Fukutsu]] [[Category:Qq]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

Due to leveling at the mound's base, its exact type is unclear, but the mound is currently estimated to have a diameter of 20 meters and a height of 9 meters. Originally, it is reconstructed to have been approximately 25 meters in diameter{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. The burial facility is a {{ill|Side-entrance chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} type {{ill|Stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening towards the south-southwest. The total length of the stone chamber is 10.8 meters, and large basalt stones were used as its construction material. Despite the chamber being open for centuries, a survey of the tomb path in front of the chamber revealed wooden coffin components (nails and clamps), as well as iron products, horse gear, and fragments of [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] and Silla pottery{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. The construction of the mound is estimated to date to the early [[:en:7th century|7th century]], with possible additional burials through the late 7th century{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. Previously considered to follow the {{ill|Miyajidake Kofun|ja|宮地嶽古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyajidake Kofun|qq=}}, it is now viewed as preceding it, and is considered a successor to the {{ill|Zaiji Kentsuka Kofun|ja|在自剣塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zaiji Kentsuka Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the largest [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] in the Munakata region{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. As a tomb adopting the side-entrance chamber structure typical of the Kinai region, it holds significance for understanding the political landscape of the Munakata region{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. In [[:en:1992|1992]] (Heisei 4), the mound was Historic Site1 as a historic site by the typeer Fukuma Town, now Fukutsu City. == Excavation History == * [[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] era ([[:en:1830|1830]]–[[:en:1844|1844]]): Mentioned in the ''{{ill|Zoku Fudoki of Chikuzen Province|ja|筑前国続風土記拾遺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Fudoki of Chikuzen Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' (described as a faith site with the stone chamber already open, known as Fudo Cave){{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. * [[:en:1992|1992]]: Detailed measurements of the stone chamber{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. * October 13, 1992: Designated as a Fukuma Town Historic Site (now a Fukutsu City Historic Site){{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. * [[:en:2010|2010]]: Mound survey by the Fukutsu City Board of Education, reported in [[:en:2013|2013]]{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. * [[:en:2011|2011]]: Confirmation survey of the mound's size{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. == burial == [[File:Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Bird's-eye view of the stone chamber}} ]] [[File:Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the stone chamber}} ]] The burial facility is a {{ill|Side-entrance chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} type {{ill|Stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening south-southwest. The rear part of the main chamber narrows into rear and front chambers, showcasing a multi-chamber structure similar to those in the Kinai region. The area of the stone chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}: * Total length of the stone chamber: 10.8 meters (preserved) * Rear chamber: Length 2.1 meters, width 1.3 meters, height 1.9 meters * Front chamber: Length 1.8 meters, width 1.6 meters, height 2 meters * Passageway The chamber is constructed using basalt slabs, possibly sourced from [[:en:Ainoshima_(Shingū)|Ainoshima]]. Both the passageway and main chamber consist of a single layer of large stones, with five stones on each side wall and one stone on the rear wall. The floor is paved with large flat stones in some parts, while other parts have stone and pebble paving beneath approximately 10 cm of soil{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. == See Also == * [[:en:Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster]] ** [[Miyajidake Kofun]] == References == {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|手光波切不動古墳|version=102446439|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:手光波切不動古墳]] {{Commons category|Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q117452644}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Has wikidata]] [[Category:Kofun of Fukuoka prefecture]] [[Category:Munakata clan]] [[Category:Munakata clan|Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Fukutsu]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Japanese Kofun | name = Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun | image = [[File:Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]] | caption = Mound and stone chamber entrance | alternate_name = | part_of = {{ill|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster|en|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster|WD=Q109595331|lt=Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster}} | location = {{ill|Fukutsu, Fukuoka|en|Fukutsu, Fukuoka|WD=Q825700|lt=Fukutsu City}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture}}, Tebika 1615 (Ishibashi) | coordinates = 33 | coordinates = 46 | coordinates = 13.23 | coordinates = 130 | coordinates = 29 | coordinates = 52.58 | omitted = JP-40 | type = (Estimated) {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | area = Diameter 25m | Burial Facility = {{ill|Side-entrance chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} type {{ill|Stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}
(Wooden coffin inside) | burial goods = Iron products, horse gear, {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}}, Silla pottery | associated_tombs = | period = Early {{ill|7th century|en|7th century|WD=Q8089|lt=7th century}} | occupant = | imperial_tomb = | historic_site = Fukutsu City Designated Historic Site "Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun" | tangible_cultural_properties = | notes = | map = Japan Fukuoka | type = Round Kofun | map_dot_label = Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|33|46|30|N|130|29|40|E}} | zoom = 14 | float = right | width = 250| height = 200 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun and Miyajidake Kofun}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1 = {{Coord|33|46|13.23|N|130|29|52.58|E}} | map_dot_label1 = Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun | map_dot_label-pos1 = bottom | map_dot_label-color1 = black | mark1 = Round Kofun.png | mark-size1 = 20 | mark-coord2 = {{Coord|33|46|51.10|N|130|29|19.15|E}} | map_dot_label2 = Miyajidake Kofun | map_dot_label-pos2 = right | map_dot_label-color2 = black | mark2 = Round Kofun.png | mark-size2 = 20 }} The '''Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun''' (手光波切不動古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun}} located in Tebika, {{ill|Fukutsu, Fukuoka|en|Fukutsu, Fukuoka|WD=Q825700|lt=Fukutsu City}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture}}. It is one of the Kofuns that make up the {{ill|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster|en|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster|WD=Q109595331|lt=Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster}} and has been Historic Site1 as a historic site by Fukutsu City. == Overview == This Kofun is located on the terminal end of a hill (22–27 meters above sea level) derived from the foothills of Mount Miyaji, in northern Fukuoka Prefecture. It occupies the southernmost position among the Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster. The stone chamber has been open since ancient times, and the mound has been partially leveled. Detailed investigations of the stone chamber were conducted in {{ill|1992|en|1992|WD=Q2060|lt=1992}} (Heisei 4), while surveys of the mound were pertypeed during {{ill|2010|en|2010|WD=Q1995|lt=2010}}–{{ill|2011|en|2011|WD=Q1994|lt=2011}} (Heisei 22–23). Due to leveling at the mound's base, its exact type is unclear, but the mound is currently estimated to have a diameter of 20 meters and a height of 9 meters. Originally, it is reconstructed to have been approximately 25 meters in diameter{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. The burial facility is a {{ill|Side-entrance chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} type {{ill|Stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening towards the south-southwest. The total length of the stone chamber is 10.8 meters, and large basalt stones were used as its construction material. Despite the chamber being open for centuries, a survey of the tomb path in front of the chamber revealed wooden coffin components (nails and clamps), as well as iron products, horse gear, and fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}} and Silla pottery{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. The construction of the mound is estimated to date to the early {{ill|7th century|en|7th century|WD=Q8089|lt=7th century}}, with possible additional burials through the late 7th century{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. Previously considered to follow the {{ill|Miyajidake Kofun|ja|宮地嶽古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyajidake Kofun|qq=}}, it is now viewed as preceding it, and is considered a successor to the {{ill|Zaiji Kentsuka Kofun|ja|在自剣塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zaiji Kentsuka Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the largest {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} in the Munakata region{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. As a tomb adopting the side-entrance chamber structure typical of the Kinai region, it holds significance for understanding the political landscape of the Munakata region{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. In {{ill|1992|en|1992|WD=Q2060|lt=1992}} (Heisei 4), the mound was Historic Site1 as a historic site by the typeer Fukuma Town, now Fukutsu City. == Excavation History == * {{ill|Tenpō|en|Tenpō|WD=Q1369045|lt=Tenpō}} era ({{ill|1830|en|1830|WD=Q7576|lt=1830}}–{{ill|1844|en|1844|WD=Q7633|lt=1844}}): Mentioned in the ''{{ill|Zoku Fudoki of Chikuzen Province|ja|筑前国続風土記拾遺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Fudoki of Chikuzen Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' (described as a faith site with the stone chamber already open, known as Fudo Cave){{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. * {{ill|1992|en|1992|WD=Q2060|lt=1992}}: Detailed measurements of the stone chamber{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. * October 13, 1992: Designated as a Fukuma Town Historic Site (now a Fukutsu City Historic Site){{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. * {{ill|2010|en|2010|WD=Q1995|lt=2010}}: Mound survey by the Fukutsu City Board of Education, reported in {{ill|2013|en|2013|WD=Q1998|lt=2013}}{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. * {{ill|2011|en|2011|WD=Q1994|lt=2011}}: Confirmation survey of the mound's size{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. == burial == [[File:Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Bird's-eye view of the stone chamber}} ]] [[File:Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the stone chamber}} ]] The burial facility is a {{ill|Side-entrance chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} type {{ill|Stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening south-southwest. The rear part of the main chamber narrows into rear and front chambers, showcasing a multi-chamber structure similar to those in the Kinai region. The area of the stone chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}: * Total length of the stone chamber: 10.8 meters (preserved) * Rear chamber: Length 2.1 meters, width 1.3 meters, height 1.9 meters * Front chamber: Length 1.8 meters, width 1.6 meters, height 2 meters * Passageway The chamber is constructed using basalt slabs, possibly sourced from {{ill|Ainoshima_(Shingū)|en|Ainoshima_(Shingū)|WD=Q11581975|lt=Ainoshima}}. Both the passageway and main chamber consist of a single layer of large stones, with five stones on each side wall and one stone on the rear wall. The floor is paved with large flat stones in some parts, while other parts have stone and pebble paving beneath approximately 10 cm of soil{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. == See Also == * {{ill|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster|en|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster|WD=Q109595331|lt=Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster}} ** [[Miyajidake Kofun]] == References == {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|手光波切不動古墳|version=102446439|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:手光波切不動古墳]] {{Commons category|Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q117452644}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Has wikidata]] [[Category:Kofun of Fukuoka prefecture]] [[Category:Munakata clan]] [[Category:Munakata clan|Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Fukutsu]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

(Wooden coffin inside) | burial goods = Iron products, horse gear, {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}}, Silla pottery | associated_tombs = | period = Early {{ill|7th century|en|7th century|WD=Q8089|lt=7th century}} | occupant = | imperial_tomb = | historic_site = Fukutsu City Designated Historic Site "Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun" | tangible_cultural_properties = | notes = | map = Japan Fukuoka | type = Round Kofun | map_dot_label = Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|33|46|30|N|130|29|40|E}} | zoom = 14 | float = right | width = 250| height = 200 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun and Miyajidake Kofun}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1 = {{Coord|33|46|13.23|N|130|29|52.58|E}} | map_dot_label1 = Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun | map_dot_label-pos1 = bottom | map_dot_label-color1 = black | mark1 = Round Kofun.png | mark-size1 = 20 | mark-coord2 = {{Coord|33|46|51.10|N|130|29|19.15|E}} | map_dot_label2 = Miyajidake Kofun | map_dot_label-pos2 = right | map_dot_label-color2 = black | mark2 = Round Kofun.png | mark-size2 = 20 }} The '''Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun''' (手光波切不動古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun}} located in Tebika, {{ill|Fukutsu, Fukuoka|en|Fukutsu, Fukuoka|WD=Q825700|lt=Fukutsu City}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture}}. It is one of the Kofuns that make up the {{ill|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster|en|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster|WD=Q109595331|lt=Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster}} and has been Historic Site1 as a historic site by Fukutsu City. == Overview == This Kofun is located on the terminal end of a hill (22–27 meters above sea level) derived from the foothills of Mount Miyaji, in northern Fukuoka Prefecture. It occupies the southernmost position among the Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster. The stone chamber has been open since ancient times, and the mound has been partially leveled. Detailed investigations of the stone chamber were conducted in {{ill|1992|en|1992|WD=Q2060|lt=1992}} (Heisei 4), while surveys of the mound were pertypeed during {{ill|2010|en|2010|WD=Q1995|lt=2010}}–{{ill|2011|en|2011|WD=Q1994|lt=2011}} (Heisei 22–23). Due to leveling at the mound's base, its exact type is unclear, but the mound is currently estimated to have a diameter of 20 meters and a height of 9 meters. Originally, it is reconstructed to have been approximately 25 meters in diameter{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. The burial facility is a {{ill|Side-entrance chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} type {{ill|Stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening towards the south-southwest. The total length of the stone chamber is 10.8 meters, and large basalt stones were used as its construction material. Despite the chamber being open for centuries, a survey of the tomb path in front of the chamber revealed wooden coffin components (nails and clamps), as well as iron products, horse gear, and fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}} and Silla pottery{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. The construction of the mound is estimated to date to the early {{ill|7th century|en|7th century|WD=Q8089|lt=7th century}}, with possible additional burials through the late 7th century{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. Previously considered to follow the {{ill|Miyajidake Kofun|ja|宮地嶽古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyajidake Kofun|qq=}}, it is now viewed as preceding it, and is considered a successor to the {{ill|Zaiji Kentsuka Kofun|ja|在自剣塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zaiji Kentsuka Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the largest {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} in the Munakata region{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. As a tomb adopting the side-entrance chamber structure typical of the Kinai region, it holds significance for understanding the political landscape of the Munakata region{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. In {{ill|1992|en|1992|WD=Q2060|lt=1992}} (Heisei 4), the mound was Historic Site1 as a historic site by the typeer Fukuma Town, now Fukutsu City. == Excavation History == * {{ill|Tenpō|en|Tenpō|WD=Q1369045|lt=Tenpō}} era ({{ill|1830|en|1830|WD=Q7576|lt=1830}}–{{ill|1844|en|1844|WD=Q7633|lt=1844}}): Mentioned in the ''{{ill|Zoku Fudoki of Chikuzen Province|ja|筑前国続風土記拾遺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Fudoki of Chikuzen Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' (described as a faith site with the stone chamber already open, known as Fudo Cave){{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. * {{ill|1992|en|1992|WD=Q2060|lt=1992}}: Detailed measurements of the stone chamber{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. * October 13, 1992: Designated as a Fukuma Town Historic Site (now a Fukutsu City Historic Site){{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. * {{ill|2010|en|2010|WD=Q1995|lt=2010}}: Mound survey by the Fukutsu City Board of Education, reported in {{ill|2013|en|2013|WD=Q1998|lt=2013}}{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. * {{ill|2011|en|2011|WD=Q1994|lt=2011}}: Confirmation survey of the mound's size{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. == burial == [[File:Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Bird's-eye view of the stone chamber}} ]] [[File:Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the stone chamber}} ]] The burial facility is a {{ill|Side-entrance chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} type {{ill|Stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening south-southwest. The rear part of the main chamber narrows into rear and front chambers, showcasing a multi-chamber structure similar to those in the Kinai region. The area of the stone chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}: * Total length of the stone chamber: 10.8 meters (preserved) * Rear chamber: Length 2.1 meters, width 1.3 meters, height 1.9 meters * Front chamber: Length 1.8 meters, width 1.6 meters, height 2 meters * Passageway The chamber is constructed using basalt slabs, possibly sourced from {{ill|Ainoshima_(Shingū)|en|Ainoshima_(Shingū)|WD=Q11581975|lt=Ainoshima}}. Both the passageway and main chamber consist of a single layer of large stones, with five stones on each side wall and one stone on the rear wall. The floor is paved with large flat stones in some parts, while other parts have stone and pebble paving beneath approximately 10 cm of soil{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. == See Also == * {{ill|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster|en|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster|WD=Q109595331|lt=Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster}} ** [[Miyajidake Kofun]] == References == {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|手光波切不動古墳|version=102446439|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:手光波切不動古墳]] {{Commons category|Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q117452644}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Has wikidata]] [[Category:Kofun of Fukuoka prefecture]] [[Category:Munakata clan]] [[Category:Munakata clan|Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Fukutsu]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun]][[ja:手光波切不動古墳]] [[co:commons:Category:Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun]][[ja:手光波切不動古墳]] [[sn:commons:Category:Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun]][[ja:手光波切不動古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|coordinates={{Coord|33.77033333333333|N|130.49794444444444|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}} {{Infobox Japanese Kofun | name = Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun | image = [[File:Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]] | caption = Mound and stone chamber entrance | alternate_name = | part_of = {{ill|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster|en|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster|WD=Q109595331|lt=Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster}} | location = {{ill|Fukutsu, Fukuoka|en|Fukutsu, Fukuoka|WD=Q825700|lt=Fukutsu City}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture}}, Tebika 1615 (Ishibashi) | coordinates = 33 | coordinates = 46 | coordinates = 13.23 | coordinates = 130 | coordinates = 29 | coordinates = 52.58 | omitted = JP-40 | type = (Estimated) {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | area = Diameter 25m | Burial Facility = {{ill|Side-entrance chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} type {{ill|Stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}
(Wooden coffin inside) | burial goods = Iron products, horse gear, {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}}, Silla pottery | associated_tombs = | period = Early {{ill|7th century|en|7th century|WD=Q8089|lt=7th century}} | occupant = | imperial_tomb = | historic_site = Fukutsu City Designated Historic Site "Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun" | tangible_cultural_properties = | notes = | map = Japan Fukuoka | type = Round Kofun | map_dot_label = Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|33|46|30|N|130|29|40|E}} | zoom = 14 | float = right | width = 250| height = 200 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun and Miyajidake Kofun}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1 = {{Coord|33|46|13.23|N|130|29|52.58|E}} | map_dot_label1 = Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun | map_dot_label-pos1 = bottom | map_dot_label-color1 = black | mark1 = Round Kofun.png | mark-size1 = 20 | mark-coord2 = {{Coord|33|46|51.10|N|130|29|19.15|E}} | map_dot_label2 = Miyajidake Kofun | map_dot_label-pos2 = right | map_dot_label-color2 = black | mark2 = Round Kofun.png | mark-size2 = 20 }} The '''Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun''' (手光波切不動古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun}} located in Tebika, {{ill|Fukutsu, Fukuoka|en|Fukutsu, Fukuoka|WD=Q825700|lt=Fukutsu City}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture}}. It is one of the Kofuns that make up the {{ill|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster|en|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster|WD=Q109595331|lt=Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster}} and has been Historic Site1 as a historic site by Fukutsu City. == Overview == This Kofun is located on the terminal end of a hill (22–27 meters above sea level) derived from the foothills of Mount Miyaji, in northern Fukuoka Prefecture. It occupies the southernmost position among the Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster. The stone chamber has been open since ancient times, and the mound has been partially leveled. Detailed investigations of the stone chamber were conducted in {{ill|1992|en|1992|WD=Q2060|lt=1992}} (Heisei 4), while surveys of the mound were pertypeed during {{ill|2010|en|2010|WD=Q1995|lt=2010}}–{{ill|2011|en|2011|WD=Q1994|lt=2011}} (Heisei 22–23). Due to leveling at the mound's base, its exact type is unclear, but the mound is currently estimated to have a diameter of 20 meters and a height of 9 meters. Originally, it is reconstructed to have been approximately 25 meters in diameter{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. The burial facility is a {{ill|Side-entrance chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} type {{ill|Stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening towards the south-southwest. The total length of the stone chamber is 10.8 meters, and large basalt stones were used as its construction material. Despite the chamber being open for centuries, a survey of the tomb path in front of the chamber revealed wooden coffin components (nails and clamps), as well as iron products, horse gear, and fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}} and Silla pottery{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. The construction of the mound is estimated to date to the early {{ill|7th century|en|7th century|WD=Q8089|lt=7th century}}, with possible additional burials through the late 7th century{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. Previously considered to follow the {{ill|Miyajidake Kofun|ja|宮地嶽古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyajidake Kofun|qq=}}, it is now viewed as preceding it, and is considered a successor to the {{ill|Zaiji Kentsuka Kofun|ja|在自剣塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zaiji Kentsuka Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the largest {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} in the Munakata region{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. As a tomb adopting the side-entrance chamber structure typical of the Kinai region, it holds significance for understanding the political landscape of the Munakata region{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. In {{ill|1992|en|1992|WD=Q2060|lt=1992}} (Heisei 4), the mound was Historic Site1 as a historic site by the typeer Fukuma Town, now Fukutsu City. == Excavation History == * {{ill|Tenpō|en|Tenpō|WD=Q1369045|lt=Tenpō}} era ({{ill|1830|en|1830|WD=Q7576|lt=1830}}–{{ill|1844|en|1844|WD=Q7633|lt=1844}}): Mentioned in the ''{{ill|Zoku Fudoki of Chikuzen Province|ja|筑前国続風土記拾遺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Fudoki of Chikuzen Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' (described as a faith site with the stone chamber already open, known as Fudo Cave){{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. * {{ill|1992|en|1992|WD=Q2060|lt=1992}}: Detailed measurements of the stone chamber{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. * October 13, 1992: Designated as a Fukuma Town Historic Site (now a Fukutsu City Historic Site){{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. * {{ill|2010|en|2010|WD=Q1995|lt=2010}}: Mound survey by the Fukutsu City Board of Education, reported in {{ill|2013|en|2013|WD=Q1998|lt=2013}}{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. * {{ill|2011|en|2011|WD=Q1994|lt=2011}}: Confirmation survey of the mound's size{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. == burial == [[File:Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Bird's-eye view of the stone chamber}} ]] [[File:Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the stone chamber}} ]] The burial facility is a {{ill|Side-entrance chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} type {{ill|Stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening south-southwest. The rear part of the main chamber narrows into rear and front chambers, showcasing a multi-chamber structure similar to those in the Kinai region. The area of the stone chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}: * Total length of the stone chamber: 10.8 meters (preserved) * Rear chamber: Length 2.1 meters, width 1.3 meters, height 1.9 meters * Front chamber: Length 1.8 meters, width 1.6 meters, height 2 meters * Passageway The chamber is constructed using basalt slabs, possibly sourced from {{ill|Ainoshima_(Shingū)|en|Ainoshima_(Shingū)|WD=Q11581975|lt=Ainoshima}}. Both the passageway and main chamber consist of a single layer of large stones, with five stones on each side wall and one stone on the rear wall. The floor is paved with large flat stones in some parts, while other parts have stone and pebble paving beneath approximately 10 cm of soil{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. == See Also == * {{ill|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster|en|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster|WD=Q109595331|lt=Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster}} ** [[Miyajidake Kofun]] == References == {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|手光波切不動古墳|version=102446439|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:手光波切不動古墳]] {{Commons category|Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q117452644}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Has wikidata]] [[Category:Kofun of Fukuoka prefecture]] [[Category:Munakata clan]] [[Category:Munakata clan|Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Fukutsu]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun]][[ja:手光波切不動古墳]] [[co:commons:Category:Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun]][[ja:手光波切不動古墳]] [[sn:commons:Category:Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun]][[ja:手光波切不動古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|coordinates={{Coord|33.77033333333333|N|130.49794444444444|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}} {{Infobox Japanese Kofun | name = Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun | image = [[File:Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]] | caption = Mound and stone chamber entrance | alternate_name = | part_of = {{ill|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster|en|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster|WD=Q109595331|lt=Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster}} | location = {{ill|Fukutsu, Fukuoka|en|Fukutsu, Fukuoka|WD=Q825700|lt=Fukutsu City}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture}}, Tebika 1615 (Ishibashi) | coordinates = 33 | coordinates = 46 | coordinates = 13.23 | coordinates = 130 | coordinates = 29 | coordinates = 52.58 | omitted = JP-40 | type = (Estimated) {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | area = Diameter 25m | Burial Facility = {{ill|Side-entrance chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} type {{ill|Stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}
(Wooden coffin inside) | burial goods = Iron products, horse gear, {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}}, Silla pottery | associated_tombs = | period = Early {{ill|7th century|en|7th century|WD=Q8089|lt=7th century}} | occupant = | imperial_tomb = | historic_site = Fukutsu City Designated Historic Site "Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun" | tangible_cultural_properties = | notes = | map = Japan Fukuoka | type = Round Kofun | map_dot_label = Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|33|46|30|N|130|29|40|E}} | zoom = 14 | float = right | width = 250| height = 200 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun and Miyajidake Kofun}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1 = {{Coord|33|46|13.23|N|130|29|52.58|E}} | map_dot_label1 = Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun | map_dot_label-pos1 = bottom | map_dot_label-color1 = black | mark1 = Round Kofun.png | mark-size1 = 20 | mark-coord2 = {{Coord|33|46|51.10|N|130|29|19.15|E}} | map_dot_label2 = Miyajidake Kofun | map_dot_label-pos2 = right | map_dot_label-color2 = black | mark2 = Round Kofun.png | mark-size2 = 20 }} The '''Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun''' (手光波切不動古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun}} located in Tebika, {{ill|Fukutsu, Fukuoka|en|Fukutsu, Fukuoka|WD=Q825700|lt=Fukutsu City}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture}}. It is one of the Kofuns that make up the {{ill|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster|en|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster|WD=Q109595331|lt=Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster}} and has been Historic Site1 as a historic site by Fukutsu City. == Overview == This Kofun is located on the terminal end of a hill (22–27 meters above sea level) derived from the foothills of Mount Miyaji, in northern Fukuoka Prefecture. It occupies the southernmost position among the Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster. The stone chamber has been open since ancient times, and the mound has been partially leveled. Detailed investigations of the stone chamber were conducted in {{ill|1992|en|1992|WD=Q2060|lt=1992}} (Heisei 4), while surveys of the mound were pertypeed during {{ill|2010|en|2010|WD=Q1995|lt=2010}}–{{ill|2011|en|2011|WD=Q1994|lt=2011}} (Heisei 22–23). Due to leveling at the mound's base, its exact type is unclear, but the mound is currently estimated to have a diameter of 20 meters and a height of 9 meters. Originally, it is reconstructed to have been approximately 25 meters in diameter{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. The burial facility is a {{ill|Side-entrance chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} type {{ill|Stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening towards the south-southwest. The total length of the stone chamber is 10.8 meters, and large basalt stones were used as its construction material. Despite the chamber being open for centuries, a survey of the tomb path in front of the chamber revealed wooden coffin components (nails and clamps), as well as iron products, horse gear, and fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}} and Silla pottery{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. The construction of the mound is estimated to date to the early {{ill|7th century|en|7th century|WD=Q8089|lt=7th century}}, with possible additional burials through the late 7th century{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. Previously considered to follow the {{ill|Miyajidake Kofun|ja|宮地嶽古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyajidake Kofun|qq=}}, it is now viewed as preceding it, and is considered a successor to the {{ill|Zaiji Kentsuka Kofun|ja|在自剣塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zaiji Kentsuka Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the largest {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} in the Munakata region{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. As a tomb adopting the side-entrance chamber structure typical of the Kinai region, it holds significance for understanding the political landscape of the Munakata region{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. In {{ill|1992|en|1992|WD=Q2060|lt=1992}} (Heisei 4), the mound was Historic Site1 as a historic site by the typeer Fukuma Town, now Fukutsu City. == Excavation History == * {{ill|Tenpō|en|Tenpō|WD=Q1369045|lt=Tenpō}} era ({{ill|1830|en|1830|WD=Q7576|lt=1830}}–{{ill|1844|en|1844|WD=Q7633|lt=1844}}): Mentioned in the ''{{ill|Zoku Fudoki of Chikuzen Province|ja|筑前国続風土記拾遺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Fudoki of Chikuzen Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' (described as a faith site with the stone chamber already open, known as Fudo Cave){{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. * {{ill|1992|en|1992|WD=Q2060|lt=1992}}: Detailed measurements of the stone chamber{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. * October 13, 1992: Designated as a Fukuma Town Historic Site (now a Fukutsu City Historic Site){{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. * {{ill|2010|en|2010|WD=Q1995|lt=2010}}: Mound survey by the Fukutsu City Board of Education, reported in {{ill|2013|en|2013|WD=Q1998|lt=2013}}{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. * {{ill|2011|en|2011|WD=Q1994|lt=2011}}: Confirmation survey of the mound's size{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. == burial == [[File:Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Bird's-eye view of the stone chamber}} ]] [[File:Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the stone chamber}} ]] The burial facility is a {{ill|Side-entrance chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} type {{ill|Stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening south-southwest. The rear part of the main chamber narrows into rear and front chambers, showcasing a multi-chamber structure similar to those in the Kinai region. The area of the stone chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}: * Total length of the stone chamber: 10.8 meters (preserved) * Rear chamber: Length 2.1 meters, width 1.3 meters, height 1.9 meters * Front chamber: Length 1.8 meters, width 1.6 meters, height 2 meters * Passageway The chamber is constructed using basalt slabs, possibly sourced from {{ill|Ainoshima_(Shingū)|en|Ainoshima_(Shingū)|WD=Q11581975|lt=Ainoshima}}. Both the passageway and main chamber consist of a single layer of large stones, with five stones on each side wall and one stone on the rear wall. The floor is paved with large flat stones in some parts, while other parts have stone and pebble paving beneath approximately 10 cm of soil{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. == See Also == * {{ill|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster|en|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster|WD=Q109595331|lt=Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster}} ** [[Miyajidake Kofun]] == References == {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|手光波切不動古墳|version=102446439|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:手光波切不動古墳]] {{Commons category|Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q117452644}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Kofun of Fukuoka prefecture]] [[Category:Munakata clan]] [[Category:Munakata clan|Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Fukutsu]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:pages]]

revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun]][[ja:手光波切不動古墳]] [[co:commons:Category:Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun]][[ja:手光波切不動古墳]] [[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun]][[ja:手光波切不動古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|coordinates={{Coord|33.77033333333333|N|130.49794444444444|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}} {{Infobox Japanese Kofun | name = Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun | image = [[File:Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]] | caption = Mound and stone chamber entrance | alternate_name = | part_of = {{ill|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster|en|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster|WD=Q109595331|lt=Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster|1=Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster}} | location = {{ill|Fukutsu, Fukuoka|en|Fukutsu, Fukuoka|WD=Q825700|lt=Fukutsu City|1=Fukutsu}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}}, Tebika 1615 (Ishibashi) | coordinates = 33 | coordinates = 46 | coordinates = 13.23 | coordinates = 130 | coordinates = 29 | coordinates = 52.58 | omitted = JP-40 | type = (Estimated) {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | area = Diameter 25m | Burial Facility = {{ill|Side-entrance chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} type {{ill|Stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}
(Wooden coffin inside) | burial goods = Iron products, horse gear, {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}}, Silla pottery | associated_tombs = | period = Early {{ill|7th century|en|7th century|WD=Q8089|lt=7th century|1=7th century}} | occupant = | imperial_tomb = | historic_site = Fukutsu City Designated Historic Site "Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun" | tangible_cultural_properties = | notes = | map = Japan Fukuoka | type = Round Kofun | map_dot_label = Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|33|46|30|N|130|29|40|E}} | zoom = 14 | float = right | width = 250| height = 200 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun and Miyajidake Kofun}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1 = {{Coord|33|46|13.23|N|130|29|52.58|E}} | map_dot_label1 = Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun | map_dot_label-pos1 = bottom | map_dot_label-color1 = black | mark1 = Round Kofun.png | mark-size1 = 20 | mark-coord2 = {{Coord|33|46|51.10|N|130|29|19.15|E}} | map_dot_label2 = Miyajidake Kofun | map_dot_label-pos2 = right | map_dot_label-color2 = black | mark2 = Round Kofun.png | mark-size2 = 20 }} The '''Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun''' (手光波切不動古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} located in Tebika, {{ill|Fukutsu, Fukuoka|en|Fukutsu, Fukuoka|WD=Q825700|lt=Fukutsu City|1=Fukutsu}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}}. It is one of the Kofuns that make up the {{ill|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster|en|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster|WD=Q109595331|lt=Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster|1=Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster}} and has been Historic Site1 as a historic site by Fukutsu City. == Overview == This Kofun is located on the terminal end of a hill (22–27 meters above sea level) derived from the foothills of Mount Miyaji, in northern Fukuoka Prefecture. It occupies the southernmost position among the Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster. The stone chamber has been open since ancient times, and the mound has been partially leveled. Detailed investigations of the stone chamber were conducted in {{ill|1992|en|1992|WD=Q2060|lt=1992|1=1992}} (Heisei 4), while surveys of the mound were pertypeed during {{ill|2010|en|2010|WD=Q1995|lt=2010|1=2010}}–{{ill|2011|en|2011|WD=Q1994|lt=2011|1=2011}} (Heisei 22–23). Due to leveling at the mound's base, its exact type is unclear, but the mound is currently estimated to have a diameter of 20 meters and a height of 9 meters. Originally, it is reconstructed to have been approximately 25 meters in diameter{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. The burial facility is a {{ill|Side-entrance chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} type {{ill|Stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening towards the south-southwest. The total length of the stone chamber is 10.8 meters, and large basalt stones were used as its construction material. Despite the chamber being open for centuries, a survey of the tomb path in front of the chamber revealed wooden coffin components (nails and clamps), as well as iron products, horse gear, and fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} and Silla pottery{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. The construction of the mound is estimated to date to the early {{ill|7th century|en|7th century|WD=Q8089|lt=7th century|1=7th century}}, with possible additional burials through the late 7th century{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. Previously considered to follow the {{ill|Miyajidake Kofun|ja|宮地嶽古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyajidake Kofun|qq=}}, it is now viewed as preceding it, and is considered a successor to the {{ill|Zaiji Kentsuka Kofun|ja|在自剣塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zaiji Kentsuka Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the largest {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} in the Munakata region{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. As a tomb adopting the side-entrance chamber structure typical of the Kinai region, it holds significance for understanding the political landscape of the Munakata region{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. In {{ill|1992|en|1992|WD=Q2060|lt=1992|1=1992}} (Heisei 4), the mound was Historic Site1 as a historic site by the typeer Fukuma Town, now Fukutsu City. == Excavation History == * {{ill|Tenpō|en|Tenpō|WD=Q1369045|lt=Tenpō|1=Tenpō}} era ({{ill|1830|en|1830|WD=Q7576|lt=1830|1=1830}}–{{ill|1844|en|1844|WD=Q7633|lt=1844|1=1844}}): Mentioned in the ''{{ill|Zoku Fudoki of Chikuzen Province|ja|筑前国続風土記拾遺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Fudoki of Chikuzen Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' (described as a faith site with the stone chamber already open, known as Fudo Cave){{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. * {{ill|1992|en|1992|WD=Q2060|lt=1992|1=1992}}: Detailed measurements of the stone chamber{{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. * October 13, 1992: Designated as a Fukuma Town Historic Site (now a Fukutsu City Historic Site){{Sfn|Iura Hajime|2014}}. * {{ill|2010|en|2010|WD=Q1995|lt=2010|1=2010}}: Mound survey by the Fukutsu City Board of Education, reported in {{ill|2013|en|2013|WD=Q1998|lt=2013|1=2013}}{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. * {{ill|2011|en|2011|WD=Q1994|lt=2011|1=2011}}: Confirmation survey of the mound's size{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. == burial == [[File:Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Bird's-eye view of the stone chamber}} ]] [[File:Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the stone chamber}} ]] The burial facility is a {{ill|Side-entrance chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} type {{ill|Stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening south-southwest. The rear part of the main chamber narrows into rear and front chambers, showcasing a multi-chamber structure similar to those in the Kinai region. The area of the stone chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}: * Total length of the stone chamber: 10.8 meters (preserved) * Rear chamber: Length 2.1 meters, width 1.3 meters, height 1.9 meters * Front chamber: Length 1.8 meters, width 1.6 meters, height 2 meters * Passageway The chamber is constructed using basalt slabs, possibly sourced from {{ill|Ainoshima_(Shingū)|en|Ainoshima_(Shingū)|WD=Q11581975|lt=Ainoshima|1=Ainoshima (Shingū)}}. Both the passageway and main chamber consist of a single layer of large stones, with five stones on each side wall and one stone on the rear wall. The floor is paved with large flat stones in some parts, while other parts have stone and pebble paving beneath approximately 10 cm of soil{{Sfn|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster III|2013}}. == See Also == * {{ill|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster|en|Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster|WD=Q109595331|lt=Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster|1=Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster}} ** [[Miyajidake Kofun]] == References == {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|手光波切不動古墳|version=102446439|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:手光波切不動古墳]] {{Commons category|Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q117452644}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Kofun of Fukuoka prefecture]] [[Category:Munakata clan]] [[Category:Munakata clan|Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Fukutsu]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:pages]]
